articles.title: 'The Spectre of the Opera' or 'The Sorcerer’s Secret'
articles.content:
[h2]Players Information[/h2]
The players overhear a rather loud discussion at the @[Mixolydian Mode](landmark:11295957-2b7d-4d5c-bb99-2a0a765035f6) between a foppishly dressed actor and a whining, drunken man who appears to be some sort of theater manager. The actor is complaining about the unsafe conditions at the theater, describing several incidents (that sound suspiciously like undead to our brave adventurers) in which his limbs, yea, his very life could have been endangered (see rumors, @[Mixolydian Mode](landmark:11295957-2b7d-4d5c-bb99-2a0a765035f6), for a sample incident)! His tirade ends with the complaint that now the opening has been postponed yet again, and his "public is not going to stand for this outrage!" After the actor stomps out in a grand exit, the manager sits alone, looking mournfully into his beer.

Casual questioning of nearby patrons of the tavern will reveal that the drunken man is @[Phineous Tarkin](person:22ebacd4-69c7-4c04-ab9a-a87ddb31f2e4), manager of the Muses' Theater. The complainer is the actor, @[Chiles Putney](person:798ad3ab-65bd-4a13-8b49-e72b92ded4c5), the star of the up-and-coming production at the theater, "The Sorcerer’s Secret". The show has been “coming soon" for the past several weeks, and rumor has it that it may never open. 

"'Tis said that the spirit of Arasmus, the mage about whom the play was written, is angry about his life and loves being thus revealed and haunts the theater." 

Many of the stagehands and other actors will not enter the theater. Only @[Chiles Putney](person:798ad3ab-65bd-4a13-8b49-e72b92ded4c5) seems unaware of the spirit, since it never bothers him. @[Phineous Tarkin](person:22ebacd4-69c7-4c04-ab9a-a87ddb31f2e4) sank a lot of money into producing this play, and if he cannot put it on.

[h2]GM's Notes[/h2]
Muses' Theater is indeed haunted, although not by the spirit of Arasmus, as most suppose. On the stage, which is set up as a mage's workshop, are many items of magical paraphernalia. Most will read as magical to a Detect Magic spell, although they produce only special effects. One item that radiates magic is a humanoid skull. However, it is not used in the play other than as a prop. 

The skull belonged to a long-dead Elvish mage, @[Xandrian](person:12592fad-fc18-4721-be9f-05136736ba40) and it is his spectre that haunts the theater, moving furniture and props, setting off the pyrotechnical special effects, leaving notes written in an obscure High Elvish dialect, and in general trying to call attraction to his plight. The spectre, unfamiliar with humans and theater, does not realize that @[Chiles Putney](person:798ad3ab-65bd-4a13-8b49-e72b92ded4c5) is only an actor on a stage, and has been trying to communicate with him. It will defend the "workshop" against those it considers to be intruders (stagehands, for example).

All the spectre desires are to reunite his skull with his skeleton (buried in the basement of Ravensrook, a house on Magic Street) and to be laid to rest somewhere nice - the Elvenwold, for instance. But @[Xandrian](person:12592fad-fc18-4721-be9f-05136736ba40) cannot speak, nor does he understand the Common tongue, and is having grave difficulties getting the message across.

@[Xandrian](person:12592fad-fc18-4721-be9f-05136736ba40) is most active when @[Chiles Putney](person:798ad3ab-65bd-4a13-8b49-e72b92ded4c5) is not on stage. The spectre’s approach will set off the player-character's trap sense. His touch does 2d12 in damage, plus draining 1d8 points from a character's CON, (INT save vs. DC 15) for half damage). @[Xandrian](person:12592fad-fc18-4721-be9f-05136736ba40) will only touch as a last resort, to prevent disturbance of the "workshop" on stage.

An elf has a 40% chance of recognizing the skull is elven, not human. A mage will Detect Magic use whenever the spectre is active.

articles.title: Bridge: Post Goblin Trouble
articles.content:
This bridge links the tail end of @[Key: Goblin Trouble](plot:25f6e55a-c63f-4bb8-bdf2-f0d263fbbd73) to the return to Haven which then leads to @[Key: Night of the Living Dolls](plot:736ca018-3455-4e5c-9d31-3326c4353c79) and/or @[Key: Clockwork Tower](plot:5dab46dc-b84f-4f3f-b884-45a98b0ac6a6).

[h1]Heading Home from Oakenville[/h1]
-- run across group of kobols talking to zombie unicorn
-- if attacked, unicorn runs off while goblins attack
-- captured kobold ([b]Girbo[/b]) doesn't know much
---- scary, ghosty guy told their chief to come so they did
---- big meeting with scary ghosty guy and many goblins
---- adventurers broke in
---- chief Kirich told he knows what to do and we left
---- he doesn't know what that is
---- ran down corridor and came out in the woods (can't backtrack to where that is)
---- dead unicorn met them on the way to get them a ride?
---- then more adventurers, fight, capture


[h1]Arriving in Haven[/h1]
-- time lurch of 3 more weeks so they arrive home 1 month after leaving: date is now Earthday, Wealsun the 6th
-- it's late in the day, sun setting, gates are about to close
-- waiting at the city gates are two women --> Jillie and Lucille
-- Jillie is pissed at Martin for disappearing for a month
-- Lucille has two message for them
---- Lyza is mad and needs to see them
---- Strange attacks happening at night
-- Oh, and there's a tower in town that appeared a week ago accompanied by a "time lurch" felt city-wide


[h2]Jillie's Anger[/h2]
Use the following and pause after each. As soon as Martin starts to speak Jillie cuts him off with the next line.

-- Martin Plowhearth you get your skinny little butt down off that wagon and explain to me what you've been doing for the past 4 weeks!  
-- Yes, you heard me, 4 weeks!!!

-- You left me without even an goodbye! 
-- What am I supposed to think when you run out on me like that?!?  
-- You and your so-called friends even ran out on your employer!
-- You could be lying dead somewhere and how would I even know?
-- How could you think so little of me?

-- And what about your parents (Lysra/Arbin)?  
-- Tough I guess you already left Corbin, Dalin, and Hilda in the lurch looking after them without your help. 
-- Have you even told any of them you're galavanting about instead of patrolling the city?!?

articles.title: CT: Artificer's Library
articles.content:
This grand room with high ceilings and a damaged roof houses the Clockwork Tower's wealth of knowledge. However, adventurers must tread carefully as the Archives are protected by Cogsprocket Spiders, small constructs tasked with repairing and maintaining the historical records which are not particularly fond of intruders.

[aloud]The room you've entered has a grandeur about it; tall ceilings stretch high above you, bearing evidence of past opulence, now marred by wear and damage over time. Great bookshelves appear throughout the room, though many are toppled or in disarray. Intricate cogs and gears are embedded in the design of the room, especially overhead along the ceiling where there is a set of tracks and mechanisms above the shelving. At several locations the tracks are borken or misaligned.

Curiously, the air is filled with a subtle rhythmic ticking, punctuated by the skittering sound of many legs. As you venture further, you spot mechanical spiders moving among the shelves, their crystalline eyes focused on their tasks or turning toward you with wary caution.[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
In the Artificer's Library, employ the Cogsprocket Spiders for intricate combat dynamics. Their repair capabilities present novel hurdles as players grapple with foes that mend themselves continually. The towering bookshelves serve multiple purposes: as cover, climbing aids, or hiding spots. When introducing larger constructs like the Cogsprocket Sentinel or Cogsprocket Weaver, think about enhancing their skill sets to harmonize with the smaller arachnid mechanisms. It's vital to understand that these spiders prioritize the Library's upkeep above all; they are guardians, not killers.

Within the Artificer's Library lies the Chronos Codex, an artifact of unparalleled significance. This tome stands out not just for its rich historical content but also for its powerful magical attributes. Nestled deep within the library's core, the Codex offers wielders immense power and holds the potential solutions to rectify the temporal distortions plaguing both the Clockwork Tower and the lands that surround it.

[h2]Tick and Tock[/h2]
[aloud][ul][li][b]@[Tick](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f):[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Cogsprocket Sp-sp-spiders lurk, guarding knowledge, their tireless work!"

[li][b]@[Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f):[/b]  "Tock-tock! Mind where you s-s-step, or in spiders' web, you might get swept!"[/ul][/aloud]

[h2]Books found in the library[/h2]
[h3][b]Chronosmith's Compendium of Clockwork Arcana[/b][/h3] This hefty, leather-bound tome has a cover adorned with intricate etchings of gears and runes. It was penned by @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) himself and contains a wealth of knowledge on clockwork devices and temporal magic. Flipping through the pages, adventurers can find a section dedicated to the Chronos Resonator. It not only describes the Resonator's function but also lists its essential components, making references to the Crank of Quickening, Echoing Cog, and Temporal Fragment. The book also provides clues on proper alignment techniques for these components. A character who spends 1 hour reading this section can make one Arcana check with advantage when attempting to restore the Chronos Resonator.

[h3][b]Temporal Fragments: A Study in Theoretical Arcanodynamics[/b][/h3] This scroll is stored in a decorative, cylindrical container that looks much more recent than the surrounding volumes. It's a highly technical paper, filled with mathematical equations and magical symbols. At the heart of it, the paper theorizes on the nature of "temporal fragments," which are essential components of devices that manipulate time, such as the Chronos Resonator. A character who takes time to decipher the scroll ([u]requires a DC 15 Arcana check and 30 minutes of concentration[/u]) gains a unique insight into the Temporal Fragment needed for the Chronos Resonator. This understanding grants a one-time advantage on the DC 20 Arcana check required to align the Temporal Fragment with the Temporal Core of the Resonator.

[h3][b]Guardians of Gear and Cog: A History of Clockwork Constructs[/b][/h3]  This historical account describes the origins and advancements in clockwork constructs throughout the ages. It delves into the various types of constructs, their purposes, and their roles in society, from simple clockwork toys to advanced Clockwork Sentinels. Illustrations pepper the pages, showing the intricate designs of these mechanical wonders. A character who spends 2 hours reading this book gains advantage on History or Arcana checks related to clockwork constructs for the next 7 days.

[h3][b]Celestial Orbits and Temporal Flux[/b][/h3] The Alchemy of Time: A scholarly tome filled with complex diagrams and charts mapping the flow of celestial bodies and their potential influence on the passage of time. It's a challenging read, laden with advanced magical theories that ponder the relationship between the heavens and time itself. Characters who successfully read and comprehend this book (requires a [u]DC 18 Arcana check and 2 hours of study[/u]) can use the celestial patterns described within to gain a one-time reroll for any failed saving throw against spells or effects that manipulate time.

[h3][b]The Pocket Guide to Arcane Sigils[/b][/h3] This small, pocket-sized book offers a comprehensive overview of commonly used arcane symbols, glyphs, and runes. While it is designed to be more of a reference guide than a scholarly text, it provides valuable information for anyone working with arcane arts, including brief notes on potential uses or misuses of each symbol. Characters who spend at least 30 minutes skimming through this guide can add +1 to any single Arcana check related to identifying arcane symbols for the next 24 hours.

[h1]The Chronos Codex[/h1]
The Chronos Codex is a legendary artifact, not merely for its historical value but also for its potent arcane properties. Located at the  heart of the Artificer's Library, it promises both power and solutions to the time anomalies afflicting the Clockwork Tower and its surroundings. 

The Codex can be found upon an ornate pedestal, enclosed by a 10-foot radius protective barrier of swirling time energy.  If the Codex is disturbed without disabling the defenses (see below), a [b]a Cogsprocket Sentinel[/b] emerge from hidden compartments to defend the Codex (one each time the codex is disturbed).

[i]When they first approach the force shield:[/i]
[aloud]
Looking through the swirling field of energy you can see a large book on a lectern.
[/aloud]

[h2]Chronos Codex Defences[/h2]
[h3]1) Temporal Vortex[/h3] Any creature that steps within 10 feet of the Codex without deactivating the protection suffers from a localized time distortion. They must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Constitution saving throw[/u] or be affected by the Slow spell for 1 minute. Meanwhile, the [b]Cogsprocket Sentinels[/b] are alerted by the disturbance of the barrier and activate to defend the Codex. 

The barrier can be deactivated with an [u]Intelligence (Arcana) check or a straight Intelligence check (using tinker's tools) at DC 17[/u]. Finishing and repairing the functional issues of the library also work to weaken the barrier. Successfully completing both tasks deactivates the Temporal Vortex barrier entirely.

[b]Success:[/b] The vortex disappears, allowing physical access to the Codex.

[i]When that can see more and/or touch the book:[/i]
[aloud]
This appears to be a large book that literally reeks of magic. There is however no obvious way to open the book as what would normally be the spine goes all the way around.

On what might be the one side are symbols of the sun, Hasp and Celene - Antheros's two moons - and two other symbols. There are lines forming a grid, but with random crossing lines. You think it might be possible to slide the symbols along the lines.
[/aloud]

If anyone asks, the lines and symbols mimic those on the platform in one corner of the room.


[h3]2) Temporal Lock[/h3] To open the Codex, players must align symbols on the book's cover corresponding to the current positions of celestial bodies. This requires a [u]DC 18 Intelligence (Investigation) check[/u]. Failure by 5 or more triggers a 
[spoiler]If the glyph is triggered, a shockwave is produced knocking all creatures withing 10' back by 10' and causing [roll:1d8] bludgeoning damage ([u]Dexterity save DC 10 for no damage[/u]).  Further, if a creature is knocked back into a wall they take an additional [roll:1d6] bludgeoning damage (no save).|Glyph of Warding[/spoiler] stored within the book.

If one of the party has read [i]Celestial Orbits and Temporal Flux[/i] (above), they will recognize the other two symbols as referencing the stars Ebla and Tabit.

Note that there are (at least) two ways to accomplish this: 
[ol][li]Repair the Time-telling pillars (see below) in the corner which will result in the pillars sliding into the appropriate locations.
[li]Have someone who understands astronomy/astrology translate the symbols for them.  This person may also note that the symbols as positioned on the book are not in the correct relative positions.
[/ol]

[b]Success:[/b] The binding on three sides disappears, allowing the book to be opened.

When the codex is opened:
[aloud]
[img:5908118|right|40|nolink]As the book opens, you feel as if there is another "time glitch" but it is very short, almost not there at all.  On the first page the is a strange symbol. 
[/aloud]

The glyph is called Mnemosyne and represents memory.  A successul [u]Arcana check (DC 12) will recall this meaning[/u].  


[h3]3) Memory Ward[/h3] 
Those who attempt to read the Codex without disabling the Mnemosyne ward will have their memories of the Codex's contents erased after 1 hour. The ward can be disabled with a succesful [u]DC 16 Intelligence (Arcana) check[/u].


[h2]Deciphering the Codex[/h2] 
The pages are encrypted. [u]Decoding requires an Intelligence (Arcana) check[/u].[ul][li][b]DC 14:[/b] Takes 1 hour to decipher a page.
[li][b]DC 17:[/b] Takes 10 minutes.
[li][b]DC 22:[/b] Almost instant comprehension.[/ul]


[h2]Importance for the PCs[/h2]
[b]Restoring Balance:[/b] The temporal anomalies affecting the Clockwork Tower and the surrounding area of Haven can be cured using a ritual from the Codex. The ritual requires:[ul][li]1 hour of uninterrupted casting.
[li]At least three casters proficient in Arcana.
[li]Expending a spell slot of 5th level or higher.[/ul]

[b]Personal Stakes:[/b] The Codex has the potential to restore a PC suffering from a temporal curse. This would require a caster to make a successful [u]DC 22 Intelligence (Arcana) check[/u] and expend a spell slot of 4th level or higher in order to tap into the Codex's power to reverse time-bound ailments.

[b]Power and Knowledge:[/b] Arcane casters who study the Codex for 8 hours and succeed on a [u]DC 25 Intelligence (Arcana) check[/u]  can temporarily add the spells [u]Time Stop[/u] and [u]Haste[/u] to their spellbooks or list of known spells for the next 7 days. After 7 days, the knowledge fades, and the spell must be re-learned from the Codex.


[h1]Incomplete Work and Wear & Tear[/h1]
As the adventurers navigate through the grandeur and decay of the Artificer's Library, the incomplete projects and wear and tear that Constantine mentioned becomes evident. These manifestations challenge the party, but also provide an opportunity for them to make tangible progress in stabilizing the Clockwork Tower.

[h2]Incomplete Work: The Time-Telling Totems[/h2]
[ul]
[li][b]Task:[/b] On one corner of the library, stands an array of six totem-like pillars, each with intricate carvings of different celestial bodies. A close inspection ([u]DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check[/u]) reveals that these totems are mechanisms meant to keep track of the positions of the celestial bodies.  However, two of the totems are incomplete [ul][li]Ebla,[li]Tabit[/ul]. Symbols on the other four correspond to: [ul][li]Antheros (green), [li]Celene (yellow) [li]Hasp (blue), and [li]the sun (red).[/ul]

[li][b]Retrieve the parts:[/b]  Hidden among the library shelves, adventurers can find two intricately carved pieces that fit the two broken  totems. A [u]DC 17 Wisdom (Perception)[/u] or a [u]DC 14 Intelligence (Investigation)[/u] check will help locate these carvings.

[li][b]Assembly:[/b] Once retrieved, a [u]DC 16 Intelligence (Arcana) check[/u] or a [u]DC 14 Dexterity check (can use tinker's tools to gain advantage)[/u] will successfully attach the carvings to the totems. Once all totems are complete, they synchronize and slide into relative positions aligning to the current positions of their corresponding celestial bodies. There is also a soft glow that surrounds the Chronos Codex, indicating a weakening of its Temporal Lock (see above).
[/ul]

[h2]Wear and Tear: Decaying Book Retrieval System[/h2]
[container:indent-40]
The library once had an ingenious mechanism to fetch books from high shelves: a series of pulleys, ropes, and mechanical claws. However, over time, these have worn out, making many books inaccessible.

[b]Repairing the mechanism:[/b]

Players can use their skills to mend broken ropes, realign pulleys, and recalibrate mechanical claws. This requires:
[ul][li][u]DC 10 Intelligence check (using tinker's tools)[/u] to mend the broken ropes.
[li][u]DC 16 Strength (Athletics)[/u] check to realign the mechanism's heavy pulleys.
[li][u]DC 15 Intelligence check (using tinker's tools)[/u] to recalibrate the mechanical claws.
[li]A total of three successful checks stabilizes the mechanism.[/ul]

Once operational, the system can be used to retrieve a unique tome, Mechanisms of the Clockwork Realm, from a top shelf. This book provides invaluable insights into the workings of the Clockwork Tower and provides the reader a +2 bonus on skill checks related to understanding or repairing mechanisms within the tower.

While the adventurers are working on these tasks, the [b]Cogsprocket Spiders[/b] might perceive this as tampering and become more aggressive. Alternatively, if players can communicate their intentions (perhaps using a spell or ability to speak with constructs or presenting the tools in a non-threatening manner), the spiders might even assist, or at least refrain from hindering the party's efforts.

Lastly, repairing these mechanisms and completing the unfinished work will not only aid in accessing the Chronos Codex but also stabilize a part of the Clockwork Tower, fulfilling Constantine's request and bringing the adventurers one step closer to rectifying the tower's anomalies.
[/container]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the Library takes a lair action to cause one of the following effects: 

[ol]
[li][b]Clockwork Ambush:[/b] A hidden compartment in one of the bookshelves slides open, releasing an additional 2 Cogsprocket Spiders.  The spiders are focused on their repair tasks and are less aggressive - the won't attack unless threatened directly.

[li][b]Temporal Distortion:[/b] A device in the room momentarily distorts time. All creatures except the spiders have their speed halved until the next round.

[li][b]Overdrive:[/b] All Cogsprocket Spiders and their variants gain an additional reaction until the end of the next round.

[li][b]Mechanical Malfunction:[/b] Random gears and cogs in the room spin out of control, creating difficult terrain in a 15-foot radius from a random point in the room.
[/ol]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409605]|Cogsprocket Spider[/spoiler]

articles.title: CT: Assembly Room
articles.content:
This room, with its worn-out workbenches and rusted tools, was once a bustling hub of innovation. Olivia Chronosmith, the city's watchmaker, might provide the required tools and instructions to restore the Assembly Room.

[aloud]The ornate doors creak open to reveal an expansive room. Rows of long, dust-covered tables stretch from one end to the other, filled with rusted tools and disassembled mechanical artifacts. The room seems to be in a state of neglect, bearing testament to its once-great significance. Ancient sketches and mechanical designs adorn the walls, providing a glimpse into the genius that once thrived here. Discarded tools, their once-sharp edges now dulled by rust and time, litter the floor. Dust covers workbenches and tools alike. Despite the decay, an aura of past glory and innovation hangs in the air, beckoning you to restore it to its former splendor.
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The main goal of this encounter is for the PCs to realize the importance of the Assembly Room and seek out Olivia Chronosmith for help in its restoration.

[h2]Tick and Tock[/h2]
[aloud][ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! O-O-Olivia knows, how to fix, where it goes!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Sprites play tricks, on the way to Olivia's mix!"[/ul][/aloud]

[h2]Roleplaying Olivia[/h2]
Olivia, with her deep ties to the Clockwork Tower through her ancestry, is eager to help the PCs. She’s knowledgeable, passionate, and might even be willing to guide the players through some of the more intricate aspects of the Clockwork Tower.

If Olivia is with the PCs, she provides guidance and can offer a simple puzzle or riddle related to the room's restoration. Successfully solving it reveals a hidden cache of tools or a minor magic item.

[h1]The Clockwork Puzzle[/h1]
If Olivia is with the party:

[aloud]Upon inspecting the room, Olivia’s eyes fixate on a peculiar desk at the far end. The surface of this workbench is inlaid with a decorative array of polished gears and cogs – its rare ornamental beauty a clear indication of its importance. As she walks towards the workbench she remarks, “Ah! This is the Mechanism Table. Legend has it that Constantine Chronosmith, my ancestor, would use such tables to test the aptitude of his apprentices. It’s said that solving the puzzle reveals a special cache that he hid for his most promising students. Let's see if you can decipher it.”[/aloud]

Olivia guides the PCs to the Mechanism Table, but instead of gears, there are five indented circles set into the wood. Around the table are a single six-sided die , 4 cards, a coin, and a wodden cup. A nearby plaque reads:

[center]"Five circles tell of time and tower,[br]
Use what's around, unlock the power.[br]
Die casts its fate, a coin does gleam,[br]
Cup holds a secret, cards tell a dream."
[/center]

[h2]The Puzzle[/h2]
[b]Die (Dice):[/b] The single six-sided die, glows with an arcane energy, suggesting it has a role in the puzzle. While the number 4 seems initially arbitrary, players versed in arcane or natural lore may draw connections to the four classical elements of Earth, Air, Fire, and Water, or perhaps the four cardinal directions. A [u]DC 15 Arcana or Nature check would reveal the significance of the number 4[/u], hinting at the elements or directions it might represent. The players must place the die in one of the circles with the [b]number 4 facing up[/b].

[b]Coin:[/b] The coin is not of the usual mint found in the realm. Its shiny, reflective surface captures the room's dim light, shimmering like a small beacon. When placed in one of the circles, the coin seems to evoke the celestial, perhaps symbolizing the sun or moon, or even the glint off the Clockwork Tower's metallic exterior. A [u]DC 13 History or Religion check would make a character recall[/u] the symbolic significance of coins in ritual, leading them to place the coin as a representation of the sun or moon in a circle.

[b]Cup:[/b] The wooden cup may appear unremarkable at first glance, but its role becomes clearer when filled with water. It could symbolize the moat around the Clockwork Tower or perhaps the life-giving rivers that traverse the lands. [u]DC 12 Survival or Insight check would allow a character to understand[/u] that water typically represents life or barriers, encouraging them to [b]fill the cup and place it in a circle[/b].

[b]Cards:[/b] Four cards lay on the table, each adorned with a different emblem - a tower, a flame, a cloud, and a rock. While these symbols may seem incongruous, someone with the right training might see them as markers of civilization's evolution, progressing from the simplicity of a rock, through fire-wielding communities, up to cloud-reaching towers. A [u]DC 16 History or Arcana check would let the players realize[/u] the cards represent stages of development or elements, guiding them to place the cards into a circle, [b]stacked in the following order, from bottom to top: rock, fire, tower, and cloud[/b].

[b]The Fifth Circle:[/b] An emblem of clock hands, frozen at midnight, is engraved into the fifth circle. It stands apart from the others—no object fits here. Instead, the key is time itself. A [u]DC 14 Perception or Insight check would allow a player to notice[/u] the intricacy of the clock hand engraving, deducing that time, rather than objects, is the key to this circle. They would understand that they should [b]wait without disturbing the table for one minute[/b].

[h2]Solution[/h2]
The order of placement doesn’t matter, but all elements must be set correctly. Once everything is in place, and the players have waited for a minute for the fifth circle, a drawer pops out from the table's side.

[h2]Reward[/h2]
[h3]Clockworker’s Medallion[/h3] 
This ornate bronze medallion features intricate engravings of gears and cogs. When worn, the medallion provides the following benefits:
[ul][li][b]Attunement to the Tower:[/b] The wearer gains advantage on skill checks related to understanding or interacting with the mechanisms within the Clockwork Tower.
[li][b]Artificer's Insight:[/b] Once per day, the wearer can tap into the medallion’s power to cast either the Mending cantrip or the Identify spell without using a spell slot.
[li][b]Temporal Resonance:[/b] The medallion has 3 charges. As a reaction, which is taken when the wearer is hit by an attack roll, they can expend a charge to rewind a few moments in time and automatically cause the attack to miss instead. The medallion regains all expended charges at dawn.[/ul]

Inside the cache, the PCs also find a neatly folded note:

[container: indent-40]"To the one who understands the rhythm of the cogs and the dance of time, wear this medallion with pride. For you are now a guardian of this tower's legacy. [br]- C. Chronosmith"[/container]


[h1]Wear and Tear Restoration Tasks[/h1]
[h2]Worn-out Workbenches[/h2] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Carpentry check to restore benches.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Players can make an Intelligence check (using carpenter's tools). DC 12 for basic repair, DC 18 for a masterful restoration that might provide a +1 bonus on crafting checks made on this workbench in the future.[/ul]

[h2]Rusted Tools[/h2] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Clean and restore tools.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Players can make a Dexterity check (using smith's tools or tinker’s tools). [u]DC 10 to clean off rust, DC 15 to sharpen and realign the tools to provide advantage on the next use.[/u][/ul]

[h2]Dust-Covered Tables[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Clean the tables.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] A simple [u]Dexterity check (can use tinker’s tools) (DC 8)[/u] ensures the tables are cleaned without damaging them. Using a Prestidigitation spell instantly cleans one table.[/ul]

[h1]Incomplete Work[/h1]
[h2]Disassembled Mechanical Artifacts[/h2] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Reassemble artifacts.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Players can make an Intelligence check (using tinker's tools for advantage). DC varies based on artifact complexity (range DC 10-20). Some artifacts might require specific components or Arcana checks to integrate magical elements.[/ul]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the Assembly Room activates one of the following effects; it can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

[ol]
[li][b]Whirling Tools:[/b] The rusted tools in the room spring to life, animated by some unseen force. They fly around the room, forcing every creature in the room to make a [u]DC 13 Dexterity saving throw or take 2d6 slashing damage[/u].

[li][b]Clockwork Shadows:[/b] The remnants of old constructs emerge as ghostly apparitions. These shadows provide half cover to all constructs present in the room.

[li][b]Mechanical Floor:[/b] Parts of the floor rotate, shift, or rise. A 10-foot square section of the ground either rises by 5 feet or sinks by 5 feet (DM’s choice), potentially changing the battlefield's dynamics.

[li][b]Emergency Repairs[/b] Arcane energy flows through the room, repairing any constructs by 2d8 hit points.
[/ol]

articles.title: CT: Chronos Lobby
articles.content:
The Chronos Lobby is an opulent entrance to the Clockwork Tower, featuring walls lined with ornate timepieces of all sizes. The main focus is a grand central timepiece that towers over the room. However, it is silent and lifeless, requiring numerous parts for restoration. The clockwork bird guides, @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f), can be found here fluttering around, offering guidance to adventurers willing to listen. The required parts can be discovered in Haven's @[Great Junkyard](plot:e9b17b41-1091-4b00-8c06-32e8eae89575), a sprawling wasteland of discarded clockwork and machinery. There, adventurers might face Rust Engineers, ghosts of obsolete workers that were once alive with purpose.

[aloud]As you enter the Clockwork Tower, you find yourselves enveloped in the splendor of an opulent space, where it feels as if time has come to a standstill. Walls around you are adorned with countless timepieces, from miniature hourglasses to grandiose mechanical clocks. Every timekeeping device imaginable resides here, each more intricate than the last, yet all frozen in place, emphasizing the room's silence. Dominating the heart of the room is a colossal central timepiece that stretches from floor to ceiling - its magnificent potential clear; yet for now, it remains silent and dormant.

A soft fluttering catches your ear, leading your gaze to two small clockwork birds darting between the clocks as they fly in synchronized harmony. Their wings, a blend of feathers and fine metals, glint in the ambient light. Landing before you, their mechanical eyes focus intently on you. With curious tilts of their heads, they seem poised with intelligence and purpose.[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
[b]Grand Central Timepiece:[/b] A giant ornamental clock, currently non-functional. Restoring it might be key to helping reactivate the Clockwork Tower. Parts required for its restoration can be found in Haven's @[Great Junkyard](plot:e9b17b41-1091-4b00-8c06-32e8eae89575).

[b]@[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f):[/b] These two clockwork birds are ready to assist adventurers in understanding the tower's mysteries. If approached diplomatically, they can offer hints, historical insights about @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), and guidance on potential dangers ahead. If the adventurers are aggressive or destructive, @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) can activate defense mechanisms or redirect them into more dangerous paths.


[h1]Traversing Rooms in the Tower[/h1]
The easiest way to picture the Tower interior is to imagine each room is on a separate level with a stairway between them that twists time and space. 

As the player climb/descend, they will feel a "lurch" in the pit of their stomachs  when they pass a point between rooms,  which  represents their translocation between areas ([u]DC 12 CON save to avoid 10 minutes of nausea, adjusted rolls under 7 means they actually vomit[/u]). 

Players on either side of the translocation point can communicate normally but cannot see each other - all they see is that the stairway continues. Watching a player cross to the other side, it appears as if their body is streched in time as it is pulled through.


[h1]Communicating with Tick and Tock[/h1]
The PCs can ask @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) questions. The stuttering, sing-song manner of @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) can provide not only helpful hints to adventurers but also inject some lightheartedness into an otherwise serious and potentially intense environment of the Clockwork Tower. Here are some chirpy, stuttering, sing-song responses from @[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f) regarding locations inside the Clockwork Tower:

[b]Regarding the Chronos Lobby:[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! The L-Lobby door, where time does snore! Needs p-p-pieces from junkyard floor!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! In the j-j-junkyard lore, find what the clock does implore!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the @[Gear Foundry](plot:2096333a-caf6-4548-b095-b21cfd241094):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! The forge lies c-c-cold, needs metal and coal, so b-b-bold!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Gearhounds b-b-bark and guard, fetching metal might be hard!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the Assembly Room:[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! O-O-Olivia knows, how to fix, where it goes!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Sprites play tricks, on the way to Olivia's mix!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the @[Artificer's Library](plot:f1faca19-130f-4e86-a79b-ca1bfd5d6354):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Cogsprocket Sp-sp-spiders lurk, guarding knowledge, their tireless work!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Mind where you s-s-step, or in spiders' web, you might get swept!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the @[Timepiece of Eternity](plot:5d14ce6a-79be-4565-af70-1c41911df5ba) Room:[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! The p-p-pedestal's hollow call, seeks a crystal, to rise, not fall!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! @[Luminous Caverns](plot:f8920a29-7a22-4ce1-93c4-636d4ba89f99) gleam, but Pendulum Horrors lurk and scheme!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the @[Echo Chamber](plot:de51804f-2855-42d2-aeb1-a8d4a340032f):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Echoes broken, mirrors lost! Mayor Elspeth knows the cost!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Phantoms of P-p-paradox play, in @[Council Chambers](plot:f8ec5847-9a50-4741-86c5-eb60047c10e6), causing disarray!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the Echo of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Constantine's s-s-song, trapped in time, all along!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Wise yet wild, the inventor's spirit, time's lost child!"[/ul]

[h1]Parts Needed to Restore the Grand Central Timepiece[/h1]
Below is a list of parts needed to restore the Grand Central Timepiece. They can be found in the @[Great Junkyard](plot:e9b17b41-1091-4b00-8c06-32e8eae89575).

[h3][b]Chronosmith's Balance Wheel[/b][/h3] Central to the timepiece's operation, this wheel will replace the damaged section of the Grand Central Timepiece.

[h3][b]Temporal Mainspring[/b][/h3] A source of energy, this mainspring can store and manage the flow of time, crucial for the clock's mechanisms.

[h3][b]Timeless Pivot[/b][/h3] Essential for the clock's tick-tock motion, this pivot ensures smooth and continuous operation.

[h3][b]Everlasting Spring[/b][/h3] The clock will require a continuous energy source, and this spring ensures that the clock doesn't need to be constantly wound up.

[h3][b]Crank of Quickening:[/b][/h3] To initially set the time and start the clock, this crank will make the process faster.


[h1]Wear and Tear Restoration Tasks for the Chronos Lobby[/h1]
[h3][b]Dusty Timepieces[/b][/h3] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Clean each clock and hourglass.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Simple cleaning requires a [u]Dexterity check (can use tinker's tools) (DC 8)[/u]. Casting the Prestidigitation spell can instantly clean a single timepiece.[/ul]

[h3][b]Misaligned Clock Hands[/b][/h3] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Realignment to ensure accurate timekeeping.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Players can make an [u]Intelligence check (using tinker's tools). DC 12[/u] ensures all smaller clocks are synced. Failing by 5 or more causes a clock to break, needing further repairs.[/ul]

[h3][b]Unlubricated Gears[/b][/h3] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Lubricate the gears of the clocks to ensure smooth movement.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Requires oil or an equivalent substance. Applying oil without damaging the clocks requires a [u]Dexterity check (can use tinker's tools) (DC 10)[/u]. Failure by 5 or more could cause temporary damage to a clock.[/ul]

[h3][b]Cracked Glass on Timepieces[/b][/h3] 
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Replace or mend the glass.
[li][b]Directions:[/b] Glass can be replaced with materials found in the junkyard. Alternatively, a Mending spell can repair minor cracks. Replacing requires a [u]Dexterity check (using tinker's tools) (DC 12)[/u] to ensure it's done without further damage.[/ul]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the Clockwork Tower activates a defense. The tower uses a random defense , or the DM can choose one.

[ol]
[li][b]Time Dilation:[/b] A section of the lobby warps time, making actions within it faster or slower. This can hasten or slow players, affecting their speed and action economy for one round as though affected by the Haste or Slow spell.

[li][b]Temporal Echo:[/b] An illusory duplicate of one of the PCs appears, mimicking their actions but operating on a delay. This echo might distract or confuse players, potentially misdirecting their attacks.
[/ol]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409110]|Clockwork Sentinal[/spoiler]
[spoiler][block:1409069]|Clockwork Guardian[/spoiler]

[h1]NPC[/h1]
@[Tick and Tock](person:98040fff-4014-41dc-b234-0f005a4e944f)

articles.title: CT: Clockwork Tower Exterior
articles.content:
An ancient and mysterious mechanical tower that has just sprung up in the middle of the city. It can only be accessed by playing the "Song of the Gears". If the PCs are able to access the Clockwork Tower, they will enter at the @[Chronos Lobby](plot:50f71366-e513-49e2-8758-c82cf2ea30d9) in the Clockwork Tower section.

[aloud]
Before you stands an enormous tower rising into the sky like a monolith of brass and iron. It's a whirlwind of gears and pistons, all moving with a beautiful, orchestrated precision that speaks to an incredible, advanced design. The sound is overwhelming, a continuous cacophony of whirs, clicks, and the soft puffing of steam. The only entrance appears to be a great circular door adorned with intricate clockwork mechanisms, a kaleidoscope of interlocking gears and cogs.
[/aloud]

[h2]Entrance Options[/h2]
The party has two potential methods of entry. The first, perhaps more obvious option is to confront the tower's defenses directly. A successful [u]DC 15 Investigation check [/u] reveals an array of intricate traps protecting the entrance (see below), all complex clockwork mechanisms inextricably linked to the door's opening mechanism. Daring or foolhardy adventurers might attempt to bypass or disable these traps, risking injury or worse in the process.

Alternatively, the group may seek a safer, more proper entry into the tower by way of a key of some sort. That method is found with the aid of @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23), a direct descendant of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) and the current custodian of a unique music box that plays the "Song of the Gears". When played correctly, this melodious key has the power to safely disable the tower's traps and open the way forward. Olivia, however, may not part with such a valuable family heirloom so easily. The adventurers will need to persuade her of their noble intentions or assist her in some significant way to gain her trust and the much- needed access to the Clockwork Tower.

[h1]Traps[/h1]
[h2]Steam Jet[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Description:[/b] When a gear is turned incorrectly, a hidden pipe releases a jet of scalding steam.

[li][b]Detection:[/b] A successful [u]DC 15 Perception check[/u] allows a character to hear the faint hissing of steam and notice the moisture around the pipe's hidden outlet.

[li][b]Disable:[/b] A [u]DC 13 Dexterity check[/u] (using thieves' tools) can disable this trap.

[li][b]Triggered:[/b] When triggered, PCs in a 5-foot radius must make a [u]DC 12 Dexterity saving throw[/u]. On a failed save, they take 2d6 fire damage. [roll:2d6][/ul]

[h2]Slamming Piston[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Description:[/b] Hidden within the metallic walls, a large piston is ready to slam out if the incorrect sequence of gears is turned. It is painted to blend seamlessly with the surrounding walls.

[li][b]Detection:[/b] A [u]DC 15 Investigation check[/u] allows a character to notice the slight difference in coloration and texture on the section of the wall where the piston is housed.

[li][b]Disable: [/b]A [u]DC 12 Dexterity check[/u] (using thieves' tools) can disable this trap.

[li][b]Triggered:[/b] When triggered, a PC standing in front of it must make a [u]DC 13 Dexterity saving throw[/u]. On a failed save, they take 2d10 bludgeoning damage and are knocked prone. [roll:2d10]
[/ul]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
The Clockwork Tower can take a action to cause one of the following effects:

[ul][li][b]Disconcerting Din:[/b] The tower emits a series of whirring and clanking noises, disorienting the party. Each PC must make a [u]DC 15 Wisdom saving throw[/u] or be unable to take reactions until the next round. 

[li][b]Pummeling Pistons:[/b] Pistons on the walls move in and out erratically. Each PC must make a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u] or take 2d10 [roll:2d10] bludgeoning damage from being hit by a piston.
[/ul]

articles.title: CT: Council Chambers
articles.content:
This scene takes place in the @[Hall of the Council of Boroughs](landmark:8d84988c-9527-4822-9201-5cdb110dce34), next to the @[Mayor's Palace](landmark:c0ab8179-cf4b-415f-b825-6c2535ae893b).

The Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185), tasks the adventurers with investigating the mysterious appearance and potential danger of the Clockwork Tower. Mayor @[Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185) could provide the necessary mirrors to restore the @[Echo Chamber](plot:de51804f-2855-42d2-aeb1-a8d4a340032f). However, she might request assistance in dealing with the Paradox Phantoms that have started appearing in the Council Chambers, causing havoc with their temporal abilities.

[aloud]
As you step into the grand Council Chamber, you're immediately struck by the air of authority that fills the space. Tall windows allow sunlight to pour in, casting a warm glow on the polished hardwood floor. The room is dominated by a large semi-circular desk where council mamer sit. Opposite that is a table for delegates wishing present or simply obseve the procedings. Between them are a speaker's podium and council secretary's desk.

The room resonates with a palpable sense of power, reflecting the weight of decisions that shape the community. Yet, something is not right. The air itself seems to quiver, almost as if vibrating to a frequency you can't quite hear. From the corners of your eyes, you catch fleeting glimpses of ghostly figures, vanishing as quickly as they appear.
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
[b]Identify @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23):[/b] Mayor Windermere hopes to convince the PCs to help the town. She will tell them of the relationship between @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23) and the Clockwork Tower.

[b]Deal with the Paradox Phantoms:[/b] Mayor Windermere is plagued by these temporal nuisances, which have been causing chaos in the Council Chambers. If the PCs can get rid of them, she will be more inclined to assist them in their quest.

At some point in the conversation with the Mayor, two Paradox Phantoms will appear and fly around room, causing time shifts.

[b]The Crate:[/b] Investigation will show the crate to be labled "Extremely Fragile - Mirrors".

[b]Obtain the Mirrors:[/b] The mayor has the necessary mirrors required to restore the @[Echo Chamber](plot:de51804f-2855-42d2-aeb1-a8d4a340032f) inside the Clockwork Tower. However, she will only hand them over once the PCs have dealt with the Paradox Phantoms and shown that they are capable and trustworthy. If the PCs ask why the mirrors are in the Council Chambers, Mayor Windermere will explain that the mirrors have been ordered as part of a new cultural display titled by a local artist as “Self¬Reflection.” The mirrors will be needed to repair the @[Echo Chamber](plot:de51804f-2855-42d2-aeb1-a8d4a340032f) in the Clockwork Tower.

[h1]Lair Actions[/h1]
At initiative count 20 (losing all ties), the [b]Paradox Phantom[/b] can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects:

[ul][li][b]Temporal Shift:[/b] The phantom blinks out of time and space, reappearing at another location within the Council Chambers that it can see.

[li][b]Temporal Disruption:[/b] The phantom causes a minor time anomaly. Up to two creatures that the phantom can see must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or be affected by [spoiler]
[h3][b]Slow[/b][/h3]
3rd-level transmutation

[b]Casting Time:[/b] 1 action[br]
[b]Range:[/b] 120 feet[br]
[b]Components:[/b] V, S, M (a drop of molasses)[br]
[b]Duration:[/b] Concentration, up to 1 minute[br]

You alter time around up to six creatures of your choice in a 40-foot cube within range. Each target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by this spell for the duration.

An affected target’s speed is halved, it takes a -2 penalty to AC and Dexterity saving throws, and it can’t use reactions. On its turn, it can use either an action or a bonus action, not both. Regardless of the creature’s abilities or magic items, it can’t make more than one melee or ranged attack during its turn.

If the creature attempts to cast a spell with a casting time of 1 action, roll a d20. On an 11 or higher, the spell doesn’t take effect until the creature’s next turn, and the creature must use its action on that turn to complete the spell. If it can’t, the spell is wasted.

A creature affected by this spell makes another Wisdom saving throw at the end of each of its turns. On a successful save, the effect ends for it.[br]|Slow[/spoiler] until the next initiative count 20.

[li][b]Chrono Echo:[/b] The phantom manifests [mark]echoes of past meetings and discussions[/mark] in the Council Chambers. These echoes provide a distraction and cause all creatures other than the Phantom to have [u]disadvantage on Wisdom (Perception) checks[/u] until the next initiative count 20.[/ul]


[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409609]|Paradox Phantom (x2)[/spoiler]

[h1]NPC[/h1]
Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185)

articles.title: CT: Echo Chamber
articles.content:
This room, with its shattered mirrors and dull echo, is where the players can often hear the fractured advice from the Echo of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17). It's in this chamber that the spirit of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) resonates, caught between moments in time. 

Note: Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185) could provide the necessary mirrors to restore this room.

[aloud]As you step into the chamber, a haunting stillness envelops you. The walls are lined with fragmented mirrors of various sizes, each reflecting distorted images of the room and its occupants. The floor and ceiling seem to hum slightly, a low resonance echoing through the chamber. Occasionally, there's a whisper or a faint murmur, like a voice carried on a distant wind.

Then a soft, fragmented voice emerges from the stillness, at once familiar and alien, filled with both wisdom and sorrow. It sounds strained and distant, as if fighting through layers of time. "Adventurers... the mirrors, they are my link to this tower, to all of you. The fractures... they scatter my thoughts, my essence. Without them whole, my counsel is but echoes lost to time. Please...aid me. Restore them. And I shall be your guide in this place of chaos."

Following the spectral plea, the atmosphere grows tense. You see the scattered shards and the broken mirrors reflecting this fragmented soul of an older man with salt and pepper hair, the agelines in his turned in an expression of urgency. The resonance in the room hints at the importance of the mirrors, not just as a means of communication, but perhaps as a key to understanding and navigating the tower's many enigmas.[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The players must repair the shattered mirrors in the Echo Chamber to reestablish a clear connection with Constantine's ghostly essence.

[b]Tick and Tock:[/b]
[aloud][b]Regarding the @[Echo Chamber](plot:de51804f-2855-42d2-aeb1-a8d4a340032f):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Echoes broken, mirrors lost! Mayor Elspeth knows the cost!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Phantoms of P-p-paradox play, in @[Council Chambers](plot:f8ec5847-9a50-4741-86c5-eb60047c10e6), causing disarray!"[/ul]

[b]Regarding the Echo of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17):[/b]
[ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! Constantine's s-s-song, trapped in time, all along!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Wise yet wild, the inventor's spirit, time's lost child!"[/ul][/aloud]

[h1]Gathering Materials [/h1]
While some pieces of the original mirrors remain in the room, they are too fragmented and damaged to recreate the original mirrors' clarity and power.

If the party has already visited Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185), they might recall her recent shipment of full panel mirrors in a crate on the floor of the Council Chamber. The DM can call for a straight Intelligence check to see if anyone in the party remembers or makes the connection to the mayor's collection of mirrors. If not, subtle hints dropped by the environment or even a brief, clearer connection with Constantine can nudge them in the direction of seeking her out.

[container:indent-40][b]Example:[/b] A fleeting image of the mayor's house or a voice whispering, "Seek the mayor... she holds reflections of the past."[/container]

Upon visiting Mayor Windermere and inquiring about the mirrors, she acknowledges possessing mirrors with the unique property to hold and reflect ethereal energies. She's willing to part with them either for a price - a favor to be named in the future - or if she's convinced of the urgency of the situation.

[h1]Installation[/h1]
Once the mirrors are obtained, installing them in the Echo Chamber requires precision. The mirrors should be placed in a specific pattern to channel Constantine's essence properly. Players can attempt an [u]Arcana check to discern the correct placement[/u].

[ul][li][b]DC 10[/b] for a basic understanding of where the mirrors might go.
[li][b]DC 15[/b] to gain insight into the arcane intricacies of the setup.
[li][b]DC 20[/b] to perfectly align the mirrors, ensuring the clearest connection with Constantine.[/ul]

Alternatively, if the players have a blueprint or notes from other parts of the tower, they can use them as a guide, [u]granting them advantage on the check[/u].

[h1]Consequences of Repair[/h1] 
Once the mirrors are correctly installed, a clear, though still ethereal, image of Constantine forms in the combined reflection of the mirrors. He appears as a dignified older man, his face etched with both wisdom and sorrow. His gratitude is palpable, and he is now far more willing and able to aid the party, offering clearer advice and guidance.

However, if the mirrors are improperly placed, the connection remains unstable. Constantine's image flickers and distorts, and while he can offer some help, it might occasionally come in riddles or fragmented sentences, making the party's journey in the tower more challenging.

[h1]Constantine's Plea to Stabilize the Tower[/h1]
Once the mirrors in the Echo Chamber have been fixed and Constantine can communicate effectively, he will convey to the party the pressing needs of the tower. His message will be riddled with urgency, remorse for his past mistakes, and hope that they might succeed where he failed. Read aloud or paraphrase to the party:

[aloud]
The ethereal figure of Constantine forms in the shimmering reflection of the newly restored mirrors. His translucent visage contorts with a mix of relief and despair.

"Adventurers," he begins, his voice echoing through the chamber with a hollow resonance, "my Clockwork Tower, a marvel of time and engineering, now stands on the precipice of collapse. I need your aid to right my wrongs.

"In my zeal and ambition, I left much undone. As you ascend, you will find my research, incomplete designs, and abandoned constructs. The time control device, my magnum opus, remains unfinished, a testament to my hubris. Seek the blueprint, lost in the maze of gears and pendulums. Only by completing my work can you restore harmony to the tower's temporal core.

"Time, while a fascinating subject of study, is an unforgiving master. The tower groans and grinds, its once seamless operations marred by decay and neglect. Every jammed door, every grinding gear, is a cry for help. 

"The very heart of the tower flutters erratically, like a bird trapped in a cage. Power surges and wanes, causing rooms to vanish, constructs to go awry, and time itself to warp." His voice grows desperate, "You must find an alternative source of power or stabilize the existing source." 

He looks at you, hope gleaming in his spectral eyes, "Help me set things right. Restore the Clockwork Tower to its former glory. Only then can I find peace, and the tower continue its journey."
[/aloud]

Throughout their time in the Clockwork Tower, Constantine's guidance can be sought at key junctures. Depending on the actions and choices of the players, he may offer further clues, riddles, or even direct interventions. Remember to use him judiciously, as too much assistance can make the adventure less challenging, but too little might leave the party feeling directionless.

[h3]Hints[/h3]
[b]Regarding unfinished work:[/b] [i]"Seek the blueprint, lost in the maze of gears and pendulums. Only by completing my work can you restore harmony to the tower's temporal core."[/i] [ul][li]Reference to the Chronos Codex (see @[CT: Artificer's Library](plot:f1faca19-130f-4e86-a79b-ca1bfd5d6354)) [/ul]

[b]Regarding wear and tear:[/b] [i]"Throughout the tower you might find hidden caches of spare parts, or search the junkyard. Use these parts to restore what has degraded. You may also call upon the tower's constructs, some of whom remain loyal to the tower's purpose and may assist you."[/i][ul][li]Parts might be found in the Tower or in the @[Great Junkyard](plot:e9b17b41-1091-4b00-8c06-32e8eae89575).[/ul]

[b]Regarding unstable power:[/b] [i]"Delve into the tower's depths, or perhaps master the arcane energies that pulse through its walls."[/i] [ul][li]Reference to the Earthheart Emeralds that must be channeled into fuel in the @[Timepiece of Eternity](plot:5d14ce6a-79be-4565-af70-1c41911df5ba) Room.[/ul]


[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 the Echo Chamber can take one of the following actions:

[ol]
[li][b]Reflective Confusion:[/b] The shattered pieces of the mirrors levitate and spin, reflecting disorienting lights throughout the room. All creatures in the room must make a [u]DC 15 Wisdom saving throw[/u] or be stunned until the end of their next turn.

[li][b]Temporal Shift:[/b] Constantine's connection to the room's temporality is strong and time speeds up or slows down momentarily. Roll a d6: On a 1-3, all creatures in the room benefit from the Haste spell for a round. On a 4-6, all creatures in the room become affected by the Slow spell for a round.

[li][b]Ethereal Whispers:[/b] Whispered secrets from various timelines flood the room. All creatures must make a DC 15 Intelligence saving throw or become frightened until the end of their next turn as they hear disconcerting truths from possible futures or pasts.
[/ol]

[h1]NPC[/h1]
Echo of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17)

articles.title: CT: Gear Foundry
articles.content:
This massive room is filled with dormant machinery, including a massive, cold forge at the center. It's in need of high-quality metal and coal to reignite the flame. The multi-armed automaton, @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0), can be found here, endlessly attempting to stoke the dead fires.

[aloud]As the grand doors creak open, a chorus of metallic groans fills the air, joining the ever-present ticking from the heart of the Clockwork Tower. Before you stretches a colossal chamber, its expanse punctuated by silent, hibernating machinery in the dim lighting. Dominating the scene, a once mighty forge lies cold and still, awaiting the materials required to breathe life into its embers. 

Your eyes are drawn to a towering, silhouetted figure which, after some moments of observation, proves to be that of a giant construct actively using its many appendages for various functions. Though the unique automaton remains mostly stationary, its myriad arms continue working tirelessly, if futilely, to kindle the forge's dormant fires. A grand plaque advertising Haven's Ironworks as the builder of the forge.[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The primary function of @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) is the upkeep of the Clockwork Tower. Combat isn't its forte, and clever players might negotiate or utilize its repair abilities.  @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) prefers to avoid combat if possible. If confronted, it tries to push away intruders using its hydraulic arms, dealing non-lethal damage.

[b]Cogsprocket Spiders:[/b] 3-5 spiders come to investigate the disturbance, curious more than aggressive. They might bite if cornered or if any harm comes to the machinery they're working on.

[b]Tick and Tock[/b]
[aloud][ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! The forge lies c-c-cold, needs metal and coal, so b-b-bold!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! Gearhounds b-b-bark and guard, fetching metal might be hard!"[/ul][/aloud]

[h1]Clues to the Gear Foundry's Needs[/h1]
[h2]Dormant Machinery[/h2] The first noticeable sign is the dormant machinery. Large contraptions are still and silent. Those with a keen sense for machinery or experience in blacksmithing (calling for either a straight Intelligence check (using smith's tools or tinker's tools) or an [u]Intelligence (Investigation) check (DC 14)[/u] might recognize that the contraptions are lacking fuel (see Blueprint, below).

[h2]Cold Forge[/h2] The central forge is cold to the touch, not a hint of residual warmth. Scattered around are remnants of coal and smelted metal, but these are old and insufficient to reignite the forge. A successful [u]DC 12 Intelligence check (using smith's tools)[/u] might reveal that the forge needs high-quality coal to restart. 

[h2]M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.[/h2] @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) is seen trying to reignite the forge, but without success. Occasionally, the automaton emits a series of frustrated mechanical noises, almost like sighs. If observed for a while, adventurers may notice it trying to use whatever residual resources are available in a futile effort to stoke the dead fires.

[h2]Blueprint on a Worktable[/h2] On a dusty table, there's a faded blueprint showing a diagram of the forge's power source. It mentions the specific fuel needed - high- quality metal, @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71), and an unknown energy source represented by a green gem. A successful [u]DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check[/u] reveals that the gem is probably a type of energy-focusing crystal.

[h1]Interaction with M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.[/h1]
Players who approach @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) for interaction could have a few paths to discover the needs of the Gear Foundry:

[h2]Nonverbal Communication[/h2]
@[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) communicates through a series of mechanical noises, which can be deciphered with a DC 18 Intelligence (Arcana) or Wisdom (Insight) check. Success will allow players to understand its plea for resources.

[h2]Manual Onboarding[/h2]
@[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) may try to lead a character to the blueprint or the forge, pointing at the missing elements in a display of urgency.

[h2]Repair Capability[/h2]
If the players ask or gesture for repairs, @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) might indicate (either by answering persistent yes/no questions with a nod or a headshake, or through its own communication system) that it lacks the resources to make significant repairs. This could serve as a hint for players to figure out the needs of the Gear Foundry.

[h2]M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.'s Nonverbal Communication[/h2]
[container:tablebox-100]
[table]
[tr]
    [th]Beep Sequence[/th]
    [th]Meaning [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Short beep - Short beep - Long beep[/td]
    [td]Greetings/Hello [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Long beep - Long beep[/td]
    [td]No/Negative [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Short beep - Short beep[/td]
    [td]Yes/Affirmative [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Alternating high-pitched squeak and low-pitched beep rapidly repeated three times [/td]
    [td]Alert/Warning/Danger [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Two short beeps followed by two long beeps[/td]
    [td]Please follow me/This way [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Ascending series of squeaks, increasing in pitch[/td]
    [td]Confusion/Query [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Long beep followed by three rapid short beeps[/td]
    [td]Resources needed/Urgency [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Prolonged, low-pitched hum gradually fading out[/td]
    [td]Sadness/Despair [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Short beep - Pause - Short beep - Short beep[/td]
    [td]Thank you [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Quick series of uneven squeaks and beeps, erratic in nature[/td]
    [td]Malfunction/Error [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Series of rhythmic, consistent beeps, similar to a heartbeat[/td]
    [td]All is well/Content [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Descending series of beeps, decreasing in pitch[/td]
    [td]Powering down/Rest [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Single long, high-pitched squeak [/td]
    [td]Pain/Damage [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Three short beeps followed by a long beep[/td]
    [td]Awaiting instructions/Ready [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Three rapid short beeps followed by a long beep - Short pause - Ascending series of squeaks, increasing in pitch[/td]
    [td]"Materials required. What are they?" [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Two short beeps followed by a low-pitched hum - Short beep - Short beep - Long beep[/td]
    [td]"Need @[Haven Steel](material:d3e332b3-51f9-4013-ae98-611d3b0d36c6). Essential." [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Two short beeps followed by a medium-pitched hum - Two short beeps[/td]
    [td]"Need @[Ironwood Coal](material:4bbbbf5a-8a88-4be1-bbe2-9c1781cf2c71). Important." [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td]Two short beeps followed by a high-pitched squeak - Short beep - Short beep - Long beep[/td]
    [td]"Need @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d). Vital." [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[/container]

[h1]Wear and Tear Restoration Tasks[/h1]
[h2]Rusty Forge Grate ---> fixed[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Remove rust and restore its sturdiness.
[li][b]Description:[/b] Requires either [u]manual scraping[/u] and a coat of rust-resistant material or the use of a [u]Mending spell[/u]. For manual scraping, a [u]Strength check (DC 10)[/u] is necessary. If using rust-resistant material, a [u]Dexterity check (using smith's tools or tinker's tools) DC 12[/u] ensures even application.[/ul]

[h2]Cold Forge[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Gather high-quality metal and coal.
[li][b]Description:[/b] Players must journey to Haven's Ironworks to obtain the required materials. Once acquired, reigniting the forge requires a successful [u]Intelligence check (smith's tools) DC 14[/u] or using a [u]fire-based spell in combination with a DC 14 Arcana check[/u].[/ul]

[h2]Jammed Gears in Machinery[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Unjam the gears and lubricate the machinery.
[li][b]Description:[/b] A [u]Strength check (DC 13)[/u] is required to move the jammed gears. Then, a [u]Dexterity check (using tinker's tools) DC 10[/u] is necessary to lubricate the machinery without causing any more jams.[/ul]

[h2]Misaligned Conveyor System ---> fixed[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Realign the conveyors so materials move smoothly.
[li][b]Description:[/b] An [u]Intelligence check (using tinker's tools) DC 15[/u] ensures the conveyors are aligned properly. A Natural 1 may cause temporary damage, requiring the parts to be replaced or repaired with a Mending spell.[/ul]

[h1]Incomplete Work for the Gear Foundry[/h1]
[h2]Incomplete Metalworks[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Finish shaping the half-completed metalworks scattered around the Gear Foundry.
[li][b]Description:[/b] Requires an [u]Intelligence check (using smith's tools) DC 12[/u]. Success means the work is finished to a high standard, while failure might mean the work needs to be melted down and started over.[/ul]

[h2]Uncalibrated Instruments[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Task:[/b] Calibrate the measurement instruments in the Gear Foundry to ensure accurate readings.
[li][b]Description:[/b] Players must spend about 30 minutes working with the instruments, requiring an [u]Intelligence check (using tinker's tools) DC 14[/u] to ensure precision.[/ul]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing all ties), @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) can activate a lair action, choosing from:

[ol]
[li][b]Overdrive:[/b] The dormant machines roar to life briefly, making a 20-foot radius area difficult terrain. Any creature in this zone at the start of their turn or entering it must make a [u]DC 15 Dexterity save[/u] or sustain 2d6 bludgeoning damage from errant machinery. [roll:2d6]

[li][b]Ventilation Release:[/b] Steam gushes from floor vents, creating a heavily obscured area in a 20-foot radius, lasting until the subsequent lair action.

[li][b]Automaton Aid:[/b] @[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0) deploys miniature repair drones (use Modron stats). 2-4 drones appear, focusing on repairing any damaged constructs or hindering adventurers.
[/ol]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409605]|Cogsprocket Spider[/spoiler]

[h1]NPC[/h1]
@[M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.](person:7267ca0f-4f12-4940-b565-5799dc7901b0)

articles.title: CT: Great Junkyard
articles.content:
Located on the outskirts of the city, this massive junkyard is where obsolete and broken machinery ends up. Adventurous scavengers might find useful spare parts among the piles of discarded clockwork.

[aloud]
As you crest a hill on the outskirts of Haven, the sprawling expanse of the Great Junkyard unfurls before you. A graveyard of discarded machinery and forgotten inventions, it spans as far as the eye can see. Mountains of rusted gears, shattered clockwork mechanisms, and the skeletal frames of obsolete automata lie in organized chaos. Despite the decay, the air hums with an uncanny energy, and you can't shake the feeling of countless invisible eyes watching you.
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
This encounter takes place as the party ventures into the junkyard seeking parts to repair the grand clock in the @[Chronos Lobby](plot:50f71366-e513-49e2-8758-c82cf2ea30d9) of the Clockwork Tower. 

Their main opponents are the [b]Rust Engineers[/b], the spectral remnants of mechanics forever bound to their last moments of life. The specters are not innately hostile but will fiercely defend the machinery they are committed to repair. There are [u]at least 3 Rust engineers in the junkyard but only one will be encountered at a time[/u].

[h1]Items in the Great Junkyard[/h1]
The following items can be found in the Great Junkyard. Some items are necessary to fix broken parts within the Clockwork Tower:

[ul]
[li][b]Chronosmith's Balance Wheel:[/b] The balance wheel of a clock created by @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) himself is necessary to replace the damaged part in the @[Chronos Lobby](plot:50f71366-e513-49e2-8758-c82cf2ea30d9) clock. Its metallic composition, unique to the master clockmaker, can withstand the temporal energies of the Clockwork Tower. Finding this item is quite difficult, as it is small and looks like many of the other discarded wheels in the junkyard [u](Investigation DC 20)[/u]. The balance wheel can be found within the rusted remains of an old clock tower timepiece that now lies disassembled and discarded.

[li][b]Temporal Mainspring:[/b] This is no ordinary mainspring. Imbued with Constantine's magic, the Temporal Mainspring can store and regulate the flow of temporal energy necessary for the @[Chronos Lobby](plot:50f71366-e513-49e2-8758-c82cf2ea30d9) clock's operation. It requires a keen eye to spot this amongst the sea of ordinary springs [u](Investigation DC 18)[/u]. This can be found tightly coiled within a broken Gearhound, its energy seemingly depleted.

[li][b]Timeless Pivot:[/b] A seemingly mundane clock pivot, but crafted from a unique, unaging metal alloy by Constantine, making it resistant to the wear of time. Distinguishing it from other pivots can be quite a challenge [u](Investigation DC 17)[/u]. The pivot is lodged within a mangled clockwork spider that was once responsible for maintenance.

[li][b]Everlasting Spring:[/b] A tiny, but densely wound clockwork spring. Any mechanical device that uses this spring as a component never needs to be wound up or recharged. The spring is located within an old, rusted grandfather clock, its magic keeping the timepiece barely ticking even now [u](Investigation DC 18)[/u].

[li][b]Crank of Quickening:[/b] This ornate hand crank is made from an enchanted alloy. When attached to a mechanical device and turned, it halves the amount of time required for the device to complete its task. The crank is hidden within the discarded remains of a mechanical forge, a testament to an inventor's ambitious project that never saw completion [u](Investigation DC 16)[/u].

[li][b]Glowing Lubricant Vial:[/b] A vial containing a glowing, slightly warm lubricant. When applied to a mechanical device, it ensures the device operates silently for 24 hours. The vial is tucked away in an old, unlocked safe along with other now useless items [u](Investigation DC 19)[/u].





[li][b]Phantom Gear:[/b] A ghostly, translucent gear that phases in and out of existence. When added to a construct or clockwork creature, it provides the creature with the ability to move through other creatures and objects as if they were difficult terrain. This gear is on top of a towering heap of scrap, occasionally flickering into existence [u](Perception DC 22)[/u].

[li][b]Echoing Cog:[/b] A seemingly ordinary cog, but when listened to closely, whispers of past conversations can be heard. The cog can be used to replay any conversation that occurred within 30 feet of it during the last 24 hours. This cog is found near an old assembly line of automatons, having heard and stored countless dialogues over the years [u](Investigation DC 20)[/u].

[li][b]Constantine's Chronoscrews:[/b] These incredibly rare screws were crafted by Constantine himself. A construct or mechanical device repaired using these screws has its hit points maximized. The screws are found in a small, rusted tin box buried under layers of dust and discarded mechanical parts [u](Investigation DC 24)[/u].

[li][b]Resonating Wire:[/b] This length of wire vibrates with a faint hum. When attached to a musical instrument, it can play any tune the user has heard before, perfectly and without need for a performer. This wire can be found wrapped around the neck of a broken violin, its previous owner having left it behind [u](Investigation DC 16)[/u].

[li][b]Temporal Fragment:[/b] A glowing shard of a once larger clock, this fragment flickers with an ethereal light. Can be used as a component in powerful time-altering spells or to fuel time-based magical devices. This fragment lies close to the rusted remains of a mysterious, unidentifiable machine of considerable size [u](Investigation DC 21)[/u].

[li][b]Self-Tightening Bolts:[/b] Once screwed, these bolts will never loosen on their own. They are useful for permanently fixing joints or parts together in mechanical constructs. These bolts are found in a small pouch inside the toolbox of a long-abandoned workbench [u](Investigation DC 17)[/u].

[li][b]Relic of the Master Artificer:[/b] A small tool that once belonged to @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17). It might not have any magical or mechanical properties, but as a piece of history, it could be worth quite a lot to the right collector or could be used to gain the favor of @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23). The tool can be identified with the initials “CC” in Constantine's distinctive handwriting engraved on the base of the relic. This item is found inside an old, locked chest, the lock having rusted over time [u](Investigation DC 23)[/u].

[/ul]

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
The junkyard takes an action to cause one of the following effects; the same effect can't be used two rounds in a row:

[ol]
[li][b]Construct Rumble:[/b] The junkyard rumbles as discarded machines spontaneously assemble themselves into hostile constructs. Summon 1d4 animated armors [roll:1d4]  (or other suitable constructs) that will join the battle at the start of the next round.

[li][b]Debris Cloud:[/b] A spectral wind sweeps through the junkyard, stirring up a cloud of dust and debris. The area within 120 feet of the party becomes heavily obscured until initiative count 20 on the next round. 

[li][b]Spectral Assault:[/b] The spectral essence of the Rust Engineers infuses the machinery in the junkyard. All creatures within 30 feet of a piece of machinery must succeed on a [u]DC 13 Dexterity saving throw[/u] or take 3d6 necrotic damage [roll:3d6] as spectral tools emerge from the machinery to attack.
[/ol]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409614]|Rust Engineer[/spoiler]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop:
[b]Location[/b][br]
Southwest of the city, a mile outside Horse Gate

[hr]
[var:ct-toc-phre3d]

articles.title: CT: Haven City Archives
articles.content:
A vast repository of the city's historical records, where the renowned scholar Professor @[Thaddeus Gearhardt](person:b1e28c32-ce9e-479c-8860-1cfc4ce08cc7) can be found, along with the blueprints of the Clockwork Tower. The PCs will need the blueprints to help finish the construction of the tower.

[aloud]
As you enter the main room of Haven's City Archives, a sense of age and wisdom permeates the area. The air is filled with the scent of old parchment and polished wood. Rows upon rows of towering bookshelves fill the grand hall, each one overloaded with documents, blueprints, scrolls, and books. The faint whisper of turning pages echoes through the grand hall, blending with the occasional hushed conversation. 
[/aloud]


When they find the Professor's office...
[aloud]
At the end of the hall a cluttered office becomes visible. Filled with stacks of books, half-finished clockwork contraptions, and scattered blueprints, it's a veritable hurricane of academic pursuit. At its center, behind mountains of papers, you hear the sound of someone busily scribbling notes. After a moment, an elderly man with tousled gray hair peers curiously over the wall of books lining his desk. He squints at the party through his spectacles, which are perched precariously on the end of his nose. "Oh, hello... I didn't see you there.”
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The City Archives are managed by Professor @[Thaddeus Gearhardt](person:b1e28c32-ce9e-479c-8860-1cfc4ce08cc7), who over time has become the beating heart of the city's knowledge and rich history. His extensive knowledge can help the PCs find blueprints and instructions on how to fix the Clockwork Tower.
