Here are ways in which the PCs can convince Professor @[Thaddeus Gearhardt](person:b1e28c32-ce9e-479c-8860-1cfc4ce08cc7) to help them:

[container:indent-40]
[h3][b]Persuasion[/b][/h3] Professor Gearhardt is a passionate scholar and generally amiable, but he values his time. Characters may need to successfully use persuasion to convince him to aid them, demonstrating that their cause aligns with his interests. A successful DC 15 Persuasion check would likely be enough to pique his interest, though if characters mention @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), the Clockwork Tower, or any related topics, they should be given advantage on the roll.

[h3][b]Bribery[/b][/h3] As a scholar, Gearhardt is more interested in knowledge than gold. Instead of traditional bribery, PCs can offer rare books, artifacts, or information that they have found during their adventures. This "intellectual bribe" could reduce the DC of the subsequent Persuasion check or eliminate the need for it altogether.

[h3][b]Helping Hand[/b][/h3] Professor Gearhardt has many projects and research lines going on at once, so the PCs could offer to assist him with some of his work. Perhaps the professor needs a rare book retrieved from the depths of the archives, or a particularly stubborn clockwork device fixed. This could take the form of a skill challenge, with successful checks leading to the professor's assistance.

[h3][b]Demonstration of Skills[/b][/h3] Professor Gearhardt appreciates practical demonstrations of knowledge and skill. If a character has relevant skills (such as Arcana, History, or proficiency with artisan's tools), they could show off these skills to impress the professor. Successfully doing so could win his favor and aid.

[h3][b]Shared Goals[/b][/h3] If the PCs can convincingly explain how their goals align with Gearhardt's own academic interests, he will be more than happy to help them. This could involve emphasizing the historical significance of their quest, the potential for new discoveries, or the direct relation to the Clockwork Tower and Chronosmith's legacy.
[/container]


[h1]Documents in the Archives[/h1]
Below is a list of the documents that can be found in the archives:

[h3][b]Chronosmith's Preliminary Sketches[/b][/h3] This is a bound folio of Constantine's original sketches for the Clockwork Tower. While many of these ideas did not make it into the final design, they give an insight into Constantine's thought process and can be used to help understand and predict the logic behind other elements of the tower's design.

[h3][b]Temporal Matrix Blueprints[/b][/h3] Detailed diagrams outlining the construction of a complex arcane device dubbed the "Temporal Matrix". It appears to be a pivotal component of the Clockwork Tower, presumably serving as its heart, regulating the flow of temporal energies throughout the structure. If the Temporal Matrix in the Clockwork Tower is malfunctioning or incomplete, this blueprint would be instrumental in repairing or finishing it.

[h3][b]Chronosmith's Personal Journals[/b][/h3] Professor Gearhardt has managed to obtain a collection of Chronosmith's personal journals. They contain notes, musings, and a record of the inventor's struggles and successes during the tower's creation. These can provide valuable context to the PCs and help them understand Constantine's mindset, providing the reader a +2 bonus to any Intelligence-based checks made to solve or understand riddles or puzzles left behind in the tower. All journal entries are dated over 100 years ago.

[h3][b]Patent for the Automaton Worker Model M.A.I.N.T.E.N.A.N.C.E.[/b][/h3] This is the approved patent for an automaton worker designed by Chronosmith. The automaton was designed to perform various tasks, including construction and repair of complex structures like the Clockwork Tower. If the party comes across any dormant or malfunctioning automatons in the Tower, these blueprints could help repair them, possibly recruiting new allies or gaining access to otherwise unreachable areas. The date listed on the approved patent is over 100 years ago.

[h3][b]Annotated Edition of the Principles of Temporal Manipulation[/b][/h3] A book written by Chronosmith detailing the principles and theories behind manipulating time. Many notes have been scribbled in the margins by Gearhardt, providing further insight. Any character who reads this book for 1 hour receives a +2 bonus to any Intelligence-based checks to understand temporal anomalies or time-based quandaries within the Clockwork Tower.

[h3][b]Design Specifications for the Clockwork Tower's Defense System[/b][/h3] This document outlines the defense systems built into the Clockwork Tower, including traps, clockwork sentries, and potentially defensive spells. It provides valuable insight into what the party should be prepared for when entering the tower and may even hint at how to bypass or deactivate some of these defenses. This will include detailing that music, specifically the family song, can be used to bypass the traps and safely enter the Clockwork Tower.

[h1]Environmental Actions[/h1]
[ol]
[li][b]Book Barrage:[/b] A shelf of books flies open, and a whirlwind of papers fills a 20-foot-radius sphere. The area becomes heavily obscured until initiative count 20 on the next round.

[li][b]Knowledge Surge:[/b] A burst of arcane energy fills a chosen PC, temporarily granting them expertise in a chosen Intelligence-based skill until the end of their next turn.

[li][b]Clockwork Messenger:[/b] A small clockwork bird darts out from amongst the papers, delivering a short message that serves as a clue or hint for the PCs.
[/ol]


[h1]NPC[/h1]
Professor @[Thaddeus Gearhardt](person:b1e28c32-ce9e-479c-8860-1cfc4ce08cc7)

articles.title: CT: Haven Ironworks
articles.content:
A large industrial complex where most of the city's metal goods are made. The Ironworks could be a source of replacement parts, and its workers may have the expertise needed to repair some of the more complex machinery in the tower. The party might need to negotiate, do a favor, or pay for their help. Haven's Ironworks, a source of sturdy metals and coal, could replenish the @[Gear Foundry's](plot:2096333a-caf6-4548-b095-b21cfd241094) supplies. But obtaining them won't be easy. Gearhounds, loyal clockwork dogs, patrol the Ironworks and will fiercely protect their resources. The PCs will also need to find a way to move the large and heavy items.

[aloud]
As you approach Haven's Ironworks, the smell of burning coal and molten metal assails your senses. The enormous industrial complex is a hive of activity, with burly workers hauling heavy loads, stoking forges, and beating hot iron into all manner of shapes. Towering furnaces belch out plumes of smoke that blot out the sky, while the relentless clanging of hammers on anvils creates a constant, deafening cacophony. Amongst the chaos of activity, you notice the sleek forms of mechanical dogs patrolling the perimeter, their metallic bodies reflecting the orange glow of the nearby fires.
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
The following are key objectives for the PCs:

[b]Obtain Replacement Parts and Expertise:[/b] The PCs need to negotiate with the workers of the Ironworks for the necessary parts and their help in repairing the complex machinery in the Clockwork Tower.

[b]Deal with the Gearhounds:[/b] The Gearhounds will fiercely protect their territory, and the PCs must find a way to either evade, disable, or defeat them.

[b]Transport the Resources:[/b] Once the PCs have secured the parts and the help they need, they must find a way to transport the heavy materials to the Clockwork Tower. This might involve securing a cart, enlisting strong laborers, or finding a magical solution.

[h1]NPCs[/h1]
There are many people who work at the Haven Ironworks. Below is a list of the types of workers the PCs can encounter:

[ul][li][b]Dalia Forgestoker:[/b] Human, blacksmith. Dalia is the chief blacksmith at the Ironworks. She is an incredibly skilled artisan and oversees much of the work here. While she has a tough exterior, she's got a soft spot for adventurers and is willing to lend a hand, especially if the party has someone who can talk shop about smithing.

[li][b]Grimm Boulderbeard:[/b] Dwarf, foundryman. Grimm is in charge of the smelting furnaces and casting metals.
He's a gruff and somewhat surly individual who doesn't trust outsiders easily. He might be persuaded to help if the PCs can pass a [u]DC 15 Persuasion check[/u] or bribe him with a good quality ale or gold.

[li][b]Ellie Quickfingers:[/b] Halfling, tinker. Ellie is responsible for repairing machinery around the Ironworks. She's eager to help, but she's currently swamped with work. If the PCs can help fix a few broken machines around the Ironworks (requiring successful Intelligence checks (using tinker's tools) or the use of spells like Mending), she'll be free to assist them.

[li][b]Korbin "The Bear" Ironhide:[/b] Goliath, laborer. Korbin is one of the heavy lifters in the Ironworks. He's indifferent to the PCs' plight, but if they can beat him in an arm-wrestling match [u](Strength (Athletics) check with a DC of 18)[/u], he'll respect them enough to lend his considerable strength.

[li][b]Seraphina Steamspark:[/b] Gnome, engineer. Seraphina oversees any complex machinery or clockwork components. She's enthusiastic and loves new projects, but she's also scatterbrained and forgetful. If the PCs remind her of an important tool she lost (perhaps through a minor fetch quest or a high DC Investigation check in the clutter of the Ironworks), she'll be grateful enough to assist.

[li][b]Lars Koppercoil:[/b] Half-elf, accountant. Lars is responsible for the financial side of the Ironworks. He is a stickler for the rules and won't offer any assistance unless it's properly logged, recorded, and paid for. A bribe might work, but a well-reasoned argument [u](DC 17 Persuasion check)[/u] or helping him sort out a logistical error in the Ironworks' accounts could also do the trick.

[li][b]Marigold Gravelfoot:[/b] Dwarf, security chief. Marigold oversees the safety and security of the Ironworks. She's not easily swayed and is suspicious of the party's intentions. However, if the party can provide proof of Mayor Windermere's endorsement or help Marigold solve a theft problem at the Ironworks, she'll grant them access to the materials they need.
[/ul]

[h1]Raw Materials[/h1]
Below is a list of the raw materials made at the Ironworks.

[ul]
[li][b]Haven Steel:[/b] A high-quality, versatile steel alloy that's the backbone of Haven's metalworks industry. Known for its exceptional strength and durability. [b]Price:[/b] 2 gold pieces per pound.

[li][b]Ironwood Coal:[/b] Sourced from the nearby Ironwood forests, this coal burns hotter and longer than regular coal. Essential for the intense heat needed in Haven's Ironworks. [b]Price:[/b] 5 silver pieces per pound.

[li][b]Tempered Copper:[/b] This copper has been carefully treated in the Ironworks' furnaces to increase its resilience and conductivity. It's frequently used in the manufacture of various clockwork devices. [b]Price:[/b] 1 gold piece per pound.

[li][b]Chronosmith Brass:[/b] A special alloy named in honor of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), this brass is particularly resistant to wear and corrosion. It's favored in the creation of long-lasting gears and clockwork mechanisms. [b]Price:[/b] 3 gold pieces per pound.

[li][b]Elemental Iron:[/b] A rare type of iron infused with elemental energy during the smelting process. This material is highly sought after for the creation of magical items and constructs. [b]Price:[/b] 50 gold pieces per pound.

[li][b]Galvanized Tin:[/b] A protective metal known for its resistance to rusting and corrosion. It's used in the creation of weather-resistant structures and machinery. [b]Price:[/b] 8 silver pieces per pound.

[li][b]Ironworks Bronze:[/b] A durable and hardy alloy of copper and tin. Bronze from Haven's Ironworks is particularly prized for its acoustic properties and is often used in the construction of musical instruments. [b]Price:[/b] 2 gold pieces per pound.
[/ul]

[h1]Transporting Raw Materials[/h1]
Use the following to transport raw materials from the Ironworks:

[ul][li][b]Handcart:[/b] This is a simple two-wheeled cart that one person can use to transport heavy materials. Can carry up to 200 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] 2 gold pieces to rent for a day, 15 gold pieces to buy. No special skills needed.

[li][b]Draft Horse and Wagon:[/b] A strong horse hitched to a sturdy wagon. Can carry up to 2000 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] 10 gold pieces to rent for a day (includes feed for the horse), 200 gold pieces to buy. Requires an Animal Handling skill check to drive effectively.

[li][s][b]Steam Loader:[/b] This clockwork contraption has a set of articulated arms designed for lifting and moving heavy loads. Can carry up to 1000 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] 50 gold pieces to rent for a day, 800 gold pieces to buy. Requires proficiency in tinker's tools to operate.[/s]

[li][s][b]Gnomish Gyrocopter:[/b] A small, one-person flying machine capable of carrying small but heavy loads. Can carry up to 500 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] 75 gold pieces to rent for a day, 1500 gold pieces to buy. Requires proficiency in Vehicles (Air) to fly.[/s]

[li][b]Bag of Holding:[/b] A magical item that can hold much more than it should be able to, based on its size. Can carry up to 500 pounds or 64 cubic feet. [b]Payment:[/b] Not usually available for rent due to its high value, but typically costs around 500 gold pieces to buy. No special skills needed.

[li][b]Floating Disk (Tenser's Floating Disk Spell):[/b] A magical disk that hovers at waist height and can carry loads. Can carry up to 500 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] Spellcasters with this spell in their repertoire can cast it, or it could be cast for a fee by a local wizard - around 10-20 gold pieces per casting. No special skills needed beyond the ability to cast the spell.

[li][s][b]Porter Golems:[/b] Large constructs specifically designed for heavy lifting and transportation. Can carry up to 1500 pounds. [b]Payment:[/b] 100 gold pieces to rent for a day, not available for purchase as they are typically owned and operated by larger organizations. Requires a command amulet or proficiency in Arcana to operate.[/s]

[li][s][b]Haven Pneumatic Tubes:[/b] A network of pressurized tubes used for transporting goods across the city. Can carry up to 50 pounds per capsule. [b]Payment:[/b] 1 silver piece per capsule sent. No special skills needed, though larger items may require special packaging.[/s]
[/ul]

[h1]Lair Actions[/h1]
At initiative count 20 (losing all ties), the Gearhounds can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects:

[ol][li][b]Industrial Haze:[/b] The Gearhounds activate the nearby furnaces, filling the air with thick smoke. The area within 60 feet of the Gearhounds is heavily obscured until the next initiative count 20.

[li][b]Rapid Repairs:[/b] A swarm of tiny clockwork drones bursts from hidden compartments within the Ironworks, repairing the Gearhounds. Each Gearhound regains 10 hit points.

[li][b]Mechanical Overload:[/b] The Gearhounds overcharge their systems, causing sparks to fly from their bodies. Each creature within 10 feet of a Gearhound must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or take 7 (2d6) lightning damage. [roll:2d6]
[/ol]


[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409608]|Gearhound[/spoiler]

articles.title: CT: Introduction
articles.content:
[h1]Introduction[/h1]
[aloud]
As the first rays of dawn break over the horizon, an ominous shadow falls over the city of Haven. The inhabitants wake to a strange spectacle - a colossal, intricate tower stands in the heart of their city - in Guilder's Park (O16) - where nothing had been the night before. This massive edifice, humming with an unseen power, reaches towards the heavens. Its strange metallic surface shimmers in the morning light, and various parts of the tower shift and move like a grand clockwork mechanism come to life.

Meanwhile, you find yourselves in the bustling city of Haven. The city is usually alive with activity, cobblestone streets regularly filled with people and the lively sounds of busy marketplaces. Yet, an eerie quiet seems to have descended over the city today. The reason becomes quite apparent as you pass the once-empty square of Haven where a giant tower now stands, as though it had sprung up overnight.

In a short time, you are summoned to the the @[Council Chambers](plot:f8ec5847-9a50-4741-86c5-eb60047c10e6) in the @[Mayor's Palace](landmark:c0ab8179-cf4b-415f-b825-6c2535ae893b). Mayor @[Elsbeth Windermere](person:b2466c19-d35e-45af-b070-831261248185) is a pragmatic woman, known for her determination and her love for the city she governs. She greets you in the grand Council Hall, appearing grave and worried. 

Her voice echos off the tall ceiling,  as she says: “Welcome. I’m sure you’re by now aware of our... unusual predicament. We don’t know why the tower has appeared, or what it portends. That is where you come in, adventurers.” 

She looks at each of you intently. “We need your help to investigate this tower and determine if it poses any threat to our city.”
[/aloud]

articles.title: CT: Luminous Caverns
articles.content:
A series of caves in the @[Khuz Mountains](location:806c0e95-17cf-4c38-9d20-c4cbfce74d4d),  rumored to be rich in @[Earthheart Emeralds](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d). These crystals might serve as a potential alternative energy source for the tower. The caverns are treacherous and might be inhabited by subterranean creatures, making this a potential side adventure. The @[Earthheart Emeralds](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d) are also desired by Pendulum Horrors who have been lured away from the Clockwork Tower due to the energy pulsating off the crystals.

[aloud]As you journey outside the city, following old rumors and weathered maps, you find yourselves standing before the mouth of an expansive cavern. The entrance of the @[Luminous Caverns](location:db5b92b1-7c72-4bf9-8424-38f27ba0f5aa) looms before you, seemingly swallowing the daylight. Within, a faint, pulsating green light dances on the stone walls, illuminating the interior with an ethereal glow. As you tread cautiously into the cavern, the source of the glow becomes clear. Countless emeralds, radiant with a powerful and raw energy, stud the cavern walls, illuminating the cavern in an otherworldly display of lights and shadows. Their soft luminescence dances across your faces as you proceed deeper into the heart of the cavern, a mesmerizing sight that belies the potential danger lying in wait.[/aloud]

[h1]Earthheart Emeralds[/h1]
These deep green crystals are vibrant and richly colored, pulsing with a soft, inner light. Ranging in size from that of a pebble to as large as a melon, each @[Earthheart Emerald](material:63f8cdd2-8d51-4846-996e-19525db18f7d) is a marvel of nature's craftsmanship. They are raw and rugged, yet hold an untamed beauty that speaks of the elemental power locked within.


[h1]Lair Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), the Pendulum Horror can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects; it can't use the same effect two rounds in a row:

[ol]
[li][b]Crystalline Surge:[/b] The Pendulum Horror draws on the energy of the Earthheart Emeralds, causing a surge of raw, earthen energy to erupt from the cavern walls. All creatures within 15 feet of the walls must make a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u], taking 3d6 radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. [roll:3d6]

[li][b]Seismic Tremor:[/b] The Pendulum Horror taps into the cavern's natural seismic energy, causing a localized tremor. Each creature on the ground in the cavern must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u] or be knocked prone.

[li][b]Emerald Shard Eruption:[/b] Crystals from the cavern wall burst out in a shower of sharp, energized shards. The Pendulum Horror chooses a point it can see within 60 feet. Each creature within a 20-foot radius centered on that point must make a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u]. A creature takes 4d6 slashing damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. [roll: 4d6 ]
[/ol]


[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409611]|Pendulum Horror[/spoiler]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop:
[b]Location[/b][br]
250 miles northeast of the city, in the Khuz Mountains. About 4 days travel by horseback. 8 by wagon / cart.
[hr]
[var:ct-toc-phre3d]

articles.title: CT: Resolution
articles.content:
When the PCs complete fixing, repairing and fueling the Clockwork Tower, the DM can read the following resolution:

[aloud]
As the final energy source slides into place, a noticeable change envelops the Clockwork Tower. The ambient hum of machinery harmonizes into a crescendo, gears turning and levers shifting with newfound purpose. Every surface glimmers, and an unseen force seems to radiate from the tower's core.

Suddenly, out of the ether, a hazy form manifests, growing more solid with every passing second. Standing before you is a man of dignified appearance, his attire reminiscent of a bygone era, his face lined with wisdom and age, yet his eyes sparkle with life. It is @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17), fully realized as the man he once was.

For a moment, he seems lost in thought, taking in the surroundings of the reborn tower. Then, fixing his gaze upon each of you, he speaks, 

[ul]"I did not expect to walk these halls again, much less in my true form. You have done what many deemed impossible."[/ul]

Walking up to each adventurer, he offers words of gratitude, tailored to their efforts and sacrifices. 

[ul]"To you, [Adventurer 1], for your perseverance," he says, "to [Adventurer 2], for your unmatched wit," and so on.[/ul]

Finally, he gestures towards the grand door of the Clockwork Tower. 

[ul]"Your journey here has reached its end, but know that the story of this place, and your part in it, will be remembered for eons. Follow me."[/ul]

As you all traverse the intricate hallways and clockwork passageways, you feel a sense of completion and warmth, as if the tower itself is bidding you farewell. At the entrance, Constantine turns to face you, his eyes seem to hold galaxies within them as he gazes at you, each flicker of light a different moment in time. 

[ul]
"The tower, you see, was but a focal point, a hub in the infinite web of time and space. My journey never truly ends. There are still an incalculable number of timelines branching off, each with their own set of problems, wonders, and mysteries. I must be their warden, their caretaker. The role of a Chronosmith is eternal, indeed."

"First," he continues, a soft smile crossing his lips, "I plan to visit the dawn of creation. There is a disturbance there that has set ripples throughout history, affecting even the nature of magic itself. I need to investigate and, if possible, mend it."

"Next, I intend to venture to a point in time where history and myth intersect—a war in the heavens where gods and titans clashed. I've heard tales that a rogue element aimed to use time itself as a weapon. I cannot let that come to pass." 
Constantine's eyes darken a bit as he adds, "There are also moments in history burdened with such sorrow and despair that they cast long shadows over countless lives. Though some of these moments should not be changed, I can strive to understand it better, to insulate the future against repeating such mistakes." 

"Finally," he says, his eyes twinkling like stars, "I want to explore the unknown possibilities, the 'what-ifs,' the roads not taken. There are timelines where magic has evolved in unimaginable ways, or where technology has reached heights that would be considered fantastical in this realm. I yearn to see them, to learn from them."

"And through it all," Constantine concludes, "I'll be carrying the lessons I learned from each of you, and from this tower. Every cog, every tick of the clock, contributes to the vast mechanism that is time. Your lives are indelible parts of that machinery. Go forth and make your own journeys matter, just as you've made this one matter for me."
[/ul]

He pushes open the grand circular door, revealing the familiar landscape of Haven just outside. 

[ul]"Beyond this threshold, you'll find tokens of my appreciation. May they serve you well in your future endeavors."[/ul]

With a final nod, he steps back, allowing you to exit. As the last adventurer steps out and glances back, they see Constantine's enigmatic smile. The grand door closes with a soft thud, and there's a moment of uncanny stillness.
Suddenly, the Clockwork Tower, with all its grandeur, begins to shimmer and waver, becoming translucent. Like sand caught in a gust of wind, it fades, leaving behind only the memories of its wonders and the echoing laughter of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17).

In its place, neatly arranged, are ornate chests, each inscribed with a name of a different adventurer in your party. These are Chronosmith's gifts: artifacts and treasures from epochs unknown, imbued with the very essence of time. A sense of anticipation fills the air; what rewards must await you for restoring the heart of the Clockwork Tower?
[/aloud]

[h1]Rewards[/h1]
The following rewards are split depending on the level of the character.

[h2]Beginner (Level 1-5)[/h2]
[b]Chrono-Pendant:[/b] A small brass pendant in the shape of an hourglass. Once per day, as a reaction, the wearer can reroll a failed saving throw. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Temporal Sand Pouch:[/b] A tiny pouch filled with golden sands. Sprinkling it over wounds allows the user to cast the Cure Wounds spell at 1st level. Contains enough sands for three uses before the pouch is empty. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Watchful Compass:[/b] A compass that doesn't point north, but instead always points to the most immediate danger within a 1-mile radius. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Boots of the Swift Minute:[/b] These boots add +10 feet to the wearer's movement speed for the first minute of combat. Wondrous item, rare

[b]Clockwork Locket:[/b] A beautifully crafted locket with intricate designs. Inside, there's a miniature ticking clock. Worth 50 gold pieces (gp).

[b]Temporal Brass Ring:[/b] A ring fashioned from a coiled brass spring with a tiny hourglass gemstone set atop it. Worth 75 gp.

[b]Silvered Sundial:[/b] A portable sundial with silver filigree designs and an etched signature of Chronosmith. Worth 40 gp.

[b]Gilded Hourglass:[/b] A small hourglass with gold dust instead of sand. Worth 100 gp.

[h2]Intermediate (Level 6-10)[/h2]
[b]Hourglass of Short Rest:[/b] Once per day, flipping this hourglass allows the user and their allies within 30 feet to gain the benefits of a short rest in just 10 minutes. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Cloak of Lost Seconds:[/b] A cloak made from interwoven strands of silver and twilight. It grants the wearer the ability to become invisible for 12 seconds (2 rounds) once per day. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Chrono-Blade:[/b] A weapon with intricate clockwork designs. Once per day, upon hitting a target, the user can decide to freeze the target in time, effectively paralyzing it for one round. Magic weapon, rare

[b]Ring of Recalled Moments:[/b] Allows the user to memorize and replay any 10-second moment they've witnessed in perfect detail, thrice per day. Wondrous item, uncommon

[b]Golden Pocket Watch:[/b] An ornate pocket watch made of gold with Chronosmith's insignia engraved on the back. Worth 250 gp.

[b]Emerald Timepiece Pendant:[/b] A pendant featuring a miniature clock with an Earthheart Emerald face. Worth 400 gp.

[b]Platinum Sundial with Ruby Accents:[/b] A magnificent sundial adorned with tiny rubies marking each hour. Worth 600 gp.

[b]Silvered Chrono-Flute:[/b] A musical instrument with clockwork mechanisms. Playing it produces a melody reminiscent of ticking clocks. Worth 350 gp.

[h2]Advanced (Level 11+)[/h2]
[b]Temporal Armor:[/b] An intricately designed full plate. It allows the wearer to "store" a turn. During any round of combat, they can decide to take two turns in a row, but they will then lose their next turn. Magic armor, rare

[b]Chrono-Staff:[/b] A staff made of intertwining clocks and gears. It holds 10 charges. The wielder can expend charges to cast Time Stop (5 charges), Haste on an ally (2 charges), or Slow on an enemy (2 charges). The staff regains 1d6 + 4 expended charges daily at dawn. Magic staff, very rare (requires attunement)

[b]Goggles of Future Sight:[/b] When worn, these goggles allow the user to see a few seconds into the future, granting them advantage on all saving throws. It can be used for 10 minutes per day, divided as the user wishes. Wondrous item, very rare (requires attunement)

[b]Amulet of Past Echoes:[/b] Once per day, the wearer can invoke the amulet to summon an illusory double of themselves that mimics their actions from the previous round, effectively letting them repeat their last turn. Wondrous item, rare

[b]Diamond-Studded Timepiece:[/b] A grand clock adorned with diamonds on every hour mark and Chronosmith's seal in pure gold. Worth 2,500 gp.

[b]Ornate Hourglass with Dragon's Breath Sand:[/b] This hourglass, framed in mithril, contains sand said to be grains of ground dragon scale, causing it to shimmer in multicolor brilliance. Worth 3,000 gp.

[b]Golden Harp of the Ticking Melody:[/b] A harp with strings fine-tuned to produce notes that sound like the
Platinum Cufflinks with Sapphire Hourglass Inlay: High fashion cufflinks that showcase Chronosmith's craftsmanship. Worth 2,000 gp per pair.

[h1]Message from Chronosmith to the People of Haven[/h1]
[aloud]
As the Clockwork Tower disappears with the enigmatic smile of Chronosmith, the adventurers are left in awe with their respective gifts. But something more catches their eye. In the exact spot where the Clockwork Tower stood, a heavy chest adorned with clockwork mechanisms is revealed. Inscribed on it in elegant, looping script are the words "To the people of Haven." 

Upon opening the chest, you find it filled to the brim with gold coins, shimmering under the sun (a sum total of 10,000 gold pieces).  On top of the gold coins, there's a sealed envelope made of thick, royal blue parchment. An intricate wax seal bearing the mark of a timepiece encloses the flap. The envelope reads "To the Honorable Mayor of Haven." Inside the letter, written in Chronosmith's impeccable handwriting, the note says: 

[ul]
"To the Esteemed Mayor of Haven, [br][br]
[ul]
   It has been many years since I last set foot in Haven and in my zealous pursuit [br]
   of my inventions, I may have unknowingly wronged the people of this great [br]
   town. Please accept this gold as a gesture of reconciliation and to aid in the[br]
   growth and prosperity of Haven.[br][br]
   In time, all things find balance. I hope this helps tip the scales back in favor of[br]
   Haven's continued success and glory. With all due respect and apology,[br][br]
[/ul]
Constantine Chronosmith"   
[/ul]
[/aloud]

articles.title: CT: Scaling Monster Numbers
articles.content:
[container:tablebox-50]
[b]Monster #s by Party Size[/b]
[table]
[tr]    [th]Party Size[/th]    [th]Increase number by [/th][/tr]
[tr]    [td]5-8[/td]    [td]x 1.5 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]9-12[/td]  [td]x 2 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]13[/td]     [td]x 2.5 [/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]
[container:tablebox-50] 
[b]Monster #s by Party Level[/b]
[table]
[tr]    [th]Party Level[/th]    [th]Increase number by [/th][/tr]
[tr]    [td]5-8[/td]     [td]x 1.5 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]9-12[/td]   [td]x 2 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]13[/td]      [td]x 2.5 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]16+[/td]    [td]X 3.5 [/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]
Use the tables at right to determine adjustments for party size and level and then multiply these numbers together.

[container:indent-40]
For instance, if you have a Party Size of 5 with an average Party Level of 10, then you would add 1.5 + 2.5 to get 4 times the monsters. 
[/container]

Three creatures for a level 10 party are not a challenge, especially if there are 6 or 7 PC's. Increase that to 12, and suddenly you have a challenge. Feel free to adjust as you see fit.

In addition to scaling monsters, many of the traps and puzzles in the game have a sliding scale that the DM can use to change the level of difficulty depending on the skillset of the characters.

articles.title: CT: The Watchmaker
articles.content:
This encounter takes place in the home and workshop of @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23), a young and talented artificer and direct descendant of the legendary artificer @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17). This can be found on a back alley off Jewel St. (P15).

The PCs have a chance to converse with Olivia and even persuade her to share information about the “Song of the Gears”. The party can also discover the necessary tools and information to restore the Assembly Room in the Clockwork Tower.

[aloud]As you approach the shop, you see a distinctive clockwork insignia above the entrance door, signaling that you've arrived at The Watchmaker. The workshop is a building made of worn but sturdy stone. It's noticeably quiet, except for the faint rhythmic sound of ticking clocks that spills out onto the streets. As you step inside, you're met with an array of complex mechanical contraptions and fascinating timepieces lining the walls. The aroma of oil and aged wood fills the air. At a workbench towards the back of the room, a young woman with focused eyes and hands deftly adjusting a tiny mechanism catches your attention.[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
As the party enters the heart of The Watchmaker, they face three key objectives, each integral to the unraveling of the Clockwork Tower's enigma. Their mission is to 1) earn the trust of @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23), 2) acquire the "Song of the Gears" from her ancient music box, 3) and gather crucial items including Constantine's old blueprints and specialized tools, all while navigating an Aladdin's cave of remarkable clockwork creations, esoteric arcane components, and enticing curiosities.

[h3][b]Identify @[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23)[/b][/h3] The PCs need to understand that Olivia is a direct descendant of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) and thus likely has essential knowledge about the Clockwork Tower. They can achieve this through conversation, observing her work, or even spotting a family portrait or other heirloom that identifies her lineage. As a proud member of the Chronosmith family, Olivia will not hide this fact once asked directly. She will inform the party that she is the great artificer @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17)'s great-great-great-granddaughter. Neither Constantine nor his grand Clockwork Tower have been seen for nearly a century. The PCs should also learn about the "Song of the Gears", a melody only known to the Chronosmith family that Olivia can play on a music box she keeps.

[h3][b]Spring Loaded Sprites[/b][/h3] Olivia is a tinkerer and her mind is constantly searching for new challenges. The Spring Loaded Sprites are her invention to help a young, single woman running her own workshop. They provide companionship, guard her workshop and challenge her mind as she continuously works to improve their functionality.

The Spring-Loaded Sprites see the newcomers as playthings. They might start causing minor mischief like hiding tools, flying around to distract the PCs, or even attempting to 'fix' them.

[h3][b]Restore the Assembly Room in the Clockwork Tower[/b][/h3] In order to restore the Assembly Room in the Clockwork Tower, the PCs will need Olivia's help. She has kept old blueprints and a set of specialized tools that once belonged to her ancestor. These tools are in a secured cabinet in her workshop, and she'll only allow the party to use them if they can prove their intentions are noble and their competence in handling such tools.

[h1]Items of Interest[/h1]
The workshop is a treasure trove of fascinating and potentially useful items. The following can be found in The Watchmaker:

[h3][b]Chronosmith's Tools[/b][/h3] A complete set of finely made artificer's tools once owned by @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17) himself. They are perfectly balanced and incredibly durable.

[h3][b]Constantine's Blueprints[/b][/h3] Detailed blueprints of several of @[Constantine Chronosmith](person:4397abe5-31b6-4eab-b75f-ac79fd772e17)'s designs, including the Clockwork Tower and its Assembly Room.

[h3][b]“Song of the Gears” Music Box[/b][/h3] A small, ornately decorated music box that, when wound, plays the "Song of the Gears".

[h3][b]Assorted Clockwork Parts[/b][/h3] There are bins of gears, cogs, springs, and other parts. These could be useful for anyone who dabbles in mechanical constructs or repairs.

[h3][b]Arcane Components[/b][/h3] Olivia, like her ancestor, blends magic with mechanics. She keeps a variety of arcane components on hand.

[h3][b]Mysterious Clockwork Device[/b][/h3] This device, while currently non-functional, is a puzzle box that Constantine himself designed. Who knows what secrets it might hold when fixed and unlocked?

[h3][b]Timepiece Collection[/b][/h3] Olivia maintains a collection of fascinating and exotic timepieces from different eras and regions. A knowledgeable or observant PC might recognize a piece that is valuable or historically significant.

[h1]Lair Actions[/h1]
On initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties), Olivia can take a lair action to cause one of the following effects: 

[ol]
[li][b]Grapple from Above:[/b] Olivia activates a defensive mechanism she installed in her workshop. Mechanical arms descend from the ceiling and attempt to grapple a creature she can see. The target must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u] or be grappled ([u]escape DC 15[/u]). The arms return to the ceiling and the grapple ends if the target escapes or at the end of Olivia's next turn.

[li][b]Clockwork Rotation:[/b] A set of hidden gears in the floor rotate, rearranging the layout of the workshop. Creatures standing on a moving floor tile must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/u] or be moved 10 feet in a random direction.

[li][b]Stun Blast:[/b] Olivia activates a device that emits a dazzling array of light and sound. Each creature within 30 feet of the device must succeed on a [u]DC 15 Wisdom saving throw[/u] or be stunned until the end of its next turn as its senses are overloaded.

[li][b]Steam Shroud:[/b] Olivia throws a lever, causing a cloud of thick, obscuring steam to fill a 20-foot-radius sphere centered on a point she can see within 60 feet. The steam lasts until initiative count 20 on the next round and heavily obscures the area.
[/ol]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
Spring-Loaded Sprite

[h1]NPC[/h1]
@[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23)

articles.title: CT: Timepiece of Eternity
articles.content:
The heart of the Clockwork Tower, this room houses a hollow pedestal where Earthheart Emeralds should reside. The Eternity Warden stands sentinel in this room, its mechanical eyes always watching for threats.

Finding replacement crystals would require a trip to the @[Luminous Caverns](plot:f8920a29-7a22-4ce1-93c4-636d4ba89f99), where the Earthheart Emeralds can be harvested from the rock.

[aloud]
As you enter this grand chamber, your eyes are immediately drawn to a magnificent pedestal in its center, designed to house the illustrious Earthheart Emeralds. The room hums with a kind of temporal energy that makes your senses tingle and the very air seem thicker. 

Towering above the pedestal is the awe-inspiring figure of a distinctive construct guardian, its mechanical form gleaming with polished brass, steel, and shimmering crystal. It grasps a sizable staff that pulsates with the same scintillating energy as the guardian wielding it. 

The room itself seems to be suspended in time, with every gear, clock hand, and pendulum moving in a surreal and mesmerizing dance. As the large construct turns its attention to you, the weight of the room's importance becomes abundantly clear.
[/aloud]

[h1]Additional Information[/h1]
Deep within the Clockwork Tower lies its very heart and lifeblood: the Earthheart Emeralds. Mined from the ethereal depths of the @[Luminous Caverns](plot:f8920a29-7a22-4ce1-93c4-636d4ba89f99), these radiant gems are not just stones of beauty but the vital source powering the entire Tower. Their legends are manifold, their significance immeasurable. The Clockwork Tower does not have enough emeralds and require more. The DM can choose the amount of Earthheart Emeralds that must be mined:
[ul]
[li]Novice adventurers are charged with gathering a mere 5 lbs
[li]Medium level PCs should mine 15 to 20 lbs
[li]Experienced PCs will need to mine 100 lbs
[/ul]

Watching over this irreplaceable treasure is the Eternity Warden, a sentinel dedicated to the Timepiece's protection. While its primary essence is rooted in defense, it possesses the wisdom to negotiate if adventurers' motives align with preserving the Timepiece.

[b]Tick and Tock:[/b]
[aloud][ul][li][b]Tick:[/b]  "Chirp-chirp! The p-p-pedestal's hollow call, seeks a crystal, to rise, not fall!"
[li][b]Tock:[/b]  "Tock-tock! @[Luminous Caverns](plot:f8920a29-7a22-4ce1-93c4-636d4ba89f99) gleam, but Pendulum Horrors lurk and scheme!"[/ul][/aloud]

[h1]Refueling the Clockwork Tower with Earthheart Emeralds[/h1]
[h2]Locating the Pedestal[/h2]
As the adventurers enter the chamber, they'll notice an intricate and ornate pedestal, shimmering faintly with temporal energy, positioned centrally.

Closer examination of the pedestal will show engraved patterns depicting clockwork gears and time-themed motifs.

[h2]Preparing the Earthheart Emeralds[/h2]
Each Earthheart Emerald needs to be inspected for impurities. Imperfections could disrupt the energy flow.

Using a simple jeweler's loupe (or a mage's discerning eye), scrutinize each emerald. Remove any impurities using a fine chisel or by channeling a minor purification spell.

The emeralds should now pulse with a gentle inner light, ready to be harnessed.

[h2]Activating the Pedestal[/h2]
The pedestal bears a series of small, circular indentations on its top surface. These are sockets designed specifically for Earthheart Emeralds.

Begin by touching an emerald to a socket. If the emerald is correctly prepared, it will levitate slightly and fit snugly into the socket on its own.

As each emerald is placed, the engravings on the pedestal will start to glow brighter, and the room will fill with the harmonious hum of stabilizing energy.

[h2]Channeling the Power[/h2]
Once all the required Earthheart Emeralds are in place, adventurers need to initiate the energy transfer. A successful [u]Intelligence (Arcana) check DC 14[/u] would help to determine the best way to channel power (described below).

To do this, the party must gather around the pedestal. Each member should place a hand on the pedestal's surface and concentrate, envisioning the energy from the emeralds flowing into the tower.

Arcane users in the party might feel a strong pull as they guide the raw energy into the tower's core, recharging its mechanisms.

[h2]Signs of Successful Refueling[/h2]
The Clockwork Tower's internal mechanisms will begin operating more smoothly. The erratic behaviors, the flickering of rooms, and the power fluctuations will start to diminish.

The humming sound will stabilize, transforming into a rhythmic heartbeat, synchronized with the tower's now seamless operations.

[h2]Final Steps[/h2]
With the power successfully channeled, the emeralds will have dulled slightly, their energy now fueling the tower. The party should leave the emeralds in their sockets; removing them could disrupt the stabilization process.

As a sign of gratitude, the Eternity Warden will present a Temporal Quill (see Magic Items)—a gift from the tower and a testament to the adventurers' accomplishment.

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1409607]|Eternity Warden[/spoiler]

articles.title: DNC: Altar
articles.content:
The @[Dark Mother Superior](person:01fdf692-3417-4fb0-acad-577bdb06bf5d) is standing at the Alar, chanting her litany to the dark god. 

[aloud]There is an altar with an older woman with short, straight blonde hair standing at it. She is quite tall with leathery, reddish skin. She has her back to the church and is facing the Altar. In one hand, she holds an unusual dagger, and in the other, she is clutching a book. The Altar is ablaze in ethereal light. The woman is chanting, calling on the dead to come back to life. "Hear me, all mighty dead, and join us in our dance!"[/aloud]

Behind the Altar you will see the secret passage if you have answered the ghost's riddle. If you have not spoken to the ghost, you can roll a [u]DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) to reveal the secret passage[/u]. To get to the secret passageway without disturbing the @[Dark Mother Superior](person:01fdf692-3417-4fb0-acad-577bdb06bf5d), the adventurers will need [u]DC 12 Dexterity (Stealth).[/u]

The adventurers can try to speak with The Dark Mother. A [u]DC 15 Charisma will be needed for her to talk to you[/u]. If you are successful, and she does speak to you, she will try and convince you to join her Dark Sisterhood and become Dark Monks. If you choose to become Dark Monks, then your life is forfeit. 

You can also roll a [u]DC 15 Intelligence (Religion) to explain in religious terms to the Dark Mother that she has lost her way[/u]. Your words soothe the Dark Mother's tortured soul. She converts her beliefs to a neutral deity and retreats for prayer and contemplation. If you fail, she will attack. 

[h1]Monster[/h1]
The @[Dark Mother Superior](person:01fdf692-3417-4fb0-acad-577bdb06bf5d)

[h1]Magic Items[/h1]
[ul]
[li][blocklink:1428494]
[li][blocklink:1428491] (hung around the neck of the Dark Mother Superior)
[/ul]

[h1]Treasure[/h1]
[ul]
[li]1 of 3 Books of the [blocklink:1428490] (the other two books are in the Transept)
[li]1 Golden Chalice worth 50 crowns
[li]2 upside down silver crosses, both worth 20 marks each
[/ul]

articles.title: DNC: Bedroom
articles.content: It's a bedroom. Pretty much all it is. You can choose to rest here. No one will come in for [roll:1d8] hours. If you stay longer than that time then a random encounter will happen.

articles.title: DNC: Construct Room
articles.content:
The Construct Room is where the Dark Nuns cast their reanimation spells to bring back the dead. There is a private altar in the room with a partially mummified body. Gore-encrusted chains on the walls contain the remains of dead animals that are also used in the reanimation process. Two sets of stocks at the back of the room hold Lena and Tatum.

[aloud]This appears to be a room directly from hell. A small altar has a mummified body wrapped on it. Chains, where people have been kept, line the walls. There is a large work table down the middle of the room - on the table are a verious knives, a potted plant, various other surgical tools and clamps. Three stained glass windows allow some light in. On the floor by the northeast door is a small chest.  At the south end of the room there are stocks, imprisoning a human male and a tiefling woman.[/aloud]

The mummy will [u]animate if the players touch it or the potted plant[/u].

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1429522]|Mummified Body[/spoiler]

[h1]Treasure[/h1]
The chest in the corner of the room contains 5cp. The last money from an unfortunate victim.

articles.title: DNC: Entrance
articles.content:
The outside of the church is dark and forbidding. The church is located five miles from the nearest village. It sits on the top of a small hill in the middle of a thick wood. The fog is extensive. You can see 500 ft during the daytime, but this drops to 50ft at night.

[aloud]Out of the clinging fog emerges a church and you see twin towers capped with grotesque statues. The large, heavy doors are closed. On one side of the church is a small graveyard. On the other side is a small building. As you walk closer to the church, you hear the sound of rhythmic chanting. Someone is home.[/aloud]

Before entering the church, the adventurers will see that two huge ironwood doors are closed. High above them on the roof are winged stone statues. The adventurers need to roll a [u]D15 dexterity to check if they can open the doors without waking a statue[/u]. If the adventurers fail, then a status will jump down and attack them.

[aloud]Closed, twelve-foot ironwood doors prevent you from entering the Dark Church. Looking up, you see winged statues at the top of each tower. The fog somehow feels colder.[/aloud]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
A single [spoiler][block:1422319]|Church Gargoyle[/spoiler]

articles.title: DNC: Facade
articles.content:
The Facade is a small room with no lights. Any member of the team with dark vision will be able to see that there is a D6 number of Blind Rats hiding in the shadows. Each rat has had its eyes burned from its skull. They are feral and will attack anyone coming into the Facade. There are three barrels 
filled with sweet wine in the Facade.

[aloud]A foul urine smell hangs in the air as you enter the Facade. There are no lights. You hear tiny squeaks.[/aloud]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
Five [roll:1d6+2] [spoiler][block:1422317]|Blind Rats[/spoiler]

[h1]Treasure[/h1]
[h2]Barrels filled with wine[/h2]
The wine in the barrels is delicious. The Dark Nuns make it themselves and use it as an offering to their dark gods. One glass of wine is the equivalent of [u]1 hour of rest[/u]. 

The wine [u]is addictive[/u]. Each person must make a [u]Wisdom Saving Throw or become drunk[/u], causing them to sing loudly and dance in the darkness. Use the Random Encounter table to determine who hears the singer and comes to investigate.

articles.title: DNC: Narthex
articles.content:
The narthex is a small room between the church's front doors and the doors that lead into the Nave. To the right, a passageway leads to the Tower. To the left, a passage leads to the Facade. A trap is set into the floor directly in front of the doors leading into the Nave.

[aloud]You step into the Dark Church and enter the Church's Narthex. In front of you, two more doors lead into the Church's Nave. Passageways lead to your left and right. As you stand inside of the Dark Church, the doors behind you close. There is no light. Chanting from the Dark Nuns can be heard.[/aloud]

[h1]Trap[/h1]
[h2]Under Pressure[/h2]
[b]Trigger:[/b] This trap is triggered when an intruder steps on a hidden pressure plate directly in front of the door that leads into the Nave.

[b]Effect:[/b] Targets all players within ten ft., DC 10 WIS save or become confused for 1 (1d4) rounds Trigger: pressure plate activates when 20 or more pounds are placed on it.

[b]Countermeasures:[/b] A successful DC 10 Wisdom (Perception) check will spot the pressure plate and wedging an iron spike or other object under the pressure plate prevents the trap from activating.

articles.title: DNC: Nave
articles.content:
The main entrance to the church is the Nave. A roaring fire lights the room, and Dark Nuns chant in the shadows cast by the light. Beyond the Nave can be seen rows of pews and at the front of the church is an altar where a powerful woman stands with her arms raised into the air. The Nave is a large area 20’ wide and 30’ long.

[aloud]On either side of the nave is a pair of nuns with heads bowed and hands clasped before their chests. They are chanting intently but you can't make out the words. You look further and can see pews lined up before an Altar. With her back to you, you see the an old woman standing at the altar with her arms lifted into the air. [/aloud]

[h1]Enchantment[/h1]
The song the Dark Nuns are singing is an enchantment. Anyone who is in the Nave will be too close to the sound of the nuns Chanting and will fall under its spell. [u]A DC 12 INT will be needed to shake off the effect of the chanting[/u]. If a player loses, they will immediately prostrate themselves on the floor and being chanting with the Dark Nuns. The Dark Nuns will see the new supplicant and attack. You will lose initiative and the Dark Nuns gain an extra attack round. 

Since the chanting nuns also have their eyes closed, intent upon their chant, rolling a [u]DC 15 Dexterity check will allow the adventures to sneak past[/u] them. A failed check will ensure the Dark Nuns see the adventurers, in which case all Dark Nuns attack.

[h1]Monster[/h1]
Four [roll:1d4+2] [spoiler][block:1429452]|Dark Nuns[/spoiler]

articles.content:
The Dark Nun's Church introduces several new NPCs, Monsters, and Items unique to the plot.

articles.title: DNC: Office
articles.content:
The office has a warm fire, a comfortable sofa, and a desk with chairs around it. Sitting at the desk is a Dark Elf. Her name is Sister Alotel Galamin, and she is the scribe for the church. She talks a lot but loves to gamble. You can choose to play the game Dead to Rites against Alotel Galamin. If you win, Alotel will show you the secret door that takes you to the Construct Room.

[aloud]The office is a stark contrast to the rest of thechurch. It is warm and comfortable, and a smiling woman sits at a table with accounting books open before her. She welcomes you in and introduces herself as Sister Alotel Galamin. She asks you “Would you like to win some money?” She shakes dice she holds in her hands and her smile broadens. [/aloud]

[h1]Game of chance: Dead to Rites[/h1]
[b]Requirements[/b] 
[ul]
[li]Each player must pay 1 silver mark to play. Sister Alotel will match the number of marks added. 
[li]Five d6
[li]Paper to record players' scores
[/ul]

[b]Rules[/b]
[ul]
[li]A player rolls the five dice, and if the throw does not include a 2 or 5, they receive the score of the total numbers added together. That player is also able to roll the dice again. 
[li]When a player rolls the dice that contain a 2 or 5, they score no points, and the dice that include a 2, or 5 are excluded from any future throws they make. 
[li]A player's turn does not stop until their last remaining die shows a 2 or 5. At that point, the player is "dead to rites," and it becomes the next player's turn. 
[li]The highest total score wins.
[/ul]

The winner can choose to take all the silver or receive some information from Sister Alotel. The information is that she knows where a secret passage to the Construct Room can be found (right behind her desk) and that the people you are looking for are located there.

articles.title: DNC: Portal
articles.content:
The left Transept reveals a cauldron boiling with a foul smelling liquid. Bursting from the cauldron appears a ghost of a Dark Nun. The ghost will hurl insults at the adventurers and challenge them to a riddle. If the adventurers successfully answer the riddle, the ghost will tell them that there is a secret entrance behind the Altar to the Construct Room where Lena and Tatum are being kept.

[container:tablebox-90]
[center][b]Riddles[/b][/center]
[table]
[tr][th][roll:1d6][/th][th]Riddle[/th][th:12]Answer[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]1[/td][td]I am not alive, but I can grow. I have no lungs, but I need air to survive. What am I?[/td]    [td]Fire [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]2 [/td][td]When set loose, I fly away, never so cursed as when I go astray.[/td]    [td]Fart[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]3 [/td][td]Soft and fragile is my skin. I get my growth in mud. I'm dangerous as much as pretty, for if not careful, I draw blood.[/td]    [td]Thorn [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]4 [/td][td]Lighter than what I am made of, more of me is hidden than is seen.[/td]    [td]Iceberg[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]5 [/td][td]I go around in circles, but always straight ahead, never complain, no matter where I am led.[/td]    [td]Wheel[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]6 [/td][td]Alive without breath, as cold as death, clad in mail, never clinking, never thirsty, ever drinking.[/td]    [td]Fish[/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]


[container:tablebox-90]
[center][b]Insults[/b][/center]
[table]
[tr]    [th][roll:1d10][/th]    [th]Insult  [/th][/tr]
[tr]    [td]1 [/td]    [td]You sagging piss-stained idiot  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]2 [/td]    [td]You're not a complete idiot. Some parts are clearly missing.  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]3 [/td]    [td]I thought breath weapons were supposed to come out of your mouth!  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]4 [/td]    [td]If ignorance is bliss, then why aren't you happier?  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]5 [/td]    [td]Putting teeth in your mouth ruined a perfectly good asshole.  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]6 [/td]    [td]You dim-witted chromosome collecting whelp.  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]7 [/td]    [td]Really, I'm doing you a favor by smashing up that dim-witted pile of rotten meat you call a face.  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]8 [/td]    [td]Should I be using smaller words so you can understand?  [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]9 [/td]    [td]Were you hit by an acid arrow, or have you always looked like that? 1 [/td][/tr]
[tr]    [td]10 [/td]    [td]You're like Rapunzel, but everyone [/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]

[h1]Monster[/h1]
A single [spoiler][block:1422372]|Dark Nun Ghost[/spoiler]

articles.title: DNC: Random Encounter Table
articles.content:
There is room around the church for the adventures to wander. The following “Random Encounter” table will help keep things
lively.

[container:tablebox-90]
[table]
[tr]
    [th][center][roll:1d10][/center] [/th]
    [th]Random Encounter  [/th]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]1[/center][/td]
    [td]A Dark Nun attacks with both blades.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]2[/center][/td]
    [td]The party is attacked by [roll:1d6] [blocklink:1422317].  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]3[/center][/td]
    [td]@[Sister Ervina Banksi](person:2da47ac3-18ec-41b9-b072-b8fbfbd20609) finds them and asks for some food. In exchange, she will tell them one piece of information about the church. See @[Sister Ervina Banksi](person:2da47ac3-18ec-41b9-b072-b8fbfbd20609) for details on her character.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]4[/center][/td]
    [td]@[Kethend](person:da744c19-0b96-4687-b6dc-8c4d57796b66) stumbles onto the adventurers and asks to join them in fighting the nuns who kidnapped and killed her husband. See @[Kethend](person:da744c19-0b96-4687-b6dc-8c4d57796b66) for details on her character.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]5[/center][/td]
    [td]A reanimated hand drags itself to the adventurers.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]6[/center][/td]
    [td]@[Grilia](person:b31fd2b3-99f1-4e9a-9c86-4115ad9b5c45) finds them and asks them to join their group. See details on @[Grilia](person:b31fd2b3-99f1-4e9a-9c86-4115ad9b5c45)’s personality in the Non-Player Character section.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]7[/center][/td]
    [td]A potion is lying on the floor. The bottle has thick, viscous liquid and smells acrid. Strangely, if anyone is brave enough to drink the potion, they will receive total health and restoration. All HP returns to the same number as at the start of the game.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]8[/center][/td]
    [td]An old gardener is weeding the graveyard. He can be asked questions or bribed for 5CP. He will tell the adventures that the Tower has a trap in the base. “Watch out for the trip wire” he cackles.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]9[/center][/td]
    [td]Chanting can be heard from the church. After a few minutes, it is followed by a woman’s scream. Are the adventures too late to rescue Lena? Short answer: No, but Lena will have a nasty cut on her side that will slow her down when she is saved.  [/td]
[/tr]
[tr]
    [td][center]10[/center][/td]
    [td]A white dove lands on the ground. It is a good omen. A feather falls from the dove. It is a Holy Feather, and it can be used against the @[Dark Mother Superior](person:01fdf692-3417-4fb0-acad-577bdb06bf5d). The Holy Feather can be used against any unholy or undead monster. Any unholy or undead monster will be repelled by the person holding the feather, allowing the holder to gain an additional attack each round. The feather will only last for three rounds. [/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[/container]

articles.title: DNC: Resolution
articles.content:
The game's primary goal is to find and free Lena and Tatum. They are both found in the Construct Room inside of the church. Your team will need to get in and out of the Construct Room with Lena and Tatum. When they return to the village, they will receive their reward from Grilia. Grilia, even though he is a tiefling, is a man of his word and will pay the full 100GP. In addition, Grilia will give your team free night and board whenever they are in the village. 

[h1]Alternate Resolutions[/h1]
Well done, you have rescued Lena and Tatum. You may have collected some treasure, a magic item, and good stories to impress your friends.

But that doesn’t mean the game is over. After all, this would not be a Penny Blood Adventure if it was that easy. Here are additional ways (one of them twisted) your adventurers can end the game.

[h2]Bring hope to the Village[/h2]
It is good that you have freed Lena and Tatum, but the village still lives in fear that the Dark Nuns will continue to kidnap people. To prevent this, your team must find and remove the “Books of the New Living” from the church. The books can be destroyed or kept.

[h2]Raise the Dead[/h2]
Your team finds and reads the “Books of the New Living.” If you have someone on your team who can cast spells, they will learn the magic art of raising people from the dead.

With your new knowledge, you take control of the church and rule over the local village. The villagers must deliver a tax of 50 golden crowns each month, or you will send your Dark Nuns to kidnap a person, at random, and add them to your army of the dead.

articles.title: DNC: Secret Passage
articles.content:
Someone listening at either door will will the sound of an armoured figure pacing a short distance towards/away from the door.

The darkness in the secret passage hides the Dark Knight guarding the entrance to the Construct Room. The Unholy Knight has been waiting for a fight. 

[h1]Monster[/h1]
[spoiler][block:1429446]|Dark Knight[/spoiler]

articles.title: DNC: The Graveyard
articles.content:
To the side of the church is an unholy graveyard.

[aloud]A foul, funky smell comes from the graveyard. Fog lies on the ground and you see a scattering of headstones and crypts. Some of the graves are still fresh. The dead feel very close.[/aloud]

There are graves the adventurers can visit. If they go to a burial, you will need to roll a 1d10 to determine what the encounter will be.

[container:tablebox-90]
[table]
[tr][th][roll:1d10][/th][th]Encounter[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]1[/td][td]A Reanimated Hand scuttles out of the grave and attacks.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]2[/td][td]Five coppet bits have been left to pay safe passageway to the dead. The coins are cursed! A character can
      take the money but will permanently lose 1HP per coin they take. They cannot give the coin back,
      lose it or get rid of it in any way other than having it stolen from them unknowingly at which point
      their HP maximum will be restored. At the DM’s discretion, increase the HP loss for higher level
      characters.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]3[/td][td]A Skeleton Nun steps out of a new crypt and surprises the adventurers. The Skeleton Nun receives
      one extra attack before the group can respond. [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]4[/td][td]A weathered map is pinned to a grave marker. It shows the church and has two rooms labeled. The
      DM can decide which two rooms they want to reveal to the group.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]5[/td][td]There is a flash of lightning followed by a distant rumble of thunder. Heavy rain is coming. [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]6[/td][td]There is a small potion bottle. It is corked closed. A label on the side says “sleep.” Anyone who drinks
      the potion will fall immediately into a deep sleep for 1 hour or until they take damage.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]7[/td][td]A small, pink box with 6 shortbreads are lying on a fresh grave. Eating a shortbread will restore 2 HP
      to a maximum of 4 per character. [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]8[/td][td]You see a grave digger. He has just finished filling in a new grave. You can bribe (1SP) or threaten
      him (he is a coward and will back down quickly). He does not know much about what happens in the
      church. He can tell you that there is a trap on the floor when you enter the Narthex. [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]9[/td][td]A Blind Rat leaps out of a fresh grave, its mouth oozing with congealed blood. [/td][/tr]
[tr][td]10[/td][td]A single red flower rests next to the headstone. This is one sign that love can find a way in this world
      of terror.[/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]
