articles.title: DNC: Tower
articles.content:
The tower has no stairs. It is the place where the Gargoyles will rest. Entering the tower can trigger a trap.

[aloud]You enter the Tower. Oddly, there are no stairs or means of going up the Tower. A single candle burns on a small shelf on the opposite wall. You see a silver cross on a chain lying on the shelf.[/aloud]

[h1]Trap[/h1]
[h2]Splat Trap, a falling block[/h2]
[b]Trigger:[/b] A taught length of string stretches across the diameter of the tower base. The trap is activated if the string is moved. Once activated, a large block will fall down the Tower and crush anything under it.

[b]Effect:[/b]  Targets all creatures within a 10 ft. square area, DC 15 DEX save or take 8 [roll:2d10] damage.

[b]Countermeasures:[/b] DC 15 to find, DC 10 to disable

[h1]Treasure[/h1]
[h2]A Silver Cross[/h2]  
A silver cross lies on the shelf. The cross can be used to confuse the Dark Nuns. It is not magical, but the Dark Nuns are [u]repulsed by the religious meaning of the cross[/u]. It can be used only once in an attack. The Dark Nun will be confused when she sees the cross and skip an attack round.

articles.title: DNC: Transept
articles.content:
The right Transept contains two of the magic books used to reanimate bodies. These books should be destroyed or taken away from the church's sisters. Be careful when handling the books. There are two books, and the person picking up the books will need to roll a 1D4. If the number rolled is 4, the trap, a Close Shave, will be activated. The trap can only be activated once.

[h1]Magic Item[/h1]
[h2]Books of the New Living[/h2]
Magic books that hold the secrets to reanimating the dead. Three books are needed to ensure that the spells are cast correctly. Two are in the Transept, and the third is with the Dark Mother Superior at the Altar. 

[h1]Trap[/h1]
[h2]Close Shave[/h2]
[b]Trigger:[/b] When someone picks up either one of the “Books of the New Living” without leather gloves.

[b]Effect:[/b] +11 to hit [roll:1d20+11] against all targets within a 5 ft. arc, 9 [roll:4d10] slashing damage

[b]Countermeasures:[/b] A successful [u]DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check[/u] will know that the books are possessed. Wearing leather gloves will protect the player from activating the trap when handling the books.

articles.title: DoE: Level 1
articles.content:
[h1]Notes[/h1]
[h2]Entrances & Egresses[/h2]
[ul] 
[li][b][url:#6]Area 6[/url]:[/b] stairs up to the surface.
[li][b][url:#16]Area 16[/url]:[/b] stairs down to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#24]Level 2, Area 24[/url].
[li][b][url:#23]Area 23[/url]:[/b] stairs down to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#42]Level 2, Area 42[/url]. 
[/ul]

[h2]Wandering Monsters[/h2]
Roll every turn, 1 in 6 chance. Roll [roll:1d3] on the wandering monster chart at right. 

[h1]Level 1 Key[/h1]
[h2|6]6. Entrance[/h2]
The entrance area is a mess after centuries open to the elements. There is a lot of debris, both naturally occurring and from the ruins of the manor, scattered about the floor of this high-ceilinged chamber. Barely visible faded murals on the west wall depict a team of knights bearing the Erde family crest on their chargers looking down over a battlefield between human and elven troops. 

The door to [url:#8]Area 8[/url] is built into the hill the chargers are standing on, made to look like the entrance into a cavern. The secret door into [url:#7]Area 7[/url] is disguised into an illustration of a pile of elven bodies. It is opened by pushing in the rolled-back eyes of one of the dead elves, hooking the fingers into the recesses, and pulling the door outward into this room.

[h2|7]7. The Trophies[/h2]
This secret chamber is where the trophies from the war between the races of man and the ancient elven race of the Kale were kept by the Erde family. Four suits of fine elven plate mail hammered from the stuff of other worlds are here - each appearing to be made of heavy steel with a purple burnish to it. 

[container:indent-20]One of the four suits is a suit of+1 plate mail that bears a dark curse - every month the wearer of the armour must slay a defenseless sentient humanoid or suffer a cummulative - 2 penalty to his Wisdom as the armour slowly drives him insane. [/container]

Replicas of several elven weapons are here also, but none are of actual battlefield quality.

[h2|8]8. Antechamber[/h2]
This is the central gathering point for [b]Vera's[/b] clan of ogres within the complex. There is only [mark]one ogre here now[/mark], but the ogres in other rooms in this level often come by here for food or to rest. The bodies of a half-dozen goats are hung on wooden pegs on the east and north walls. 

There is always a small fire here, and typically a large pot of tea-like substance on the fire. The two passages exiting from this room have massive and ornate archways over them with the Erdea coat of arms at the peak and both bases of the arch.

The [mark]secret door[/mark] to [url:#7]Area 7[/url] is opened by removing one of the wooden pegs from the wall and reaching in to press a switch along the bottom of the peg hole. 

[h2|9]9. Wolf Pen[/h2]
[b]Vera's[/b] pet wolves are being kept in this room. [mark]7 wolves[/mark] (the eighth is with [b]Vera[/b]), are chained to the wall, but the stakes holding the chains are purposefully weakly mounted so the wolves can break free if very excited or in combat. 

The corridor to the south slopes downward at a 20 degree angle.

[h2|10]10. Storage[/h2]
This room was used for storage ages ago. A few shattered barrels are all that remain of the stores kept here for sieges and disasters.

[h2|11]11. Emergency Well[/h2]
This well was used in times of crisis as an emergency water supply for the manor residents. The ogres tried using it, but realized that the shine of water isn't actually water, but a [mark]massive Gray Ooze[/mark], that lives in the well. They threw it a few dwarves and it generally stays in the hole because it isn't hungry.

[h2|12]12. Ogre Storage[/h2]
Once a small barracks and storage room, this has been converted into the main storage area for the ogres. [mark]3 ogres, are here[/mark] along with the clan loot of 10,000cp, 1,000gp, and 4 x 250gp gems. There are also [ul][li]a barrel of dwarf-parts, [li]a few barrels of fresh water, [li]a keg of beer, [li]two dozen sheep skins and [li]a pile of lumber.[/ul]

[h2|13]13. There Is No Area13[/h2]
Really. This time there isn't one.

[h2|14]14. Trapped Entry[/h2]
The obvious descent from the entry area, this room was trapped by some previous tenants and the ogres have avoided it. [mark]A pressure plate[/mark] covers a ten-foot length of the room extending from one alcove to the other. 

[container:indent-20]Anyone weighing over 30 pounds stepping on this 40 x 10 Area will likely trigger the trap (1-4 on 1d6). When triggered, a cold sleeping mist blasts into the room from both walls, accompanied by a loud whistling sound.  Living beings within the room must save versus poison or be knocked unconscious for 1d4+1 turns. The whistling sound will also attract the attention of anyone alive in areas 8, 17, and 19. [/container]

[h2|15]15. Secure Entry[/h2]
In an effort to control the flow of ogres into the dangerous lower areas of the manor, [b]Vera[/b] and [b]Jubal[/b] have tried securing this room, at least making it very noisy for anyone to proceed. 

A collection of furniture has been piled up against the western door which opens into this room. Anyone pushing through from the west side will need to push the pile of furniture out of the way using the door, while those wanting to open it from inside the room will have to move the furniture first. 

In the middle of the room there is a small pile of burned debris from a fairly recent camp fire. The small room to the south was being used by the dwarves to store supplies and has been thoroughly ransacked, with wrecked barrels and crates littering the floor and only a couple of pickaxes left.

[h2|16]16. Descent[/h2]
A small alcove is at the top of a set of very well made stone steps twisting down into the depths. [mark][b]Jubal's[/b] second quasit (known as [b]CueTwo[/b]), is stationed here (invisible)[/mark], waiting for [b]Vera[/b] to return whereupon he will alert [b]Jubal[/b] and assist in killing her. 

However, seeing adventurers descending these stairs, he will follow them instead of maintaining his vigil. He will follow them until they encounter [b]Vera[/b] and will then attempt to assist them in fighting her. He will then return to his invisible state and wait for opportunities to make the adventurer's lives miserable - setting off traps, interrupting sleep (and spellcasting), and attracting the attention of monsters.

The stairs descend to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#24]]Level 2, Area 24[/url].

[h2|17]17. Jubal's Explorations[/h2]
These rooms served as a shrine in the original manor and are currently [mark]home to [b]Jubal[/b][/mark], a particularly nasty piece of work for an ogre. A year ago he split his dark and twisted soul into three pieces - one that he keeps himself, and two that have split off into smaller little “essences of [b]Jubal[/b]” that he calles Cue- One and [b]CueTwo[/b] (and that are, for all intents and purposes, Quasits). 

The room is divided into three segments - an eastern antechamber lined with benches built into the walls, an empty main room (with stairs up to the smaller private shrine and faded murals of human holy days) and the western “alcove” which contains a carved stone pulpit that currently has an open book on it and a massive and ornate iron lantern. 

[mark][b]CueOne[/b] is in this room[/mark], standing (invisibly) on the pulpit trying to make sense of the book. If the characters are stealthy enough he will flip the page and give away his presence (or at least make it look like the book is reading itself). He'll scream to alert [b]Jubal[/b] before trying to mess with the adventurers. Unlike [b]CueTwo[/b], [b]CueOne[/b] is very Ogrish in his tactics, attacking directly and going for the throat. 

The book is the [i]Tome of Cruel Lies[/i], which details many curses and also empowers any spellcaster that has studied it for a month, making their Bestow Curse spell more potent. Resisting the curses bestowed by someone who has mastered this material is more difficult (-2 on saves) and if using the random curse tables, the caster rolls twice and picks the best result.

[h2|18]18. Private Shrine[/h2]
[b]Jubal[/b] has converted this private shrine of the Erdea family into his headquarters. The room is lit by a dozen candles and is decorated with the intestines of several slain dwarves. 

[b]Jubal[/b], is a dark and twisted ogre with some spellcasting ability (although no ogre-mage by any stretch of the imagination). [ul][li]He can cast [i]Bane[/i] (the opposite of Bless), [i]Protection from Good[/i], and [i]Fear[/i], each once per day as a level 4 cleric. [li]His massive staff with dangling skulls and feathers makes the targets of his fear spell save at a -2 penalty. [li]The magical charms on the staff can be transferred to a smaller staff or similar weapon, allowing the magical benefit to be passed on to smaller spellcasters. [li][b]Jubal[/b] also has a trunk under his makeshift bed containing 1,000gp and a wand of magic detection with 14 charges.[/ul]

[h2|19]19. Ogre Quarters[/h2]
Orthur, [b]Jubal[/b] and [b]Vera's[/b] lieutenant, is based out of these rooms along with whatever ogres he has in his company at the time. Currently the main room has one ogre, in it attaching new spiky things to his club, while Orthur is in the back room wishing that [b]Jubal[/b] and [b]Vera[/b] would get along. Both will react to any loud noise coming from Areas 14, 15 or 20.

[h2|20]20. The Dying Bull[/h2]
This area was once used as emergency living quarters for the Erdea family, but was more recently converted into a small tavern by the prior dwarven inhabitants. Now the place is a disaster, destroyed by ogres looking for free beer. 

A prolonged search of the area, digging through the ruined furnishings and casks will find the warhammer of the dwarven clan leader - a [b]+1 warhammer[/b] that deals an additional +1 damage when wielded by a dwarf with a nice beard and 13+ Charisma.

[h2|21]21. Ghoulish Cunning[/h2]
This secret chamber is home to [mark]6 cunning Ghouls[/mark]. The secret trap door to the surface is made of wood and remained undetected by the dwarves while they were in the area (and the ghouls would only attack lone dwarves out at night). The ghouls have decided to not bother the much more powerful ogres. 

The floor of the room is littered with gnawed dwarven bones and those of some local wildlife. In the mix of bones and detritus there are 500sp and suit of black magical chainmail +1 that automatically cancels any light or continual light spells cast at a target or by a spellcaster , within 30 feet of the mail. 

The secret door to [url:#22]Area 22[/url] is quite visible from this side, requiring no special efforts to detect nor open.

[h2|22]22. Burial Chamber[/h2]
This chamber has three raised alcoves, each containing a stone sarcophagus, all radiating from an octagonal chamber with a massive wrought iron candelabra decorating the centre of the room. Around the base of the candelabra are the skeletal remains of a half dozen human and demihuman bodies. 

The sarcophagi each contain an elder of the Erde family, now haunting these ruins as powerful [mark]Wights[/mark]. They will not rise unless a sarcophagus is disturbed, at which point all three will rise to attack. The wight in the central sarcophagus wields a [b]longsword +1, +3 vs elves[/b].

[h2|23]23. Falling Ceiling[/h2]
This room is empty of inhabitants and the floor is cluttered with bits of rock and dust fallen from the ceiling. The dwarven ability to detect dangerous stonework, or a thief detecting traps, will show that while the ceiling is indeed on the way towards caving in, the actual collapse won't happen for many, many years.

The stairs descend to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#42]Level 2, Area 42[/url].

articles.title: DoE: Level 2
articles.content:
[h1]Notes[/h1]
This level contains three distinct sub-levels. The main areas (24-37) are linked to level 1 above and level 2 below. Areas 38-40 are a series of secret chambers and passages that link to the main areas as well as having their own stairs access to level 3. This secret area was once the home of the family assassin, but is now overrun with giant weasels who have learned how to trigger the level's secret doors. All secret doors on this level are opened by applying pressure to a “loose” stone one foot above floor level (or in the case of the one in the floor, one foot from the outer edge of the door). Finally, the family crypt in areas 41-43 also have their own links to the levels above and below.

[h2]Entrances & Egresses[/h2]
[ul]
[li][b]Area 24:[/b] stairs up to level 1, area 16.
[li][b]Area 24:[/b] stairs down to level 3, area 44.
[li][b]Area 39:[/b] stairs down to level 3, area 53.
[li][b]Area 41[/b]: stairs down to level 3, area 74.
[li][b]Area 42:[/b] stairs up to level 1, area 23.
[/ul]

[h2]Wandering Monsters[/h2]
Roll every turn, 1 in 6 chance. Roll [roll:2d3] on the wandering monster chart below.

[h1]Level 2 Key[/h1]
[h2|24]24. Great Hall[/h2]
Containing access to both level 3 and level 1, the great hall is currently home to a very angry Troll, who is waiting for the return of Vera who defeated him earlier and who he assumes must return via this route. The hall exiting on the northwest end of the hall has a noticeably foul scent emanating from it.

[h2|25]25. Guard Room[/h2]
Now abandoned, this room contains the wreckage indicating that it once housed at least a half dozen people with beds, a table, and other furniture.

[h2|26]26. Mossy Chamber[/h2]
This chamber (and the one beyond it) is quite damp from water leakage from the nearby well shaft. The walls and floor of this chamber are covered in “leafy” fungal growths. None are harmful, and they show signs of being harvested by other creatures living in the area.

[h2|27]27. Screaming Fungi[/h2]
This chamber once contained stored food supplies in piled wooden crates. Now these crates are nearly completely overwhelmed with fungi. Amongst the fungi is a colony of 6 Shriekers.

[h2|28]28. Smashed Room[/h2]
The door to this room has been quite recently smashed open and blood spatters commence in the room proper and trail off towards area 24. The room reeks of offal and rotten meat - the remnants of meals eaten by the troll who waits for Vera's return in area 24. An open chest indicates that there was once treasure here too.

[h2|29]29. Troglodyte Turf[/h2]
This section of the level has been taken over by a clan of troglodytes who moved here from the underground river below. The burned stump of a troll's arm is nailed to the door as a warning. The room itself is empty and stripped of all furnishings.

[h2|30]30. Alcoves[/h2]
The hall here has several shallow alcoves along it. A single Troglodyte is on guard at each of the three alcoves as well as in front of the doorway to area 31. Their foul stench should give them away, but it is so overwhelming in this hall that their natural camouflage still gives them the advantage of surprise on a 1-4 on a d6. When starting combat, they will call out to their kin.

[h2|31]31. Den of Stink[/h2]
A dozen Troglodytes, live in this room, along with their food, a store of drinking water, and immeasurable stench. The leader of this clan wears a pair of platinum bracers worth 1,200gp each, and [u]two of his warriors are wearing a necklace and a ring respectively worth 800 and 600gp[/u]. The troglodytes are unaware of the secret door between this room and area 36. However, Vera has discovered it and will rush through it as soon as a fight with the troglodytes is over, attempting to catch the survivors weakened and confused (unless she has already been taken care of in area 36).

[h2|32]32. Family Quarters[/h2]
These three rooms were once the apartment of a part of the Erde family unwilling to live in the manor above due to rampant paranoia and a propensity towards dark magics. This room was used to house servants of those living in areas 33 and 34. The furniture is long destroyed, but a 1 foot diameter crystaline hemisphere juts out of the northeast corner of the room at waist height. [u]Rubbing the hemisphere creates an unseen servant that follows the orders of the person rubbing the hemisphere for 1 hour.[/u] It doesn't, however, indicate this to the person rubbing the object, so most never notice that they have a magical butler to help them. The hemisphere cannot be removed from the wall without destroying the enchantment. The door to area 34 is locked.

[h2|33]33. Dark Chamber[/h2]
The walls of this room are covered in a masonry completely unlike that throughout the rest of the excavations. The walls are a rough dark blue stone held together with nearly black jointing. 4 Shadows, still lurk here, but will not pursue out of the room. The door to area 34 is locked.

[h2|34]34. Stone Cold Mad[/h2]
This sealed chamber contains a massive bed, a table, a desk, a variety of chairs and bookshelves. “Sleeping” on the bed are a pair of Crystal Living Statues, as well as one sitting at the desk and a third leaning against a bookshelf seemingly reading a book. When the door is opened, the one at the bookshelf puts down the book and walks towards the party with it's hands held wide, while the other three look on. It attacks when within reach, and is joined the next round by its kin.

[h2|35]35. Carrion[/h2]
The door to this room has been smashed from the frame, and the remains of a pair of very recently slain carcass scavengers are slowly oozing their ichor across the floor. [b]Any loud noise in this room will alert Vera in area 36.[/b]

[h2|36]36. Vera[/h2]
Vera the ogress has just finished looting this room, having found and looted a secret storage box hidden in the north wall. Vera, also has the abilities of a level 5 thief. If she is alerted to activity in areas 35 or 31, she will attempt to move silently to attack her foes from behind, trying for a backstab on her first attack. Her favourite wolf, “Fang”,, is also with her. Vera wears a ring of protection +1 and a carries a sack containing 1,000gp and 4,000sp.

[h2|37]37. Red Alcove[/h2]
The masonry in this alcove is of a reddish rock, although of immediate importance to anyone entering the room is the Rust Monster, trying to remain unnoticed by the crazed ogress who just killed his neighbours.

[h2|38]38. Store Room[/h2]
This locked room was used for storage by the resident assassin. Undisturbed these long years, it still contains crates of rotted food, a barrel of old ale, and a collection of daggers, short swords and arrows. A wooden shelf on the far wall from the entrance holds a set of bottles. Two are potions of poison, one is a potion of Poseidon's Wine (grants the imbiber the benefits of water breathing for 1d6+6 turns), and the last is a potion of battle (the imbiber gains a +1 bonus on to hit rolls, damage rolls from melee and ranged weapon attacks, and a +1 bonus to armour class for 1d6+6 turns).

[h2|39]39. Alchemical Storage[/h2]
Once filled with beakers and bottles and mixing equipment, everything here is ruined and the place has been turned into a nest for 3 Giant Weasels. Amongst the debris that they have converted into their nests is a chewed on clerical scroll with the following spells still usable: Know Alignment, Continual Light, Locate Object.

[h2|40]40. The Assassin's Den[/h2]
These two rooms were once nicely furnished - now they are a mess of ruined bedding, old straw and ruined mulched paper. 4 Giant Weasels, nest in this mess. A thourough search of the bedding will find 97gp scattered between both rooms. Closer investigation reveals that an old brazier buried in the mess also contains a locked secret compartment. Within the compartment is a sealed metal vial (a potion of poison), a 700gp ring and a set of five small 100gp rubies.

[h2|41]41. Crypt Stairs[/h2]
Overlooking the stairwell cut into this room are four zombified bugbears wearing the standards of the Erde family and wielding polearms.

[h2|42]42. Shrine[/h2]
The northern portion of this space has an arched ceiling and magical stone shelves where candles were burned down to pools of wax across the surface. Any non-lawful character who lights a candle and places it on the shelf gains the benefits of a Bless spell for 1d6+1 turns.

[h2|43]43. Embalming Chamber[/h2]
The door to this room is locked. There is a massive stone table in the centre of the room, and jars of embalming fluid surrounding it.

[/table]
 * There are no wandering monsters in areas 41, 42 and 43.
[/container]

articles.title: DoE: Level 3
articles.content:
[h1]Notes[/h1]
Deep beneath the ruins of Erdea Manor lie the depths - old caves with links to the family crypts and to deeper secrets where the dead have been buried for ages untold. There are many routes in and out of the depths, but only one way to the catacombs below. 

This level remains under the control of the ancient Erde family, or at least the cursed remnants thereof. The Erde clan fought against the Kale and like many of the humans who lead the revolts, they were aided by powers from beyond this world. And like many of these tales, the result was more and less than they had hoped for. Now the surviving members of the clan reside in these depths, in turn hiding from the vengeful dead that sleep beneath them.

The clan is broken into two remaining bloodlines who occupy the western side of the dungeon map. They have been warped in strange ways into massive beastly creatures like minotaurs and rubbery semi-aquatic creatures reminiscent of throghrin.

[h2]Entrances & Egresses[/h2]
[ul]
[li]Cave tunnel in [url:#59]Area 59[/url] leads up to the surface
[li]Stairs in [url:#44]Area 44[/url] lead up to [url: https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#24]Area 24[/url]
[li]Stairs between [url:#46]Area 46[/url] and [url:#53]Area 53[/url] lead up to [url: https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#39]Level 2,  Area 39[/url]
[li]Stairs near [url:#74]Area 74[/url] lead up to [url: https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-2-plot#42]Level 2,  Area 42[/url]
[li]Stairs in [url:#61]Area 61[/url] lead down to [url: https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-4-plot#78]Level 4, Area 78[/url]
[/ul]

[h2]Wandering Monsters[/h2]
Roll every turn, 1 in 6 chance. Roll [roll:1d2] on the wandering monster chart at right.

[h1]Level 3 Key[/h1]
[h2|44]44. Below the Great Hall[/h2]
The stairs from above lead down into the middle of this room which is something of a neutral territory between the homes of the two remaining branches of the Erde clan. 

The floor bears the marks of ancient fights, and a blood trail (at least a few days old) leads out through the archway to the east and proceeds all the way to [url:#46]Area 46[/url].

[h2|45]45. Lord Derrin Erde's Glory[/h2] 
This old meeting room (complete with rotting table, chairs and bookshelves) was dedicated to the memory of a great warrior of the clan. 

The east wall of the room contains a massive portrait of Lord Derrin with his name on a brass plate below, now faded to mostly brown and black. Anyone raising a toast to Lord Derrin in this room gains +1 on all reaction rolls with others within the room for the duration. 

Behind the portrait is a [mark]secret door[/mark] into the lower level of the assassin's run which is opened by swinging the hinged portrait away from the wall and then pressing on the name plate embedded in the wall below it.

[h2|46]46. Toad Hall[/h2] 
[mark]Four giant toads[/mark] have moved in here, seeking refuge from the Erde clan. On the floor is the chewed remains of a minotaur, their most recent meal. They are hungry and happy to see more reasonably-sized food arriving.

[h2|47]47. Rubbish[/h2]
This side room has been filled with furniture that someone was rescuing from the flooded portion of the depths. Years have passed and less even-minded members of the family have looted through this until it is just a random jumble of wood, rotted cloth, and the occasional recognizable dresser drawer. 

Of course, digging through it all to prove it is all valueless will take several turns.

[h2|48]48. Guarded Curtains[/h2]
This room is separated from the corridor via a set of heavy magical curtains. While the [mark]minotaur[/mark] with a polearm, behind the curtain seems to be the guard here, [mark]the curtain itself the true threat[/mark] to a stealthy party. 

[container:indent-20]The curtain will spot a non-invisible target who is hiding in shadows or otherwise stealthy on a 1-4 on a d6 and will then whisper the alert to the minotaur in the room. 

If the minotaur is slain, the curtain will begin to scream for backup. The magic curtain can be shut up with a good reaction check, or by killing it. It bleeds copiously when wounded or killed.[/container]

[h2|49]49. Living Space[/h2]
This room has been used by the minotaur branch of the Erde family as a communal living space. There are signs that several minotaurs have been using it, although it is uninhabited at this time.

[h2|50]50. Warrior's Hall[/h2]
This oddly shaped hall and raised corridor is home to [mark]a pair of knights[/mark] of the Erde clan. These two minotaurs each carry an iron shield and wield a longsword with four javelins as backup against ranged foes. 

They prowl around the northern portion of the room and will attempt to engage foes with hit and run tactics. 

The smaller minotaur is wearing a copper [b]static ring[/b] which provides +1 on all saves against lightning and electrical attack forms, as well as increasing the damage of all electrical spells cast by the wearer by +1 per 2 dice of damage (minimum of +1 damage).

[h2|51]51. The Elder of the Horns[/h2]
The elder of this branch of the Erde clan lives in this room, guarded by his two nephews in [url:#50]Area 50[/url]. [mark]Lord Gursin Erde is a massive and ancient minotaur[/mark], weakened by age and rapidly turning to senility. 

He wears magical [b]+1 chainmail[/b] (the Coat of Horns) that resizes to fit any humanoid wearer between 3 and 12 feet in height. 

In a chest under his bed is the hoard of the clan, 10,000cp and 4,000sp.

[h2|52]52. Impromptu Crypt[/h2]
A pair of dead throghrin have been pinned to the wall here with massive iron spikes after they were slain by the minotaurs. The heavy warhammer that was used to drive the spikes home still lies on the floor near the doorway. 

The insides of the bodies have been consumed by a [mark]furry brown fungus[/mark] and anyone who disturbs them will release a cloud of fungal spores that fill the room. 

[container:indent-20]Anyone in this cloud takes [u]1d6 damage[/u] and must make a [u]CON save, DC10[/u] to avoid a further 1d6 damage and an illness that makes them nauseous (-2 on all attack rolls, reduce movement rate by 30' to a minimum of 30') for 1d4 days or until they are subject to a [i]Cure Disease[/i] spell.[/container]

[h2|53]53. Dark Ruins[/h2]
The centre of these secret chambers has been long ignored. Damaged furnishings of dark-stained stone litter the chamber and the door to [url:#54]Area 54[/url], also made of stone, swings loosely in its frame. The smell of putrefaction is cloying an nearly overwhelming here. [u]Anyone lingering in this chamber or making noise[/u] will attract the attention of the creature in [url:#54]Area 54[/url].

[h2|54]54. Consumption Made Flesh[/h2]
This chamber is home to a mass of putrescent flesh that seems to be made of many human bodies all combined into a mass of eyes, mouths and pseudopods. The central mass is surrounded by massive tentacle-like extensions made of human arms and torsos that [u]reach out to grab any living thing[/u] that wanders within reach. 

This undead beast, has animal intelligence and cunning, but little of that even. It lashes out with its tentacles to draw prey in to its many mouths to be consumed. 
[ul][li]Once a tentacle hits in combat, it will constrict and automatically do its damage each round until severed. The victim is at -1 on all attack rolls for every tentacle that is constricting him. [li]Tentacles can be attacked with spells or edged weapons and will be severed once receiving 8 or more damage - although only half the damage done to the tentacle is actually dealt to the undead beast itself. [/ul]

The ruins around the undead beast contains:[ul][li]several suits of destroyed armour, [li]a shield +2, [li]a spear +1 that deals double damage to water elementals and their kin, and [li]a ruined and pitted crown whose jewels can be salvaged for 500gp.[/ul]

[h2|55]55. Secret Entry[/h2]
The door between this room and [url:#56]Area 56[/url] is jammed tight by moisture and rot, although a hole has been chewed through part of it that will easily fit a halfling, or a human sized adventurer without armour. The undead beast in [url:#54]Area 54[/url] and the giant weasels from level 2 entered this area through said hole. The door was obviously [u]once a secret door[/u]. The trigger to open it (a loose stone on the west wall) is [u]still trapped with a poison needle[/u], although it won't open the door anymore.

[h2|56]56. Sunken Entry[/h2]
Jammed into a small compartment in the west wall of this cave is an old rusted crowbar that was used to try to trigger the secret door once upon a time. Directly above the door, nearly 30 feet overhead, is a [mark]green slime[/mark].

[h2|57]57. The Climb[/h2]
This natural cave has a 30' tall ceiling. Access to [url:#56]Area 56[/url] requires climbing 25 feet of vertical wall before accessing the natural stairs back down. 

Noting that there is an opening up at ceiling level requires careful attention and specifically looking up for it. 

Access to [url:#59]Area 59[/url] is either via the stairs in [url:#58]Area 58[/url], or a 15' dead drop.

[h2|58]58. Displaced Residents[/h2]
[mark]Four gnolls[/mark], are guarding the stairs to [url:#59]Area 59[/url] - they have recently been displaced from their lair by a hungry cave bear.

[h2|59]59. Spoils of War[/h2]
A [mark]massive scarred cave bear[/mark], has just taken over this cave from the prior gnoll residents and is loudly tearing a gnoll apart as his well-deserved dinner. 

A natural cave to the northeast leads up to the surface nearly a half-mile from the manor itself.

[h2|60]60. Dripping Lake[/h2]
This part of the underground lake is filled with water from a subsurface spring as well as a constant dripping of water from above. The water here is crystal clear and slightly magical. 

[container:indent-20]Once per day a drink from this water heals one hit point. It probably also has long-term effects fro those who spend too much time in contact with it, as shown by the mutations of the Erde clan living in areas 65 through 69. [/container]

The waters flow through a flooded passage from here to [url:#61]Area 61[/url]. 
[ul][li]Swimming through this passage is considered difficult terrain (i.e. 1/2 movement rate) unless you have a swim speed. 
[li]It is also pitch black so you swim blind unless you have a magical source of light. 
[li]The underwater distance is 100' including swimming down the the passage and back up at the other end.
[/ul] 

[h2|61]61. The Beach[/h2]
This massive cave is eerily quiet and abandoned. A wooden bridge of fairly recent construction spans the river near a set of stairs leading further into the catacombs beneath these depths. The posts holding the bridge in place are of ancient stone and have obviously been here for ages.

[h2|62]62. Guardians at the Stairs. [/h2]
[mark]Four owl bear skeletons[/mark], stand guard in this cave.

[h2|63]63. Deep Lake[/h2]
This part of the lake goes down to a depth of 80 feet in the centre, and is quite dark. Anyone trying to look into the depths will see an illusion of a massive giant catfish swimming along the bottom and watching for intruders.

[h2|64]64. Preacher's Ledge[/h2]
Fifteen feet above the lake, this small alcove was once a place for religious contemplation. Stony debris has been arranged for some amount of comfort sitting and watching over the lake. Among the stones is an [u]ivory and silver scroll tube[/u] (worth 80gp) containing a clerical scroll of [i]Commune[/i].

[h2|65]65. Welcoming Party[/h2]
This chamber is flooded with the murky waters of the lake to a [u]depth of 2 feet[/u]. 

The flooding marks the dividing line between the portion of the Erde clan devoted to the god of war, and the remaining members of the clan who have hidden here and devolved into strange rubbery throghrins in the flooded lower areas. 

[mark]Three throghrin[/mark], defend this chamber and watch for encroaching members of the northern part of the clan. [u]Two wield polearms[/u], and the [u]third attacks with his bare hands[/u]. They also watch for those who would sneak in through [url:#66]Area 66[/url]. 

In dire emergencies, they will try to escape to [url:#68]Area 68[/url] and ring the gong.

[h2|66]66. Breached Chamber[/h2]
Once a storage chamber, erosion from the lake breached into the depths of Erdea manor here. 

[u]Beyond the breach in the wall, the water depth is some 8 feet[/u] initially and drops rapidly to the depths of the Deep Lake ([url:#63]Area 63[/url]). [u]Within the chamber the water is at a depth of 2 feet.[/u] 

The walls of this chamber are covered in shelves which are in turn enveloped in a particularly noxious [mark]slime mold[/mark]. 

[container:indent-20]Anyone trying to scoop or dig through the mold must make a [u]CON save (DC 10)[/u], at  -3  if doing so with their hands instead of tools. Failure means becoming [u]violently ill and be unable to continue[/u] for [roll:1d6] minutes, as well as engaging in any [u]combat during this time with a -2 penalty[/u] to attack rolls and saves. [/container]

Within the slime mold is: [ul][li]mostly rotten foodstuffs, but [li]there is a small locked coffer containing 1,000sp, as well as [li]a small secret compartment in the wall behind the coffer that contains a pair of 500gp rubies.[/ul]

[h2|67]67. The Magister[/h2]
This flooded side-room is the apartment of the magister, a rubbery old member of the Erde clan. He is currently out of his chamber, however, consulting with the leader of the clan in [url:#69]Area 69[/url]. His [mark]apprentice, however, is here[/mark]. 

He will try to get to [url:#68]Area 68[/url] to ring the gong if threatened or frightened.

[h2|68]68. Meeting Hall[/h2]
This small chamber has ancient moldy tapestries decorating all four walls, and a 3-foot brass gong is suspended by chains from the ceiling in the southwest corner. [u]The ringing of the gong can be heard clearly throughout this section, and can be heard faintly even at the deep lake.[/u] Ringing the gong has a 75% chance to result in a roll on the wandering monster table, as well as alerting the remaining throghrin in the area.

[h2|69]69. The Chapel[/h2]
The old chapel of the Erde clan is now home to the leader of the throghrin portion of the clan, [mark]Gaurius Erde[/mark], who is wearing his clan chain hauberk +1 that grants the wearer +1 Charisma. Gaurius is bedecked in pitted and corroded jewelry, totalling at 3,000gp value (mostly in gem value, as the metal is corroded and fragile).

He is currently in discussion with the [mark]Magister of Erde[/mark], about the present disposition of the minotaur faction of the clan (they believe there are more remaining members of the northern faction than there are now). The magister has a wand of magic detection with 8 charges. 

[mark]Two bodyguards[/mark] stand watch at the entrance to the chapel, one is wielding a polearm and the other attacks barehanded. 

[h2|70]70. Guardians of Erde[/h2]
The stone door between this room and [url:#62]Area 62[/url] is locked, but can easily be opened from this side (there is no key). A pair of [mark]iron living statues[/mark], stand eternal vigil at the doors, and the remains of a third stands where it was slain ages past.

[h2|71]71. Preparatory Chamber[/h2]
This chamber was for preparing the deceased for entombment. There are jars of ointments and strange embalming tools on the old tables and shelves.

[h2|72]72. Antechamber[/h2]
This room was a place to put aside weapons and to cleanse oneself before visiting the tomb of the lost brothers. In the centre of the room is a shallow depression magically filled with water. 

Anyone putting the water directly from the pool to a wound (or someone wounded who enters the water) suffers [u]1d8 damage[/u] as the water erupts into flames around the wound. Carrying the water out of the pool prevents this effect.

[h2|73]73. Tomb of the lost brothers[/h2]
[container:tablebox-40][table]
[tr][th][center][small][roll:1d4][/small][/center][/th][th][small]Curse[/small][/th][/tr]
[tr][td][center][small]1[/small][/center][/td][td][small]Re-roll character's prime requisite on 3d6[/small][/td][/tr]
[tr][td][center][small]2[/small][/center][/td][td][small]Healing is only half as effective as normal (natural and magical)[/small][/td][/tr]
[tr][td][center][small]3[/small][/center][/td][td][small]Lose spellcasting ability[/small][/td][/tr]
[tr][td][center][small]4[/small][/center][/td][td][small]Polymorphed into a spider[/small][/td][/tr]
[/table][/container]
The north, south and west walls of this chamber each have a stone sarcophagus built into them. Each sarcophagus is carved with the likeness of an adult human in plate mail bearing shields with the Erde family crest. 

The west sarcophagus is cracked and shows signs that it has been opened at some point through the use of heavy tools. It is visibly empty.

Anyone breaking into either of the other two unopened ones must succeed on a DC 10 WIS save or suffer a curse rolled on the table at right. [i]Remove curse[/i] will successfully end the curse.

[h2|74]74. Gelatinous Cube[/h2]
This curved corridor and the two side corridors to the east and west are swept perfectly clean by a [mark]gelatinous cube[/mark].

[h2|75]75. Tomb of Helvos Erde III[/h2]
The iron door to this tomb is [u]sealed with an [i]Arcane Lock[/i] spell[/u], and thus will most likely require the use of a [i]Knock[/i] spell to get past. 

Within is a small chamber lit by two [i]Continual Flame[/i] spells on iron globes suspended on chains from the ceiling. Between the globes is a stone plinth supporting a black iron casket with three steel locks. 

[container:indent-20]Once the first lock is picked, Helvos will make his appearance to prevent the desecration of his tomb - he is now a wraith, Fly:240 (80), and rests within the stone plinth under his casket. Within the casket is the [mark]embalmed corpse of Helvos Erde III[/mark], along with his golden chain of office as a magister of the empire (1,400gp value) and a silver dagger decorated with gems on the pommel (800gp value). 

There is a locked coffer at the feet of the corpse, [u]protected by a poison needle[/u]. Within the coffer are:
[ul][li]a [i]Potion of Control Animal[/i], [li]a [i]Potion of Levitation[/i], [li]a [i]Scroll of Protection from Lycanthropes[/i], and [li]a [i]Scroll of Fireball[/i].[/ul]

[h2|76]76. “Trapped” Skeletons[/h2]
This chamber is enclosed with a portcullis of heavy and pitted iron bars. Behind the bars are a the [u]skeletons of what appear to be a dozen or more men at arms[/u] in the remnants of their armour and weapons. Two are still clutching at the bars as if they died trying to lift the portcullis to escape. 
In total there are [mark]16 skeletons[/mark] in this chamber which will only spring to animation if someone joins them in their prison.

[h2|77]77. Tomb of Huhren Erde the Wise[/h2]
This tomb has been plundered, the sarcophagus split assunder and bones scattered across the floor. Smashed urns are in the two back corners of the room.

articles.title: DoE: Level 4
articles.content:
[h1]Notes[/h1]
Deep under the caves of Erdea Manor lie the secret catacombs and deeper recesses of the clan's estates as well as the lair of the most powerful remaining elder of their line.

[h2]Wandering Monsters[/h2]
1 in 8 chance,  check each turn and roll 2d3 for type vs. table at right.

[h1]Level 4 Key[/h1]
[h2|78]78. The Mists[/h2]
The bottom of the rough stairs leading down into this natural cave are hidden in the dark mists that fill the room and seep into [url:#79]Area 79[/url] and [url:#80]Area 80.[/url] The mists are an unnatural black in colour and are magical in nature - even if dispersed by wind or magic they will return within a turn. 

[container:indent-20]Anyone entering the mists for the first time that day must make a [u]CON saving throw (DC 10)  or suffer a -2 penalty on attacks, saving throws and damage rolls for the rest of the day[/u]. They will also have the feeling that proceeding beyond will make these penalties worse (although the penalties will actually remain the same). [/container]

[u]NPCs and monsters attempting to traverse the mists[/u] who fail their save must also make a Morale check or retreat from them.

[h2|79]79. Spiders in the Mist[/h2]
The mists from [url:78]Area 78[/url] creep along the floor of this room to a height of 2 feet. Hiding within the mists are [mark]3 Mist Widows[/mark] (a magical variant of the giant black widow spider) which appear to be undead skeletal spiders to first appearances. 

They have covered the floor of the main area of this room with their webs, and [u]anyone entering that area without first probing ahead will be trapped within their webs[/u]. 

[container:indent-20]Each creature that starts its turn in the webs or that enters them during its turn must make a DEX saving throw (DC 12). On a failed save, the creature is restrained as long as it remains in the webs or until it breaks free.

A creature restrained by the webs can use its action to make a Strength check against your spell save DC. If it succeeds, it is no longer restrained.[/container]

[h2|80]80. Mausoleum Door[/h2]
This chamber prevents entry into the secret crypts of the Erde clan. The dark mists of [url:78]Area 78.[/url] roll down the stairs to this area and covers the floor to a depth of roughly 18 inches (slightly deeper closer to the stairs, thinner by the egress to [url:82]Area 82.[/url]). 

Unlike the rough natural caves around it, the south end of this chamber is finished in smooth high-quality masonry, as is the floor and ceiling. The doors to the crypt are massive stone doors, each 14 feet tall and 5 feet wide. Three pale white stone pillars stand out in stark contrast to their surroundings, reaching from floor to ceiling. 

The [u]doors are secured by an [i]Arcane Lock[/i][/u]. Other than using a [i]Knock[/i] spell, attempting to open the massive portals will create a loud magical din that attracts an automatic wandering monster roll.

[h2|81]81. Secret Crypts[/h2]
The major room is the family tomb and mausoleum for the elders of the Erde clan, with two connected passages of crypts leading off to the east and west. A [mark]pair of rock living statues[/mark], that look like representations of clan warriors guards the chamber against interlopers. There are a total of 12 crypts off the sides of this chamber which contain:

[table]
[tr][td:5]a)[/td][td]A plain wooden coffin with the poorly preserved corpse of Hevroth Erde IV, strapped into his coffin by metal bands. [mark]He is a wight[/mark] but remains still until someone comes within easy reach.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]b)[/td][td]A dark oak casket holding a pair of wooden logs.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]c)[/td][td]A rusted and locked iron casket with a plaque dedicated to Gersidi Erde, Queen of the Rats. Within the casket are [mark]40 skeletal rats[/mark]. 
[container:indent-20]When attacking they are treated as four groups of ten rats each but each rat has only 1 hit point, making the rats hard to disrupt effectively. When there are only 30 rats left, break them into 3 groups, and into 2 groups for 20 rats, 1 group at 10 rats or less, and they are rendered ineffective in combat when reduced to fewer than 5 rats.

The rats carry a wasting disease - those injured must make a [u]DC 12 CON save become devastatingly ill[/u], losing [roll:1d6] Strength and Constitution every day for 1d6 days. If either stat is reduced to zero or below, the victim dies and reanimates as a wight. [/container]

At the bottom of the casket is the gnawed skeleton of Gersidi, wearing a magical steel medallion that protects the wearer from attacks by rats and giant rats (the rat must make a Morale check in order to attack, and suffers a -4 to hit if can attack at all).[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]d)[/td][td]Walnut casket containing a skeleton in rotten finery[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]e)[/td][td]A wooden coffin with peeling black paint, nailed shut. Within is a weathered corpse with a stake through the heart, it's head cut off and it's mouth stuffed with garlic.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]f)[/td][td]This wooden coffin has nearly rotted away.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]g)[/td][td]This fine casket with silver hinges and handles contains the [u]skeleton of Hevroth Erde II[/u] still dressed in his rottednoble finery, bearing his longsword, and wearing a massive silver necklace worth 1,300gp.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]h)[/td][td]This crypt is empty except for dust and rat droppings.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]i)[/td][td]This massive casket contains two bodies, brother princes of Erde, still strangling each other even in death.[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]j)[/td][td]The casket doesn't contain a body, but instead a portrait of Revnan Erde the Mighty, complete with his signature black flaming battle axe.[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]k)[/td][td]This rotten wooden coffin contains [u]the body of Hellenus Erde[/u], completely covered in yellow mold. Under the mold she is still wearing her prized silver and opal necklace (1,100gp).[p][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]l)[/td][td]This rusted iron casket has a plaque dedicated to “the un-named brother”. Although the casket seems empty, within is [mark]an invisible wight[/mark] dressed in tattered remnants of Erde family clothes. The [u]invisibility is as per the spell and will dissipate when he attacks[/u].[/td][/tr]
[/table]
[h2|82]82. The Well[/h2]
A strange darkness lives deep within the hole that was once a backup water supply. This [mark]dark water elemental[/mark], will [u]only attack if disturbed.[/u] 

[container:indent-20]It deals [roll:1d8] damage to anyone touching or in water, and anyone dealt damage by it must make a [u]CHA saving throw (DC12)  or be affected by a [i]Bane[/i] spell[/u] (whenever a target that fails this saving throw makes an attack roll or a saving throw before the spell ends, the target must roll a d4 and subtract the number rolled from the attack roll or saving throw).[/container]

[h2|83]83. Steel Remains[/h2]
Pinned to the west wall of this cavern by four iron spears is an odd skeleton. It appears almost typical of human remains, except that all the bones are made of steel.

[h2|84]84. Rocks Fall[/h2]
The entrance to this chamber off the side of the complex is weakened by age. Each person entering the chamber has a [u]1 in 6 chance of triggering the collapse[/u] of the entranceway and partial collapse of the chamber itself. All in the entranceway and the chamber must make saving throws versus petrification or suffer damage. Those in the room suffer [roll:1d12], those in the entranceway suffer [roll:3d12]. The collapse will block passage from the chamber to the complex except to halfling-sized or smaller characters. Clearing the rubble will take [roll:1d4] turns. And of course, the collapse will call forth a wandering monster.

[h2|85]85. Fungal Ruins[/h2]
This cave is thick with foul smelling fungus, including [mark]5 shriekers[/mark].

[h2|86]86. The Flayer[/h2]
The [mark]spectral remains of Hevroth Erde the First[/mark] haunt this cavern, although he occasionally tours much of this level. When slain, his black teeth scatter to the ground, a full dozen 250gp obsidian gems.

[h2|87]87. Secret Chamber[/h2]
Within this secret chamber is the remaining treasures of the flayer. The [u]secret door is raised four feet above floor level[/u] of the cave outside the room, and is triggered by a catch at the top of the door. 

Four massive urns are inside, each sealed with wax. [ul][li]The first contains wine, now long sour. [li]The second contains 10,000sp. [li]The third contains 3,000gp. [li]The final urn contains the decomposed and broken body of the Flayer himself, Hevroth Erde the First, along with Hevroth's Ring, a silver ring that is perpetually tarnished black, set with a ruby. The wearer of the ring can cast [i]Hold Monster[/i] 2/day and [i]Charm Person[/i] 1/day.[/ul]

[h2|88]88. Darkest Hole[/h2]
Here the tortured souls of those flayed and slain by Hevrorth Erde the First congregate in the deepest and darkest part of the catacombs. [mark]11 shadows[/mark] lurk in this cave, hiding in the darkness,

[h2|89]89. Mist Widows[/h2]
[mark]Three more mist widows[/mark] have built a colony here. 

Within their webs at the back of the cave is the body of a dwarf still wearing his [u]Chain Mail +1[/u] and carrying a [u]Battle Axe +1, +2 versus goblins[/u]. His pack contains adventuring gear and 72gp.

[h2|90]90. Nobles of Ancient Erde[/h2]
This natural cavern is the lair of [mark]5 powerful and ancient Erde ghouls[/mark]. They are antagonistic to the Flayer but stay out of his way because they understand his great power. 

Each wears a piece of jewelry denoting their ancient noble lineage:
[ul][li]a gold torc (1,200gp), [li]a silver medallion (400gp), [li]a jeweled ring (900gp), [li]a silver bracer inlayed with gold (1,200gp) and [li]a platinum diadem (600gp).[/ul]
In addition to the diadem, the strongest of the ghouls is also carrying his ancient blade, a short sword +2, +4 versus humans that glows with the light of a torch when unsheathed. 

In the two alcoves on the west side of the cavern are the remains of their prior meals, including 3,000cp, and 2,000ep.

articles.title: DOE: NPCs and Monsters
articles.content:
[h1]NPCs / Named Monsters[/h1]
[h2]Jubal[/h2]
A dark and twisted [b]ogre[/b] with some spellcasting ability (although no Ogre Mage by any stretch of the imagination). He can cast [i]Bane[/i] (the opposite of Bless), [i]Protection from Good[/i], and [i]Fear[/i], each once per day as a level 4 cleric. His massive staff with dangling skulls and feathers makes the targets of his fear spell save at a -2 penalty. 

The magical charms on the staff can be transferred to a smaller staff or similar weapon, allowing the magical benefit to be passed on to smaller spellcasters. 

[h2]Vera[/h2]

[h2]Willhelm[/h2]

[h2]CueOne & CueTwo[/h2]
A year ago Jubal split his dark and twisted soul into three pieces – one that he keeps himself, and two that have split off into smaller little “essences of Jubal” that he calles CueOne and CueTwo. While looking like [b]tiny ogres[/b],  they are [u]re-skinned Quasits[/u] (and thus size "small"). 

CueOne will likely be first encountered in [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#17]room 17[/url] while CueTwo is stationed in [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#16]area 16[/url].

[h2]Orthur[/h2]
An [b]ogre[/b], Orthur is a lieutenant to Vera / Jubal and based out of [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#19]room 19[/url]. He wishes that his bosses would get along.

[h1]Generic Monsters[/h1]

articles.title: DoE: Surface
articles.content:
[h1]Notes[/h1]
[h2]Entrances & Egresses[/h2]
[ul][li][b]Area 5:[/b] stairs down to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#6]Level 1, Area 6[/url].
[/ul]

[h2]Wandering Monsters[/h2]
Roll every turn, 1 in 6 chance. Roll [roll:1d4] on the wandering monster chart at right. 

[h1]Surface Key[/h1]
[h2|1]1. Manor Ruins[/h2] 
The manor grounds stand in ruins after several centuries of neglect. Remains of a few structures can be found with most remaining walls having been worn down to 3 to 5 feet in height. Two structures have withstood the test of time - the old keep ([url:#3]Area 3[/url]) and a 20-foot-tall tower near the old crypts ([url:#4]Area 4[/url]). The well ([url:#2]Area 2[/url] remains mostly intact also. 

In the hillside to the west of the manor structures is a staircase ([url:#5]Area 5[/url]) descending into the depths ([url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#6]Level 1, Area 6[/url]). 

There is also a secret door in the southern ruins leading to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#21]Level 1, Area 21[/url] and the depths to Erdea Manor that is unlikely to be discovered without finding it from below or with the use of a treasure map. 

[mark]4 Ogres[/mark], are camped out in the ruins (mostly near ruins in the lee of the hill where the one ancient oak door still stands) guarding against intruders and making sure the dwarves in the keep are “under control”. A fight here may be observed by the dwarves in [url:#3]Area 3[/url], and will probably be joined by the ogre in [url:#4]Area 4[/url].

[h2|2]2. The Well[/h2]
Unlike just about any other dungeon environment, the well is just that, a fairly deep surface well eight feet across and 60 feet deep, the last 20 of which are submerged under clear water. There is a [mark]rope ladder [/mark] bundled up on the ground beside the well, left here by the dwarves when they investigated it to see if there are any secret doors along the shaft.

[h2|3]3. Dwarven Keepers[/h2]
The old keep of the manor grounds is still strong and easy to defend, with the main door 8 feet above ground level and approached by steep stone stairs. 

The keep is home to [mark]15 dwarves[/mark], that survived the ogres overrunning the grounds. The dwarves stocked the keep for just such an emergency, and are good to remain here for a few weeks to a month if necessary. Having observed the ogres a bit, they believe that the ogres will not stick around for long, and are willing to sit out the so called “ogre siege” in here. 

They [mark]will not open the locked and barred door[/mark] for anything at this point, wanting nothing to do with the ogres or the ogres' enemies. 

The dwarves have 2,000pp in a locked chest trapped with two different poison needle traps (one for the lock, a second for anyone opening the chest). The leader wears a jeweled golden beard-clasp worth 1,200gp and her mate bears a pair of ceremonial platinum hand axes worth 600gp each.

[h2|4]4. Guard Post[/h2]
This tower reaches up twenty feet into the sky. The roof is long gone, and the interior of the heavy-walled tower is open to the elements. However it does provide nice protection from the wind and a place to light a camp fire without it being seen for miles around. 

Willhelm, an ogre, of great culinary skill, is in the tower cooking dwarf-bone-bread over the fire. He will join his brethren in [url:#1]Area 1[/url] if he hears a fight out there, hurling a flask of flaming oil into the fight before running in to melee. 

The only thing of value here are Willhelm's sack which contains a jar of dwarf-bone flour (300gp), and a large wheel of excellent cheese.

[h2|5]5. There is no #5?[/h2]
Actually it's behind all the shrubs and overgrowth that covers most of the the opening (doors long since gone) down to [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/doe3A-level-1-plot#6]Level 1, Area 6[/url].

articles.title: FCoH Scenarios
articles.content:
The following is a list of scenarios that can be used as side-quests or possibly as a jumping off point to a longer questline. They are grouped according to where they start and/or where the main action takes place.

[li][b]@[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b)[/b]

articles.title: FCoH Street Encounter Tables
articles.content:
The following is a list of random street encounters and where they might occur. They are primarily simple one-off encounters but a few could lead to something more significant. For encounters by borough/district, refer to the main article for that location (links at right).

articles.title: FCoH Street Encounters
articles.content:
[h1|Dog-End]1) @[Dog-end](person:43bf9989-d60c-4bb8-969d-021cdd15ee7a)[/h1]





[h1|Boy_Fight]2) Boy Fight[/h1]
As the group strolls down the street, two young boys start fighting about 10' in front of them. At the height of the conflict, a third urchin will attempt to cut an item of value from a randomly selected member of the party and make off with it.

The young thief, @[Vechin](person:ff1cf9f8-87fd-4878-a520-97a286355218) (#901) has a pickpocketing chance of 45%, but he is only 1st level. If the thief succeeds, the character will lose some valuable-appearing item worn outside his clothing (purse, scabbard, pouch, etc.). 

If the boy is noticed, he will run into, and down, an alley less than 20' away (the trio of boys stages their operation carefully) and disappear over a wooden fence. It requires CON check (DC 12) to catch the boy. While the thief is being chased, the other two will, of course, also disappear.  

[h1|Guide]3) Guide[/h1]
A young street rat (sex indeterminable, due to dirt) approaches the party, and suggests that they employ the youngster as a guide, for a silver piece.

If the party does not hire the urchin, they will follow the party for 2 blocks, hurling epithets and mud, garbage, etc. (unless chased away). If the party does hire the kid, there is an 80% chance that they know the location if it is close by, 50% if it is within a reasonably local area, and 10% if it is "somewhere in the city*". In cases of lack of knowledge, the urchin will lead the party in circles, until the group grows tired and drives the child off.

[h1|Shell_Game]4) Shell Game[/h1]
Standing at the side of the way is a man, with a small felt covered table in front of him. On the table are three silver cups, one of which conceals a little golden ball. The table has been strategically placed near the entrance to an alley. The man is rallying the crowd of (2d10) watchers around him to 

"Try your ability! Is the hand really faster than the eye? Any amount from a bit to a gold piece! Can you find the little gold ball?"
The huckster is running the old shell game, and he is good! If a character elects to play, the following method works well. 
[ol]
[li]The ball is displayed under a given cup, A, B, or C. 
[li]The huckster then makes 3d10 moves. 
[li]Roll d100 - on [ul][li]01-20, the ball is under cup A. [li]On 21-40, cup B. [li]On 41-60, cup C. [li]And on 61-00, it is concealed in his hand. If the characters demand to see the location of the ball, the huckster will slip it under the cup he is lifting – Perception roll (plus Sleight of Hand bonus) vs. DC 21 to see him do it [/ul]
[/ol]

If he should be caught cheating, he will immediately run off, leaving the table, cups, and ball (the equipment has a total worth of about 2gp) for whoever wishes to claim it. 

Note: The original formula to detect cheating ends up at approx. 85% chance of huckster doing it unnoticed: (110 + # of moves) - ([DEX + WIS + INT of highest character)

[h1|Centaur_Cab]5) Centaur Cab[/h1]
A centaur cab comes pell-mell down the street at full speed. The driver/motive power is yelling at the top of his lungs. The other pedestrians scatter quickly. The cab rattles quickly past the party.

All characters in the group should DEX check vs. DC 15 to avoid being thoroughly splashed with muddy water and garbage. If the check is missed by 1/3 of the character's DEX or more, the character has been struck by the careening cab and suffers 2d4 damage [roll:2d4]. If maximum damage is rolled, a CON check (DC 12) is required to avoid a broken bone (d10): 1-3 = arm, 4-8 = 1d6 ribs, 9-10 = leg.

[h1|Traffic_Jam]6) Traffic Jam[/h1]
Some fifteen cursing drayers, cabbies, and riders are trying to untangle their animals and vehicles from a near pile-up which blocks the near-by intersection completely. One of the wagons appears to be loaded with small parcels, but no one seems to be paying particular attention to it.

If characters wish to attempt removal of the parcels, a DEX check (DC 10) is required for each package to be removed unnoticed. The packages each contain quality merchandise (small objects d'art, less costly jewelry, bric-a-brac) worth 1d10 x 1d10 gp each.

[h1|Caravan]7) Caravan[/h1]
 [i] (Happens only on Caravan Street, otherwise no encounter)[/i] [br]

[container:tablebox-50]
[table]
[tr][th][/th][th]Small[/th][th]Medium[/th][th]Large[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]Traders[/td][td]3d6[/td][td]3d10[/td][td]6d12[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Animals[/td][td]3d20[/td][td]10d20[/td][td]10d100[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Guards[/td][td]4d8[/td][td]8d10[/td][td]10d20[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Wagons[/td][td]30% 2d4[/td][td]50% 4d6[/td][td]75% 8d10[/td][/tr]
[tr][td]Travelers[/td][td]4d6[/td][td]4d10[/td][td]8d12[/td][/tr]
[/table]
[/container]
A colorful (and relatively noisy) caravan is passing. A caravan consists of traders, animals, wagons, guards, and travelers. The animals will be (d10): 1-3 = horses; 4-9 = mules, donkeys, or burros; 10 = camels. The caravan is (d10): 1-3 = small; 4-8 = medium; 9-10 = large. 

The table at right provides the caravan makeup. It will take approximately 1/2 hour for a small caravan to file past, about 2 hours for a medium, and up to 6 hours for a very large group.

[h1|Land_Scam]8) Land Scam[/h1]
A booth with a colorful awning has been set up along the road. It is decorated with bright banners and streamers, and a man out front is bellowing about great bargains in land for those who "have always wanted to own the land that could feed them handsomely." Inside the booth's tent-like structure may be seen a desk, with a second man seated behind it.

These two entrepreneurs are selling bargain plots of land "in the beautiful foothills of the Mountains of Peace", about 250 miles north and slightly east of Haven. The land is there, and the titles are good, but the parcels will be infested with unfriendly orcs, who have been driven out of the Peace Mountains by the hordes of Lizarim which have moved in. The costs are set at only 10 gp an acre, a most reasonable price.

[h1|Rats]9) Rats[/h1]
A display of trained rats is occurring at a nearby intersection. The rodents are being put through a series of tricks and stunts by a brightly dressed man, who rewards each successful performance with a small piece of cheese from his pocket. Nearby is parked a colorfully painted caravan (a gypsy style wagon-house), from which an assistant occasionally appears to deposit a fresh cage of rats, and retrieve another.

These rats are very well-trained thieves as well as performers. There is a 15% chance that one of the rats will attempt to steal a small item from a random member of the party. It requires a saving roll against 1/4 (INT + WIS) for the character to notice the theft attempt. Otherwise, he will lose a coin, a gem, or a small item that is loosely attached. The rats return to the wagon and deposit their take (in return for food). The wagon contains some 1,500gp in rat accumulated merchandise (yes, these are pack rats).

[h1|Puppets]10) Puppets[/h1]
A small puppet theater has been set up on a street corner. As the party approaches, Punch is thwacking Judy with a club.

If the party pauses to watch (this could be decided by a saving roll against the average WIS for the party), there will be a pickpocketing attempt on a random member of the group. 

The @[Puppet Show Thief](person:c636fc50-b225-414c-9791-a73b4b78dd6e) (#902) is 4th level and has a pickpocketing skill of 65%. He is quite fleet and will be able to outdistance all but a loaded bow since it requires only 30 seconds for him to reach the nearest alley. If the theft is successful, the victim's purse (wallet, pouch, or what have you) will have been emptied, and all carried cash will be gone.

[h1|Warning]11) Warning[/h1]
A youth sprints out of a nearby alley, and crashes through the party (bumping into a randomly selected player-character). He spins around, regaining his balance, then dashes into another alley and disappears entirely.

The character who was bumped into will find that a small piece of parchment has been pressed into their hand during the confusion. The message on it is in the common script and reads "Get out of town quick - @[Zarken](person:9d2f6ace-ebdc-4e9f-9540-b5f02bb191aa) is looking for you!"

[h1|Collection]12) Collection[/h1]
Two goliaths (Kuori Anakalathia and Vimak Anakalathia), armed with stout cudgels (i.e."tree trunks"), approach the party from behind. They have very obviously been following the group for about 6 blocks. They select one member of the party by referring to a piece of vellum one of them carries and inform him (in very deep bass voices) that No-Yen expects his money back, that the note the character signed is already 60 days overdue, and that they intend to collect, NOW!

The piece of vellum is a line drawing of the debtor involved, which happens to closely resemble the character selected from the party. If the party is sufficiently awed by the sheer size of the goliaths and antes up the money (the amount claimed to be owed is 1d10 x 1d10 x 1d10 x 2gp), the giants will leave peaceably. If the party refuses, the giants will attempt direct seizure of the person of the “debtor", with the intention of hauling him off to @[No-Yen Drachmah](person:a2bf94aa-e6d4-490d-8506-eaf4c163f0b3) (see North Corridor Shops). The goliaths will fight, if necessary, to accomplish their goal. There is a 35% chance that the Guard will be attracted by the ruckus, and intervene before anyone is injured seriously, arresting all parties concerned, and hustling them off to the nearest Guard barracks.

[h1|Relative]13) Relative?[/h1]
A reasonably well-dressed person begins following the party, making no attempt to conceal their presence, or the interest they show in the group.

If the party does not turn to investigate, the person will follow the group for 2d10+10 [roll:2d10+10] minutes. Whether the party approaches the person, or the person approaches the party, they will ask one particular character many questions concerning childhood, family, relatives, etc. At some point, the well-to-do person will proclaim the character a long-lost relative (15% chance the character actually is) - from that point, the GM is on their own.

[h1|Assassin]14) Assassin[/h1]
(If encounter #11 has not yet occurred, select another encounter) An arrow narrowly misses (or just barely hits) a member of the party. As the party searches for the hidden @[Bowman](person:58128d73-dc37-4908-9d64-420287f85901) (or cowers behind cover), a cloaked figure can be spied, disappearing from a nearby building roof.

The figure is @[Zarken](person:9d2f6ace-ebdc-4e9f-9540-b5f02bb191aa) (#903), an assassin, mentioned in the note passed to the player in encounter #11. The target is the player-character who received the note (an obvious case of mistaken identity). There is a 25% chance that the arrow has struck, doing minor damage (1d4 hits [roll:1d4]). If this encounter occurs a fourth time for the same character, @[Zarken](person:9d2f6ace-ebdc-4e9f-9540-b5f02bb191aa) will not miss. There is an 85% chance that the shot will be fatal immediately.

[h1|Chase]15) Chase[/h1]
A fleet-footed young person dashes through the party, with 3 guardsmen hot on his tail.

A cutpurse is fleeing the law. They weren't quite adept enough at his chosen profession yet. If the guard catches the speedy one, they will likely never become adept.

[h1|Stallions]16) "Stallions"[/h1]
A troop of young horsemen, mounted on steeds of excellent quality, thunders down the street past the party. They are wearing green swordsman's capes, secured with white brooches, and knee-boots of black leather.

These are members of the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c). If any of the party is nobility, and is wearing evidence of rank (emblazoned shield, coat of arms, etc.), they will rein up, and start a conversation. Otherwise, chances and saving rolls are as in encounter #5.

[h1|Hell-fire]17) Hell-fire[/h1]
A reasonably decent looking woman in her late thirties is declaiming to anyone who will listen about the iniquities of just about anything that is any fun at all. She begins to follow the party, calling them sinners, consigning them to various forms of hellfire and brimstone if they don't change their "sinful ways", etc. She cannot be driven off and will continue to make a fuss until the guard arrives (2d10 [roll:2d10] minutes), and rather unceremoniously hauls her away.

As long as the party does no actual harm to the woman, there will be no problem. If harm is done to her, the guard will arrest everybody.

[h1|Book_Sale]18) Book Sale[/h1]
The party is approached by @[Ubechalyff](person:1cad61e7-69d2-444e-847d-ad6b81c617ca) (#904), a furtive, stoop shouldered man with a bushy black mustache, very thick, very mobile eyebrows, and heavy spectacles. He is wearing bulky clothing, including a coat (which is way too long for him), and has a large cigar which appears to be permanently clenched in his left fist. 

As he approaches, he walks with a curious weaving, low-hipped motion. He will, amidst a barrage of useless chatter, patter, and bad jokes, make an offer to sell the party a book. The book has a black leather cover, a cover lock (locked), and is quite thick and reasonably heavy. The man claims he "found it on @[Magic Street](settlement:7f47361e-705c-471d-9c89-8beaa6aad852), which could be significant, but then again, if you say the magic word, it could be a duck. I don't read magic, anyway." He has no key for the lock, of course.

The man will ask 1,000gp for the book. He can easily be argued down to 150gp (this may require an hour of bad puns and wise cracks, however). The book will be one of several things:

[ol]
[li]A big black book (with a list of, and ratings for, a lot of the girls on @[The Street of Silk Veils](settlement:587a7a3a-ba45-4bfd-94bc-fe6e17ae023c).
[li]A trapped demon (there is a 65% chance the demon will simply take off, glad to regain his freedom).
[li]An honest-to-gods, actual, real, not-faked magic book (GM's discretion as to contents).
[li]An incomplete halfling genealogy.
[li]A topologically altered duck (the spell will wear off in about 3 hours, and the duck will reappear).
[/ol]
Once he has either sold the book or been totally rejected, the man will @[Bob](person:a6d18909-f289-433c-ad20-5c682eac3f83) and weave on his way (...never to be seen again?).

[h1|Buy_a_Duck]19) Buy a Duck?[/h1]
The man described in encounter #18 Book Sale, @[Ubechalyff](person:1cad61e7-69d2-444e-847d-ad6b81c617ca), now carrying a duck under his arm, sidles up to the party, and inquires, "Wanna buy a duck?" He claims that the duck is magical, can talk, and lays golden eggs. He is willing to sell it for only 250gp.

As it happens, the duck fulfills 2 of the 3 claims made: it is magical, and it does lay golden eggs. Every 1d4 [roll:1d4] days the duck will produce an egg, the yolk of which is pure gold, and worth 1d10gp [roll:1d10], depending on the size of the yolk. 

However, after 5d20 days [roll:5d20], the duck will sicken, and egg production will cease. A visit to a veterinarian will provide the information that the duck is suffering from a rare disease which prevents egg laying. This disease can be arrested by feeding the duck special herbs, which can only be found in far (GM's choice of country).

[h1|Yok]20) Yok (Dup of #62)[/h1]
 [s]Down the street toward the party comes a sedan chair borne by four very large and burly men. In the sedan chair sits a gnome-like man with an elongated head. As they group draws abreast of the party, they stop, and the midget inside addresses the party. "I am Yok, the seer, who sees all the future. I know what you wish to do. For a portion of your gains, I will help you to accomplish your objectives. Come with me." At a signal, the sedan chair moves off down the street.

The sedan chair will be carried into an alley. If the party follows, the men who are carrying the chair will take a twisting turning path calculated to confuse anybody. Eventually it will lead to a cul-de-sac with a door at the end. Yok is a fraud. He can see the same distance into the future as anybody else - little or none. However, his burly aides (#905, #906, #907, #908) are accomplished muggers, who will seize the first safe opportunity (when the party is unaware) to practice their profession.
[/s]

[container:tablebox-40]
[table][tr][th][roll:d20][/th][th]Term[/th][/tr][tr][td]1–3[/td][td]1 year[/td][/tr][tr][td]4–10[/td][td]3 years[/td][/tr][tr][td]11–18[/td][td]7 years[/td][/tr][tr][td]19-20[/td][td]15 years[/td][/tr][/table]
[br]
[table][tr][th][roll:d2][/th][th]Sex[/th][th]Value[/th][/tr][tr][td]1[/td][td]Male[/td][td]x3[/td][/tr][tr][td]2[/td][td]Female[/td][td]x5[/td][/tr][/table]
[br]
[table]
[tr][th:30][roll:d100][/th][th:30]Age Group[/th][th]Random Value[/th][/tr]
[tr][td]01-25[/td][td]14-19[/td][td][roll:1d6+13*8][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]26-60[/td][td]20-25[/td][td][roll:1d6+19*10][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]61-80[/td][td]26-35[/td][td][roll:1d10+25*6][/td][/tr]
[tr][td]81-95[/td][td]36-50[/td][td][roll:2d8+34*2][/td][/tr]

[/table]
