articles.title: Key: Forest of Doom
articles.content:
[h1]Note[/h1]
[b][i]This has been modified version from the original adventure (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign. [/i][/b]

[h1]Introduction[/h1]
For as far back in time as the tales of history stretch, the area bordering what is now the Lands of Launewt has been a foreboding, practically impenetrable forest. When the lords who ruled the various borderlands united to form the Council of Launewt a century ago, the forest was in the same physical condition as it is now: thick, dark, and expansive, said to be so dense at the center that sunlight pervaded the foliage only on the brightest days.

The forest is also, as it has always been, home to all manner of vicious animals and evil-minded denizen. The animals are generally only dangerous when one intrudes on their territory. The more intelligent evil creatures of the forest, however, often come out to the borderlands to make trouble. In even the oldest historical accounts, mention is made of sporadic raids by the evil creatures on villages and outposts in the borderlands. These tribulations have long been a fact of life for the citizens of the Lands of Launewt – bothersome, sometimes to the extreme, but usually not mounted by a large enough force to do any lasting damage.

On rarer occasions, the forest dwellers have collaborated in organized mass assaults. The provinces and communities of the Lands of Launewt have defenses that are not easily breached – but large villages have been known to be overrun and plundered by hordes of attackers. The cumulative effect of a series of these organized assaults can be devastating – stripping from an area the size of an entire province virtually all the area’s material wealth and a good portion of its able-bodied citizenry.

Two generations of men have come and gone since the last time a group of such assaults took place – but now there is evidence, at least in some people’s minds, of a conflict that threatens to escalate into another costly war. A minority faction in the Council of Launewt believes that the so-called sporadic raids have, of late, become systematic and purposeful; several villages have been plundered in a short time.

But most of the council members have turned a deaf ear to the assertions of their more cautious fellows, insisting that the raids do not fall into a pattern and maintaining that occasional conflicts of this nature are not worthy of the Council’s attention, and should be left for the local militias to deal with. A splinter group within this majority has a neutral outlook; its members realize the dangerous potential of a united enemy, but hesitate to believe that there is a single force capable of consolidating and unifying all the evil humanoids of the forest.

Such differing opinions don’t mix well. As a result, the Council of Launewt is in turmoil, its normally well-ordered decision-making process now paralyzed. Nothing is being done, even to the extent of preparing a contingency plan just in case the minority turns out to be right.

The council members predicting a mass invasion have gathered physical evidence and information to support their position: items like small, strange darts smeared and smudged with a harmless, fungus-like substance; stories like those told by men who claim to have ventured close to the forest’s edge in pursuit of an evil band, and then turned back after glimpsing dark, shadowy shapes in the underbrush.

None of the evidence gathered so far has swayed any of the other council members, though. They assert that the darts are simply ordinary objects, and since the fungus coating is harmless, it must be symbolic, and not dangerous, in nature. As support for their position, they cite examples of strange short swords and other weapons recovered from the sites of battles – their blades so pitted and poorly worked that they bear mute witness to the inferiority of the creatures who forged them. The tales of dark shapes in the trees they dismiss as nothing more than fable, stories invented by terrified peasants and soldiers as a way to gain a certain measure of notoriety – or to hide their cowardice.

In hopes of obtaining conclusive evidence to support their position, several members of the minority faction have sought the aid of small groups of adventurers who would be willing – for a price, of course – to brave the evils of the forest and get at the truth of the matter. During the last few weeks, several bands of explorers have set out on missions into the forest. So far, none of them have been heard from – meaning, perhaps, that they have met and fallen to stronger adversaries, or perhaps that they have thought better of the whole idea and decided to not enter the forest after all.

Now, another such opportunity is being offered to a band of adventurers who seem more stalwart than any who have gone before them. A young lord of the Council of Launewt, after seeing his father die in a recent raid on his family’s home village, has collected a few scraps of information that he hopes will demonstrate that the threat of an evil invasion is indeed real, and will encourage this new group of adventurers to seek even more information – and avenge his father’s death at the same time.

“Goblins are not the most reliable source of information one could want,” says the young lord, “but there seems good reason to heed something I heard about just three days ago. Several of these vile creatures were killed during one of their raids into our countryside. One of them, just before it died, became delirious and babbled almost incoherently about someone or something called 'Arron’ – perhaps the name of a leader, or the name of a ruling order the goblins pay homage to. One of the goblin’s babblings translates roughly as ‘victory will belong to the noble.'

“We know that goblins are certainly not ‘noble’ – and, from what we have learned about them, they do not see themselves as noble. These words, if they are more than just the ravings of a mad gob-lin, seem to point to the existence of an evil force stronger and more devious than the goblins themselves. But the exact nature of this force is still unknown to us.

“I strongly suspect,” continued the young lord, “that the creature or organization called ‘Arron’ is responsible for pulling together the evil creatures of the forest into a fighting force that threatens the life and property of every resident of the Lands of Launewt. As revenge for my father’s death, I want proof that the evil designs of Arron have been ruined and the threat to our homeland destroyed. I offer you 5,000 gold pieces as a reward, plus the gratitude of everyone in the Lands of Launewt. Even those who have foolishly refused to recognize the threat will see the wrongness of their views when your proof is put before them.” 

After the adventurers accept the young lord’s offer, he concludes his instructions to them with a brief description of how to get to the edge of the forest. It is at this point, on the fringe of the unknown territory, where the adventure begins.
articles.fullfooter:
[h1]Credits[/h1]
FOREST of DOOM[br]
An adventure for AD&D™ players[br]
Designed by Scott Butler

For a party of 4-8 characters, each of 4th-7th level[br]
First place, Module Design Contest category A-2

As published in [i]Dragon Magazine[/i], issue 73, May 1983

articles.title: Key: Goblin Trouble
articles.content:
[h1]Foreword[/h1]
What follows is based on the Goblin Trouble module by Janek Sielicki (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign. It should also be noted that the original was for beginning characters and DMs so was scaled up for the campaign.

[h1]Introduction[/h1]
Goblin Trouble is a simple adventure that aims to introduce you into the world of Dungeons and Dragons. It is the first part of the upcoming Dark Master’s Return series. It has been designed for four 1st or 2nd level characters, but naturally it can be played with more or less people, which will make it less or more challenging. On average, it takes about four hours to play. Don’t be scared by the adven-ture’s apparent length! Half of the 80 pages are character sheets and maps and there is not that much actual reading.

[h2]Adventure Summary[/h2]
The party arrives to a small village located deep in a forest. The villagers ask them to help get rid of a group of vile goblins, which recently appeared at a nearby clearing. After a fight with a small pack of creatures, the adventurers discover the entrance to an old dungeon, where more goblins lurk. Using their might, magic, and cunning, the party explores the dungeon and finally deals with the goblin boss. However, during the adventure, it turns out that goblins allied themselves with kobolds and there is an even more sinister force in the Green Forest.

[h2]Difficulty Level[/h2]
The adventure has been designed and tested with groups of four players. If your party consists of five people, the adventure will be a bit easier for everybody. With six player characters, you might want to slightly adjust the number of foes: 
[ul][li]in the encounters with goblins, add 1 extra goblin
[li]add another spider in the spider cave (Scene 5, Area 3)
[li]Increase Grug’s hit points to 40 (Scene 5, Area 6; see page 53, Grug will have 40 hit points instead of 30). 
[/ul]

The adventure is quite difficult for three player characters and very difficult for two. In that case, reduce the number of foes in each combat encounter by 1 and halve the spiders’, Grug’s, and Toother’s hit points. If you’re playing with kids (and even seven year olds have fun with D&D—this adventure has been tested with such a group), consider increasing each PC’s hit points by 5-10.

Although Goblin Trouble is purposefully simple and straightforward, advanced players already familiar with D&D can also enjoy it. In most Scenes you’ll find a paragraph with ideas for advanced players, which make combat encounters more difficult or add extra options and ideas. Even if you’re a beginner group, try using some of them during play!

[h1]Adventure Background, or What It’s All About[/h1]
Three centuries ago a secret war ravaged the Green Forest. A nomadic tribe of Wood Elves fought a powerful sorcerer named Bel’Quath. The real reason for the war has been forgotten, the elves left and Bel’Quath has never been heard of again.

Decades passed and humans began to settle in the forest. Trees grow on old battlefields and plants cover derelict towers and fortifications. One day, Pabinus Yardon, a young wizard driven to prove to his superiors that Bel’Quath was not a legend, arrived to the Green Forest. Pabinus found Bel’Quath’s burial site and discovered that the powerful sorcerer was still alive, but bound with elven magic and unable to leave his tower. However, Bel’Quath easily dominated Pabinus’s mind, making him into his puppet. At last he could carry out his plan and regain freedom.

But the elven spells were powerful and it’s not easy to unbind the weave of magic. The elves couldn’t kill or even get close enough to Bel’Quath, so with powerful rituals they made three magic statues of the sorcerer and placed them around the forest. The lines of magical energies flowing between them converged on Bel’Quath’s tower, draining most of his magic and imprisoning him forever. Now, Bel’Quath wants to find and destroy the statues. Powerless outside the tower, he uses Pabinus as his agent.

The forest is enormous, dark, and unexplored. Bel’Quath, acting through Pabinus, began with finding more allies. He visited different tribes of goblins, kobolds, or even more terrifying creatures and used threats or promises to make them work for him. While monsters are combing the forest searching for the statues, Pabinus pretends to be a harmless hermit and lives in Oakenville, keeping tabs on humans.


[h1]Quest Hooks for My Campaign[/h1]
Where this starts in the overall campaign can vary, with the following suggestions:

[ol]
[li]As the party is returning to Haven from some other quest, they are approached by some Oakenville citizens.
[li]Oakenville representatives are encountered outside the mayor's office (or Duke's castle) after having their pleas rejected.
[li]Dejected Oakenville reps approach the party as members of the watch and thus must be heros who might help.
[li] ...
[/ol]

However the party comes across them, the goal of the villagers is the same... to gain help in their fight against the goblins. 

[h2]Setting the Hook[/h2]
Mandyk Bor, Oakenville’s leader, approaches the party’s table. His wife, Sarah Bor, accompanies him, but she doesn’t say anything. Instead, she stares at her husband the same way a teacher looks at a pupil checking if they’re well-prepared for a presentation. 

[aloud]“Hello there my dears. My name is Mandyk Bor, head man of the village of Oakenville, and this is my wife Sarah. You look like tough and clever folk who can deal with any situation. And well, it is a sad fact that our village and surrounding forest has recently become a tad dangerous due to the goblins that keep harrassing us. Our boys and girls know how to handle an axe, but most of them still haven’t returned from timber floating. So we're in kind of a desperate situation. 

We’re not a rich village, but we can spare some gold for a reward.” Mandyk stammers, but his wife elbows him and he continues. “It’s about them goblins. I talked to the people and it turns out everyone has had trouble with them. Especially since we’ve started work at the new clearing. So, can you come help us by dealing with those nasty buggers? We’ll give you 100 gold coins!”[/aloud]

[b]Note:[/b] Turin's village was raided by goblins and it resulted in his father's death. He should be motivated to assist.

[h2]Further Information[/h2]
The following info can be alluded to upon meeting Mandyk or fully explained upon reaching Oakenville.

[b][i]Since when have you had trouble with goblins? Where did they come from?[/i][/b]
[ul][li]“Well, there are always around but not so many and not so organized. But this troubles now? I think it all started a few months ago. After that huge storm that fell a lot of trees.”[/li][/ul]

[b][i]Can we get some support from you?[/i][/b]
[ul][li]“We have families, children. We can fight, sure, but only if we really must.” [b]Note:[/b] on a successful Persuasion check (DC 15), the town headman (Mandyk Bor) will assign a village guard as a guide. [/li][/ul]

[b][i]What else can you tell us?[/i][/b]
[ul][li]“Rest at the inn and depart in the morning. Visit Marsha, our herbalist. She’ll give you a few healing potions—I feel you’ll need them."[/li][/ul]

[b][i]How many goblins are there?[/i][/b]
[ul][li]“Hard to say, but not an army for sure. Probably only a few left after you’ve killed so many with Yuri!”[/li][/ul]

[b][i]How far is the new clearing?[/i][/b]
[ul][li]“Three or four hours on foot.”[/li][/ul]

articles.title: Key: Howling in the Wood
articles.content:
[h1]Note[/h1]
[i]This has been modified version from the original adventure (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign. [/i]

[h1]Introduction[/h1]
If you wander the forest in the dark of night, you can hear all manner of creatures calling from deep within the trees. Under the wrong moon however, you may hear a howl that sounds a little too human. Nearby villagers know these uncanny calls signal a season of kidnappings, ambushes, and slayed livestock, unless they turn to adventurers to investigate and cleanse the foreboding thickets. This spooky forest 5e adventure is designed for one session of low level play in any fantasy setting.

[h2]Background[/h2]
[container:tablebox-40][b]Outline[/b][ol][li]Begin the Begin this quest by hearing The Howling Call in a remote village! [li]Investigate The Wood and explore the Ruined Lodge to find the source![li]By magic or blood, Silence The Howl to save the village![/ul][/container]Centuries ago, the elders say, a god of chaos and blood witnessed a werewolf pack hunting under the blood moon. The sanguine god was so pleased by the lycanthropes’ ferocity that they claimed the pack forever. Even today, werewolves descended from that pack or anyone who is infected with lycanthropy under the light of a blood moon is cursed with a permanent, hungry hybrid form. These sanguine werewolves grow insatiable under the red lunar eclipse, heralding their attacks with what frightened villagers call The Howling. It begins with many echoing bestial voices baying, then growling and shrieking, accompanied by a soft sobbing heard loudest in the morning light. The Howling brings misfortune and takes life.

One month ago, logging caused by the expansion of a nearby city began pushing a pack of sanguine werewolves toward remote villages. This untimely occurrence has left the villagers horrified and confused, as The Howling seems to have forsaken the moon. Each day someone’s cattle–or someone–goes missing. Flyers for guard jobs and patrols cover every signpost in the region, each one promising your heroes a meager reward, but collectively relaying that these people need your party’s help!

articles.title: Key: Mine Your Own Business
articles.content:
[h1]Foreword[/h1]
What follows is taked from the Mine Your Own Business module from Robert Musci-Kurinyecz and Gergely Sepsi (full credits below). It has been changed to fit the world and campaign. 

[h1]About this module [/h1]
This module can be told as a standalone adventure or can be inserted in any campaign with a mediaeval style, high magic setting.

[aloud]Text that appears in a box like this is meant to be read aloud or paraphrased for the players when their characters first arrive at a location or under a specific circumstance, as described in the text.[/aloud]

You can play this adventure with just the Dungeons & Dragons basic rules found in the System Reference Document (SRD), which contains some of the monsters and NPCs making their appearance in this module. When the name of a creature is in bold, then its stat block will be in the SRD or in appendix A if the text tells you so. Spells and items are in italic, and are also described in the SRD or in appendix B respectively if noted.

[h1]Background Timeline[/h1] 
The following events lead up to the current time when the party arrives.

[ul]
[li]@[Theresia Terion](person:260afdeb-8364-482d-82d4-7a2463a0f1c8), wife of mayor @[Gingar Terion](person:e2bf9ecd-728e-40df-a3d4-4cdf227b24ad), wanted to climb the social ladder and warned her hubby that she'd leave him if he didn't sort out their money issues.

[li]When a gold seam was discovered in the local coal mine, Teresia saw an opportunity and insisted on additional workers and guards  be hired to exploit the [s]workers[/s] seam. 

[li]@[Theresia](person:260afdeb-8364-482d-82d4-7a2463a0f1c8) also made a deal with Pardin to oversee operations.  He was recommended by one of @[Theresia's](person:260afdeb-8364-482d-82d4-7a2463a0f1c8) tea partners from Brighton, as a person who could sort out problems with the "peasants".  He also breeds exotic 'animals' (more like rabid monsters) for fun and profit.

[li]Something happened in the mine, leading to the death of Icah, one of their number. They  have been chased away from the mine.  Plus, @[Theresia](person:260afdeb-8364-482d-82d4-7a2463a0f1c8) has lost contact with Pardin. 

[li]The workers and the sellswords are all afraid to enter the mine without the all-clear message from Pardin. 

[li]Mayor @[Gingar](person:e2bf9ecd-728e-40df-a3d4-4cdf227b24ad) locks the mine to ensure whatever is inside doesn't escape

[li]The reality is that Pardin had spent his entire life dealing with beasts, but the last monster egg he bought had no connection to the animal kingdom. Pardin, who relied on his extensive experience and magical items to tame beasts, was outmatched. The creature turned on its owner, unleashing havoc in the mine. This rampage led to the tragic death of Icah, one of the miners.

[li]The party arrives in town and desperate local miners scrape together their last coins to plead with the party to rid the mine of the scary monster. The miners are eager to resume digging for gold and reclaim their fortunes.

[li]As the party accepts the task, the mayor intervenes, refusing to hand over the key to the mine. Fearful of the possible escape of the lurking monster, the mayor insists on keeping the mine sealed.
[/ul]

articles.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn
articles.content:
[h1]Note[/h1]
This has been modified version from the original adventure (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign.  

[h1]Overview[/h1]
[container:tablebox-80]As you seek shelter from lashing rain and fierce winds, you arrive at the Old Wolf Inn.

However, a dead body has just been discovered at the roadside inn. But the guest 
doesn’t appear to have died of natural causes. In fact, all evidence indicates foul play – 
and suggests that someone at the inn is a killer. 

As the raging storm draws closer, you must solve the mystery before the murderer 
strikes again.

[i]Welcome to an evening at the Old Wolf Inn, where a web of mysteries, hidden clues, 
and secret intrigues await.[/i][/container]

[h2]about this adventure[/h2]
Murder at the Old Wolf Inn is an adventure for the world’s greatest roleplaying game (5e). It is designed for a group of three to five 2nd-level characters, but includes scalable encounters to suit any level from 1 to 20. 

This mystery adventure is inspired by the author and queen of crime Agatha Christie. The adventure can be used in any campaign or as an intriguing side quest. Some locations and events mean that the adventure works particularly well as a part of the Eastern Farraway Adventures campaign, but the adventure can easily be adapted to other settings.

[h2]featured content[/h2] 
[ul]
[li]An investigation mystery adventure inspired by Agatha Christie![br]
[li]Nine NPCs (including the unfortunate victim) with background descriptions, agendas, and secrets.[br]
[li]A detailed map of the tavern the Old Wolf Inn with 22 rooms to search for clues.[br]
[li]Open sandbox adventure.[br]
[li]New monster: Stenbumling troll.[br]
[li]New magic item: Zacki Buttercup’s Extraordinary Bathtub.[br]
[li]Legend & Lore: The Gate at Oxgate.[br]
[li]Original full-colour illustrations and maps.[br]
[/ul]

[h2]additional downloads [/h2]
Maps, VTT tokens, handouts, and pre-made characters for the adventure can be [url:http://midnight-tower.com/additional-downloads-matowi/ |tab]downloaded here[/url].

[h2]synopsis [/h2]
The characters are travelling along a trade road when a sudden storm forces them to seek shelter at a nearby inn. On their way, the characters encounter a stenbumling troll (a new monster for this adventure) that has been roused from its hibernation. When the characters have dealt with the creature, they arrive at the Old Wolf Inn at dusk.

However, a dead body has just been found at the rustic inn. Since anyone among the current guests can be the murderer, @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12), a Winterhold Guard, asks the characters for their help. 

The characters must identify the murderer before  the storm passes, or the guests will leave, and the culprit might never be found. 

[h3]optional: a non-violent finale[/h3]
This adventure can be played as a murder mystery without the need for physical conflict. In this scenario, if the correct suspect is accused of the murder, they grudgingly admit their crime. The guards then take them to Winterhold, where they will face justice. 

[h2]how to run this adventure[/h2]
This mystery is a classic investigation adventure with an open sandbox-style structure. The characters can interview the NPCs and search the tavern for clues. However, not all NPCs are willing to let strangers go through their rooms and belongings. 

There are plenty of revealing details and clues to be found, but the characters must decide which are solid leads, false information, or simply red herrings. If the characters get stuck, the DM can nudge them back on track by letting an NPC share a vital piece of information. 

The DM is encouraged to create a sense of unease by reminding the characters that while the inn is warm and cosy, they are trapped there because of the storm – and one of its friendly guests is a callous murderer! 

However, the most important part of the adventure is to have fun while trying to solve the mystery. 

[container:tablebox-80][center][b]Adventure Flowchart[/b][/center][img:6459482|nolink][/container]

[h1]Before the Adventure Begins[/h1]
The Old Wolf Inn is a rustic roadside inn owned and managed by @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), a retired dwarven adventurer. It is situated near Oxgate, a market town located on the border between the North Star region and Buckwild. The old trade road outside the inn leads to Stenur Keep and connects the towns of Winterhold and Redbridge. 

Oxgate is located at the end of a valley and surrounded by mountainous regions such as Wyver’s Hill and Craig Top. The steep, rugged mountains are home to gnolls, orcs, and goblins. The area is also often foggy, which makes passing through it even more perilous. 

[h2]markets and tolls[/h2]
Oxgate has been a market town for many centuries. During the weekend before the spring equinox, it hosts a livestock market where sheep, goats, pigs, cattle, poultry and horses of all breeds and temperaments are sold. There is also an autumn harvest market where crops, salted meat and seeds are for sale. In addition, there is a small crafting midwinter market that mainly attracts locals, as the roads close at first snow. 

The valley that cradles Oxgate is divided by an ancient 15-foot-tall stone wall that was once constructed for military purposes. A pair of massive double doors in solid iron are fitted into the wall’s arched gate, and regional lore claims that twelve strong oxen were required to open and close the doors, which gave rise to the town’s name. Nowadays, the doors are always open, but the Winterhold Guards collect tolls and taxes at the gate due to its practical location.

[h2]smuggling and contraband[/h2]
Of course, there are those who trade in goods of a dubious nature or simply dislike paying taxes and toll fees. Such merchants often hire bandits or unscrupulous rangers to guide them through the mountains. Others opt to transport their goods through the tunnels underneath the mountains, but this is a hazardous venture. Many of the tunnels were created long ago by a purple worm, and while they make for easy travel, it is easy to lose one’s way. What’s worse, beasts and monsters alike hunt the tunnels for prey – and the drow sometimes use them for their nightly raids. 

[h2]a questionable innkeeper[/h2]
The innkeeper at the Old Wolf Inn, @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), isn’t crooked at heart, but she struggles to make ends meet. She retired from adventuring several years ago, bought a tumbledown tavern, and spent all her coin on renovating it. Due to the uneven flow of customers and long winters, Inga sometimes earns some extra coin by storing contraband in a secret basement room. 

Some of the inn’s current guests are aware of Inga’s questionable dealings. @[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) is a former smuggler and knows about the inn’s hideaway, and @[Vittra Northdaughter](person:9f616252-c433-4d59-95dc-6f43de86d1e0) has seen smugglers delivering goods at the inn at night. 

[h2]guards on alert[/h2]
The dark elves’ raids near Winterhold have recently increased, so the roads are patrolled by guards who search for signs of drow ambushes, as well as highwaymen, smugglers, and other illicit activity.

Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f), @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) and @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) from the Winterhold Guards have been scouting the area around Oxgate. Kara is second in command, while Jon is a new but ambitious recruit. 

The soldiers patrol in pairs, and during a recent patrol, Artemis and Jon found an abandoned carriage peppered with black-feathered crossbow bolts. To Jon’s growing exasperation, Artemis also discovered footprints that led to a nearby cave, which showed signs of having been used by the drow for storing loot and captives abducted on raids.

Artemis realised that the cave would be a perfect location for the Winterhold Guards to ambush the drow. He decided to immediately inform his superior, Captain Osil Twoaxe, about his findings.

[h2]murder, he wrote[/h2]
However, @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) already knew about the cave. He has been supplying the drow with information recently to help them elude the patrols. His plan is to rise in rank by double-crossing the drow and revealing their operations when the time is right.

When Artemis stumbled upon the cave, Jon knew that his scheme was in jeopardy, and he callously decided to kill Artemis. His first murder attempt was to place burdocks under the saddle of Artemis’ horse and cause a riding accident. But while Artemis did fall off his horse, he suffered only a broken rib. 

Jon decided to bide his time and wait for a better opportunity – and he found it at the Old Wolf Inn.

[h2]the death of @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f)[/h2]
Upon arriving at the inn, Jon purchased a healing potion from the merchant @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc). He carefully removed the label from the healing potion and attached it to a vial of poison that he had confiscated during an earlier mission. 

Later that evening, Jon gave the false healing potion to Artemis, who was in pain because of his broken ribs. However, Jon’s visit was noticed by the guest @[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d), who saw Jon enter Artemis’ room.

When Jon left, Artemis drank the potion without realising that it was poison, and died within an hour. Later, Jon returned to Artemis’ room and took the pages from the report that Artemis had begun writing. 

[h2]red herrings and fishy affairs[/h2]
Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) had a tendency to take full advantage of the hospitality that roadside inns and taverns are legally required to offer patrolling soldiers. Although @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529) always treats the Winterhold Guards courteously, she has become weary of offering them free meals and accommodation, especially as her finances are strained. She also worries that they somehow will find out that she collaborates with smugglers.

Therefore, during the evening before Artemis was murdered, Inga sprinkled a generous dose of ground frog leaves onto Artemis’ dinner. Her plan was to make Artemis feel unwell and cause him to think that he had eaten spoiled food, which in the future would make him avoid the inn.

However, Inga is unaware that Artemis was poisoned by Jon later that evening, so she believes that she murdered him by mistake. She is also concerned that the hermit, @[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c), who gathered the frog leaves, will accidentally tell someone about it.

category.title: Module: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: Key: Night of the Living Dolls
articles.content:
[h1]Foreword[/h1]
What follows is based on the Night of the Living Dolls module from Penny Blood Adventures (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign. 

[h1]Introduction[/h1]
"Night of the Living Dolls" is a horror-themed one-shot adventure for Dungeons & Dragons 5th Edition, designed 
for characters of levels 3–5. This adventure challenges players to uncover the grim secrets hidden within an 
abandoned toy maker’s shop where eerie, lifelike dolls have begun to roam the streets at night. What once were 
innocent toys now hunt the townsfolk with malicious intent, driven by a dark force tied to the toy maker’s tragic 
past. 

The players will investigate the cursed shop, solve puzzles, and face off against the Cursed Doll, a vengeful spirit 
inhabiting a life-sized doll created by the toy maker. With its mix of tension-filled exploration, unsettling encounters, 
and intense combat, "Night of the Living Dolls" delivers a balance of mystery and horror, perfect for Dungeon 
Masters looking to immerse their players in a creepy, atmospheric setting. 

[h1]Adventure Hook [/h1]
The adventure begins when a local constable, Jorran, approaches the party with a dire plea for help. Townsfolk 
have been reporting terrifying encounters with "living dolls" wandering the streets at night. The constable explains 
that these dolls originated from the toy maker’s shop, a once-beloved establishment that has been abandoned since 
the toy maker mysteriously vanished weeks ago. With no one else to turn to, Jorran urges the party to investigate the 
toy shop and put an end to the sinister magic animating the dolls. What lies within the toy shop is more than just 
mischievous toys—it is a twisted legacy of grief, loss, and dark magic that threatens to haunt the entire town if left 
unchecked. 

[h1]Adventure Structure[/h1]
[h2]Act 1: Introduction & Exploration[/h2]
The players arrive in the darkened streets outside the toy shop as the last light of dusk fades. The eerie silence is 
broken only by the faint sound of giggling and scurrying footsteps as animated dolls leap from the shadows and 
attack. This first encounter sets the stage for the adventure, establishing the shop’s foreboding atmosphere and the 
immediate danger posed by the cursed dolls. After defeating the initial wave of dolls, the players enter the toy shop, 
where their investigation truly begins. 

[h2]Act 2: The Challenge or Conflict[/h2]
Inside the shop, the players will find themselves in a creepy, dust-filled space packed with dolls, marionettes, and 
mechanical toys—all seemingly frozen in place. But as they explore further, they’ll uncover the toy maker’s dark 
experiments in a hidden basement workshop. The players must solve a puzzle to unlock a chest containing a key 
artifact tied to the curse. However, failure triggers a trap that springs the marionettes to life, forcing the players to 
defend themselves. As they piece together the tragic story of the toy maker and his daughter, they will come face to 
face with the true source of the curse. 

[h2]Act 3: The Climax & Resolution[/h2]
In the heart of the toy shop’s hidden workshop, the players confront the Cursed Doll, a malevolent entity inhabited 
by the spirit of the toy maker’s daughter. The players must use their wits and might to defeat her in a final battle as 
she animates more dolls and toys to defend herself. The key to victory lies in a cursed mirror found in the toy shop
—an artifact that can trap souls, including the spirit animating the Cursed Doll. The players must find a way to free 
the girl’s soul and bring peace to the toy maker’s tormented legacy. If successful, the curse is lifted, and the 
remaining dolls collapse, lifeless once more. But if they fail, the curse will continue to haunt the town, and the 
players may become the next victims. 

[h1]Conclusion[/h1]
"Night of the Living Dolls" is an adventure built on creeping dread, atmospheric tension, and the eerie horror of 
everyday objects brought to life. The players will be pushed to solve puzzles, explore cursed environments, and face 
intense combat encounters as they work to break the curse animating the toy shop’s dolls. With the potential for rich 
roleplay and a powerful emotional story tied to the toy maker’s loss, this one-shot offers DMs a mix of mystery and 
horror that will keep players on the edge of their seats from start to finish.

articles.title: Key: Sins of the Past
articles.excerpt: Key for the Sins of the Past module
articles.content:
[b][i][center]This is a modified version of the [url:https://www.dmsguild.com/product/326297/Sins-of-the-Past]Sins of the Past[/url] adventure (full credits, below). It has been changed to fit the world of Antheros and the Haven Irregulars campaign (and to adjust some grammar that bothered me). One major change is the family name of the crypt owners, from Phylund to Geronde. [/center][/i][/b]


[h1]Introduction[/h1]
[quote]“Few brave souls dare to visit the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4) at night. Some might say that tales of nighttime horrors are for children, but the wise folk know better.”
-- Sir @[Ambrose Everdawn](person:2bd8d765-8490-49b4-95ea-f78d816bddec) [/quote]

[h1]Background[/h1]
Generations ago the @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87) was known in Haven as fearless and experienced monster hunters who always succeeded in their job. Their fame started with the famous knight and founder of the noble house - Ekhan Geronde, who slew a red dragon. 

Approximately 100 years ago, Ekhan’s grandson, @[Nesan Geronde](person:f5ad9047-5b50-4cba-87d3-65b000969493), decided to capture a hag coven. He planned to interrogate the hags, kill them and harvest for body parts. He and his party managed to capture the coven and kill two of the hags. One, however, managed to escape. Her name was Ursula. Furious that she lost her sisters, she cursed Nesan and his bloodline - he and his heirs started to manifest as ghosts after death. Ursula knew the family’s pride, so she set a tricky condition to break the curse: to spread the word about it.

To maintain the family's prestige, Gerondes claimed that all of the coven was destroyed. They also placed a Mirror of Life Trapping inside their mausoleum. In addition to the normal functioning of such a mirror,  this one has a special property such that any undead creature facing it immediately becomes trapped. Thus, the ghost-manifestation problem was solved.

Nesan became the first ghost who fell to mirror’s magic and over the years, the mirror has almost hit its limit. With the most recent burrial, there are now 11 ghosts trapped.

Recently, @[The Jackals](organization:723f61da-04ae-4811-ab6b-93821210d4e3), a gang of grave robbers, broke into the Geronde mausoleum in the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4). Two of them immediately fell victim to the mirror’s magic, which allowed Nesan to escape from his cage. Insane after hundreds of years of imprisonment, he slew other three gang members who tried to run, and now guards the family mausoleum, fixated on keeping the secret of the curse.

[h1]Overview[/h1]
Sins of the Past takes place in the city of Haven.  This adventure is designed for a party of 4-5 3rd-level characters and takes approximately 3-4 hours to play. It can be summarized to the following points: 

[ul]
[li]@[The Jackals](organization:723f61da-04ae-4811-ab6b-93821210d4e3), a band of five grave robbers, broke into the @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87) mausoleum and discovered a secret room with an activated mirror. Two of them immediately become trapped in it, and Nesan freed from his cage. 
[li]After Nesan was released from the mirror, he quickly escaped through the walls and slayed the remaining members of @[The Jackals](organization:723f61da-04ae-4811-ab6b-93821210d4e3) gang who tryed to run. He destroyed their jaws, making [i][b]Speak with the Dead[/b][/i] spell impossible to use on them.
[li]Since this event is more than the normal City Watch is prepared to handle, the Haven Irregulars have been called in. Their task is to find the cause of these strange deaths and eliminate a potential threat to the cemetery.
[li]After investigating the graveyard and having a brief encounter with dead gang members who have risen as vicious will-o-wisps, characters find their way into the mausoleum. 
[li]Once inside, characters are searching for the ghost’s whereabouts and eventually face the Mirror of Life Trapping, and learn about Geronde’s family dark secrets. 
[li]Characters either defeat the ghost by trapping it back into the mirror or find through Geronde’s notes details about the hag’s curse and use this information to free all ghosts and lift the family curse.
[/ul]

[h1]City of the Dead[/h1]
@[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4) is the largest public park in Haven. It features crafty sculptures, astounding mausoleums, beautiful trees and bushes, and curly walkways that allow visitors to walk and see all of its splendors. Havenites abandoned the practice of burying their dead long ago, using mausoleums instead of graves. The richest and largest of Haven’s families have extradimensional parts in their mausoleums, which they use for burials.
 
@[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4) looks most astounding at night, when hundreds of driftglobes, which usually illuminate the city, return and gather here.

[h1]House Geronde[/h1]
Details of the @[Geronde Family](organization:f19cd890-5d54-44cd-b4bf-d301b974af87) can be read elsewhere but are summarized here.
 
[ul]
[li]A noble family from Haven, originating from the city's founding. 
[li]It was founded by the famous dragon slayer Ekhan Geronde approximately 5 generations ago. 
[li]Use to specialize in the many uses of monsters, they capture and sell monsters both as pets and guard beasts. Those that couldn't be tamed are harvested for every part of their body, from their skin and bones to their flesh and organs.  
[li]Eventually the family fell upon hard times and lost most of its greatness until Lady @[Rowena Geronde](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650) became its matriarch.
[li]The @[Geronde Family Residence](landmark:aaa37936-24d9-4028-897c-f461d6e7d60c) estate can be found in @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b).
[li]@[Rowena](person:c44f3336-7f83-4e89-a5c8-78896cc3f650) is not in Haven when events of this adventure occur - she left the city for an unknown purpose. Her children are in Haven but not likely to get involved in any of this, deferring to Rowena.
[/ul]

[h1]Running the Adventure[/h1]
To run this adventure, you need the D&D fifth edition core rulebooks: Player's Handbook, Dungeon Master's Guide, and Monster Manual. 

Magic items mentioned in this adventure are described in appendix @[A. Magic Items](article:d18c9a26-1808-4ae1-92fe-4b52a5dc48e8). Stat blocks for creatures that can be found in this adventure are listed in appendix @[B. Monster Stats](article:5bf5b7b3-f86b-4cd5-b3b5-4f21eb1b18d2). Important information about adventure's main NPCs can be found in appendix @[C. NPCs](article:4e9bc44c-a6cc-4d35-869c-4fc47f369a9c).

[h2]Option: Using Tasha's Cauldron of Everything[/h2]
"Haunted", from Supernatural Regions in Tasha's Cauldron of Everything suits this adventure perfectly. If you want to include it, you can roll on this table any time the characters would encounter ghostly manifestations. This table is provided in appendix @[D. Supernatual Regions](article:cdaf27a1-daa8-489c-a94c-84fa9416c517) for convenience. But be mindful, doing so will increase the overall difficulty of the adventure.

articles.title: Key: The Girls are Missing
articles.content:
This isn't part of a specific module but rather a series of events that may or may not be related to each other.  They interweave in time with the all disappearances taking place between 10th of Wealsun and the 12th.  Unless you want to include @[Byelaya Tainley](person:fc9ce341-d93f-4d7f-a194-022a4e7ece22) , in which case it goes back further - to the 24th of Coldeven.  (c.f. Timeline page: [timeline:78d3e073-ae19-48f6-9ddf-6e390a1bf009])

[h1]Olivia and Seraphina[/h1]
@[Olivia Chronosmith](person:17bc78d2-213b-481c-8a3b-f2dc64dafe23) and @[Seraphina Steamspark](person:16390999-39e0-4310-913a-308aec509a5d) were kidnapped on 10th of Wealsun by @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) as a way to gain the tower's secrets.

They are being held in a cave used for Hand slave smuggling. Marita is also there. The cave can be accessed via @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d).  

[s][mark]Need to find a clue  pointing to this shop - use @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) as per the scenario??   Or possibly @[Georgio Iffleren](person:915f58ec-b95d-4fdc-8d11-f8c6ca8ff445) ??[/mark][/s]

[b]Related plot(s):[/b] @[Clockwork Tower](plot:5dab46dc-b84f-4f3f-b884-45a98b0ac6a6)  and @[Silks and Slavery](plot:cc3bdf6f-e133-4777-8c05-ee2f93753738).

[h1]Marita[/h1]
@[Marita Maggia](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37) was taken by @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) on 10th of Wealsun at the request of @[Mikyl Swiftsword](person:403bf324-8208-4b8f-98d1-8e98ded30ba5). 

This is an attempt to discredit @[Lyza's](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) new command - once it comes out the Irreguars were working to get Marita married it will look like a kidnapping by them.

She is being held in a cave used for Hand slave smuggling. Olivia and Seraphina are also there. The cave can be accessed via @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d). 

[mark]Need to find a clue  pointing to this shop - use @[Lucille Rilossa](person:ce375714-67ed-4fad-810b-bc3b0eaf0fad) as per the scenario??   Or possibly @[Georgio Iffleren](person:915f58ec-b95d-4fdc-8d11-f8c6ca8ff445) ??[/mark]

[b]Related plot(s):[/b] @[The Secret Ceremony](plot:93731b17-b5e9-4347-81a9-304763198ad5) and @[Silks and Slavery](plot:cc3bdf6f-e133-4777-8c05-ee2f93753738).

[h1]Byelaya (Bianca) Tainley[/h1]
@[Byelaya Tainley](person:fc9ce341-d93f-4d7f-a194-022a4e7ece22) left home on 24th of Coldeven and becomes the courtesan @[Bianca](person:e63156eb-0dbc-4d4f-acf6-7a26e6af346f), working at @[The Blue Lantern](landmark:c39a381b-8d84-4ac7-aa93-3f280a7f2dba) on @[The Street of Silk Veils](settlement:587a7a3a-ba45-4bfd-94bc-fe6e17ae023c).

She is quite happy in her new role and doesn't want to leave it. She has sent letters home saying she is safe and happy and until recently, her parents have resisted searching for her. They were of the belief that she should be allowed to "sow her wild oats" but are now regretting the decision to not send her for fostering like her brother.

Her mother Stella  is of the opinion that the family let Byelaya be, but her father wants to at least know where she is and the life she now leads. He might approach the party asking for help on this.

A couple days ago (9th of Wealsun) she was spotted by @[Cortina de la Riis](person:bc9967fb-4969-46fd-94cf-0294e48ffb27).

[b]Related plot(s):[/b] @[The Nobleman's Daughter](plot:8ff4636a-e1d3-4037-a18a-6acf9f42ad74)

[h1]Jillie[/h1]
@[Martin Plowhearth's](person:ae72d940-a17e-4fbf-8e37-b8beaf2a0ac5) girlfriend @[Jillie Ferndew](person:6f0f4057-ddb7-4306-a3df-72a4e1964b51) has disappeared but the reason is not sinister. After getting off work on the 11th of Wealsun she went home where she found her mother quite ill. She was distracted by looking after her and forgot to let anyone know.

[b]Related plot(s):[/b] None

articles.title: Key: The Hag, the Bear and the Goblins
articles.content:
[h1]Foreword[/h1]
What follows is taked from the Mine Your Own Business module from Robert Musci-Kurinyecz and Gergely Sepsi (full credits below). It has been changed to fit the world and campaign. 

[h1]Summary[/h1]
In the village of @[Oakridge](settlement:7ea6d7eb-ee01-4ebb-9e88-226dca2f1246), trouble is brewing as Thomas, the farmer's children have fallen seriously ill. Desperation sets in as no remedy seems to work, except for one whispered solution - the Hag living deep within the nearby woods. The adventurers must venture on a journey to negotiate with the Hag, whose cure is the only hope for the sick boys. However, dealing with the unpredictable creature will be challenging. As the adventurers unravel the layers of this one-shot adventure, they will discover that there's more to the situation than initially meets the eye.

[i]The Goblin's Prank Gone Wrong[/i] is a one-shot 5e compatible adventure designed for 4-6 level 5 players, offering 5-12 hours of gameplay.

[h1]About this Module[/h1]
This module can be told as a standalone adventure or can be inserted in any campaign with a mediaeval style, high magic setting.

[h1]Hooks[/h1]
A farmer sought their aid, presenting an opportunity for a genuine act of kindness.

The village mayor offers a fully covered stay in the local inn (as long as they want to stay there), along with 15 gold coins, as a gesture of appreciation.

For those still hesitant, the mayor offers two health potions acquired from a past encounter with a hag – a potent gift to address any concerns.

For my purposes, the events will unfold upon the party's return from the mine in @[Mine Your Own Business](plot:ebca0f33-09a4-4b39-85a0-992137e2152c)
