articles.title: Lions 3 – Gladiators 0
articles.content:
[h2]Players' Information[/h2]
The news floating around the @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb) is that @[Watt Mikekins](person:8aa2e90d-d46b-462c-ba3b-eae22d922254) is in the market for a couple of big cats, to stage a spectacular combat. He is usually available at @[The Cock and Bull](landmark:a92c80a2-8982-4475-904a-1963de9ba84f) and is indeed willing to pay up to 2,500gp each for as many as three big cats (lions, tigers, etc.) delivered to his tavern.

[h2]GM's Notes[/h2]
The nearest site for big cats is the Wold, the large forest area some two hundred miles west and slightly north of Haven. The area is populated by elves, who may have something to say about others invading their forest, particularly if they wander about slaughtering wildlife. Watt, of course, will pay only for goods delivered.

articles.title: MatOWI: Appendix
articles.content:
[h1]timeline[/h1]
[h2]previously[/h2]
[ul]
[li] The Winterhold Guards frequently patrol the region, as the drow have recently increased their nightly raids. 
[br][br]
[li] Artemis and Jon were patrolling and found tracks of drow. They followed the tracks and discovered a cave in which the drow store captives and stolen goods. Artemis realised the site would be perfect for an ambush. 
[br][br]
[li] However, Jon is collaborating with the drow, so he decided to kill Artemis to keep the cave secret. He first staged a riding accident, but Artemis only broke a few ribs when he fell off his horse. 
[br][br]
[li] Artemis, Jon and Kara travelled to the Old Wolf Inn, where Artemis planned to write a report about the cave to his superior. 
[/ul]

[h2]the night of the murder[/h2]
[ul]
[li] Upon arriving at the inn, Artemis demanded the best rooms as well as free meals. The innkeeper, Inga, is fed up with being used by the guards, and she dislikes their visits, as she is involved in illegal smuggling activities. She asked Enoch to collect frog leaves, a plant that can upset stomachs, and sprinkled them on Artemis’ dinner in the hope that he would become nauseated and avoid the inn in the future. 
[br][br]
[li] During the evening, Artemis grew troubled by his injuries from the riding accident, but he tried to mask his discomfort. He turned pale and left the common room early after instructing everyone that he needs privacy for writing an important report. 
[br][br]
[li] William arrived at the inn and saw the guards through the windows and decided to hide in the stable. His whereabouts was noticed by Vittra, who believed that he is a poor vagabond.
[br][br]
[li] Jon bought a Potion of Healing from Hugo, carefully removed its label, and applied it to a vial of poison that he had acquired earlier. 
[br][br]
[li] Artemis began to feel the effects of the frog leaves and ordered a mug of tea with peppermint and fennel root from Inga. 
[br][br]
[li] Hugo saw Inga deliver the tea to Artemis. 
[br][br]
[li] At midnight, Cael quietly left his room to make sure that everyone was a sleep so that he could carry out his nightly unholy Ceremony unnoticed. He saw Jon quietly knock on Artemis’ door and give him a potion, and noticed that Artemis appeared grateful.
[br][br]
[li] Cael performed his midnight ceremony. Although he kept his voice down, his activities were noticed by Vittra, who was outside to check her traps. Hugo, who is a light sleeper, also heard Cael. Inga heard a door open and close, and hushed singing, but fell asleep again and is unsure whether it was a dream. 
[br][br]
[li] Artemis drank the potion, not knowing that it was a poison, and died within an hour. 
[br][br]
[li] Later that night, Jon went downstairs, took the key to Artemis’ room from the locker by the bar, and entered Artemis’ room. After making sure that Artemis was dead, Jon removed the pages from Artemis’ report that concerned the drow and their cave. He then locked the door, put the key back in the locker, and returned to his room, where he burned the pages.
[/ul]

[h2]the next day[/h2]
[ul]
[li] The following day, the storm began to develop. Kara and Jon didn’t knock on Artemis’ door, as Artemis had requested privacy. However, when most of the day had passed with no sign of Artemis, they decided to check on him. When Artemis didn’t open the door, they asked Inga to unlock the door, and his body was discovered.
[/ul]

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Arriving at the Inn
articles.content:
[aloud]
The wind howls, and lightning bolts spar with each other across the darkening sky. The heavy rain blurs your vision, but further ahead you see the welcoming sight of a warm, roadside inn.
[/aloud]

Built three centuries ago to withstand the cold weather and harsh wind of the mountains, the Old Wolf Inn is a two-storey building in stone with tarred exposed oak beams and white chalk cladding. The stables are located in an adjacent stone building, and there is a small outhouse around the corner of the inn.

As the characters venture closer, they see a carved wooden inn sign that swings madly on its chains. The sign features a ragged wolf with bared fangs and the carved text “Old Wolf Inn”. Rain gushes down from the inn’s thick thatched roof. The ferocious winds howl and threaten to rip off the outhouse’s roof at any moment.

[h2]a warm but worrying welcome [/h2]
When the characters enter, they find themselves in a warm and cosy common room. They are met by the scents of newly baked bread, strong ale, and spiced tea. The whitewashed walls are decorated with old woodworking tools and mounted stag antlers. To the left is a bar, and a massive wolf pelt hangs over the large open fireplace to the right.

[h2]the innkeeper[/h2]
A dwarven woman with braided hair stands behind the bar. She flinches when the door to the inn opens, and looks wide-eyed at the characters. 

[aloud]“Greetings,” the dwarf says in an unsteady tone and rubs her palms against her apron. “I am @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), the innkeeper.” 

There is a long pause before Inga clears her voice and continues: “You are, of course, welcome to stay for the night. But I’m afraid that there has been an accident…” 

She trails off and takes a deep breath. “You see, a dead body has just been found here at the inn.”[/aloud]

Inga offers the characters and the other guests a round of free drinks. She asks the characters if they wish to order food and lodgings for the night, before she disappears into the kitchen. 

[h2]a murder is announced[/h2]
Two of the guests are a pair of soldiers in the armour and cornflower-blue cloaks of the Winterhold Guards. They are discussing something in between them in low but agitated voices. After a moment, they leave their place in front of the fireplace and approach the characters.

[aloud]“My name is @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12),” one of them says, “and this is @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc). We’re soldiers from the Winterhold Guards. Our commander, Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f), has just been found dead in his room upstairs.”

“Foul play may have been involved,” Jon adds quietly, “but we don’t yet know for sure.”

“Nevertheless,” Kara continues bluntly, “it is possible that a murder has been committed. Would you be willing to look into the matter?” [/aloud]

[h2]the guest who came in from the cold[/h2]
Before the characters can reply, the heavy door to the inn is pulled open. In the doorway stands a towering, broad-shouldered woman dressed in rugged hide armour and backlit by stabs of crackling lightning. What is visible of her skin is covered with blue tattoos, and a dead deer is slung over her shoulders. She steps over the threshold, pauses to shake the rain from her clothes, and wordlessly strides into the kitchen.

The woman is @[Vittra Northdaughter](person:9f616252-c433-4d59-95dc-6f43de86d1e0), a regular visitor at the inn. On nights such as this one when the weather is exceptionally bad, she stays at the inn and pays for her lodgings by delivering fresh meat to Inga. 

[h2]promised rewards[/h2] 
@[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) offers the characters a reward of 50 gp to investigate if Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) was murdered and, if that is the case, provide evidence of who committed the crime. 

Kara emphasises that because the characters weren’t present when the lieutenant died, they are above suspicion – but anyone else at the inn might have been involved. 

[h1]the guests at the inn[/h1]
The following NPCs are present at the inn. (See chapter 5 for complete descriptions of all NPCs.)

[b]@[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529)[/b], a female dwarven innkeeper who walks with a slight limp around the tables as she serves drinks to her guests. 

[b]@[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c)[/b], an elderly man with wild white hair and matching eyebrows who stands in a corner. He is dressed in a tattered dark green robe and worn sturdy leather boots, and his expression is distant and absent-minded. At present, he is engrossed in a mumbled conversation with a large black bird that sits on his shoulder. The black bird is a tame @[Raven](person:394a6d30-211a-42e3-acae-cdbcb9754456) named Crow.

[b]@[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12)[/b] and [b]@[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc)[/b], a female and a male human in their thirties. They are dressed in military uniforms and cornflower-blue cloaks embroidered with the emblem of the Winterhold Guards. Kara has short black hair and a small scar that runs across her eyebrows, while Jon is handsome and has brown side-swept hair. They both look concerned and watchful. 

[b]@[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d)[/b], a male half-elf who sits at one of the round tables with an aloof expression while he sips tea from a wooden mug. He keeps his long auburn hair tied back, and he wears a fine sky-blue robe with silver moon crescents and an elegant pointy velvet hat. 

[b]@[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc)[/b], a constantly smiling and fidgeting male human merchant. He is short and chubby with thinning black hair and a ruddy face, and he wears golden rings on all his fingers. He sits in a private booth near the fireplace and nervously dabs his forehead with a monogrammed handkerchief.

[b]@[Vittra Northdaughter](person:9f616252-c433-4d59-95dc-6f43de86d1e0)[/b], a tall female half-orc. She has long, braided hair and a stony face that is difficult to read. Her body is covered with intricate tattoos, which suggest that she originates from the Hinterlands region. She is currently in the kitchen, where she is swiftly and skilfully butchering the dead deer.

[b]@[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a)[/b], a male gnome who poses as a travelling bard. He has a radiantly charming smile and wears a black-and-red chequered cloak. He is currently hiding in the stables since he saw the Winterhold Guards through a window when he arrived and instantly decided against entering the common room. 

[h2]first impressions[/h2]
[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Wisdom (Insight):[/b] You look around and inconspicuously study the innkeeper and the other guests. Judging by Inga’s tendency to wipe her hands and offer free drinks, and the merchant’s considerable perspiration, they appear to be nervous. In contrast, the half-elven mage looks remarkably calm, although his eyes are alert. The two guards seem tense and worried, but it is harder to gauge the mood of the elderly man with the bushy hair – either he is actually talking to his tame bird, or he is an extraordinary actor. The brawny woman who carried the slain deer into the kitchen was impossible to read. 

[h1]the clue is in the menu[/h1]
If the characters order the house’s finest wine, they notice that the wine is from the Outer Islands. These types of wines are rare and expensive and quite uncommon to find at a rustic dwarven inn. 

Inga received the wine as part of her payment for hiding smuggling goods in the Hidden room (Area 19) in the basement.

[img:6459656|nolink]

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Interviewing the Suspects
articles.content:
A key part of figuring out who murdered @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) is gathering useful information by talking to the NPCs. Almost everyone at the Old Wolf Inn has something to hide – for one reason or another. 

The statistics of all the NPCs are listed in the core rulebooks, but the DM can modify these as they wish. 

The suspects are:

[row]
[col]
[ul]
[li]@[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d)

[li]@[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c)

[li]@[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc)

[li]@[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529)
[/ul]
[/col]
[col]
[ul]
[li]@[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc)

[li]@[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12)

[li]@[Vittra Northdaughter](person:9f616252-c433-4d59-95dc-6f43de86d1e0)

[li]@[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a)
[/ul]
[/col]
[/row]

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Mysterious Clues
articles.content:
When the characters continue their journey, the autumn weather steadily becomes worse. Light is fading rapidly, and dusk is approaching. 

[aloud]
You continue along the trade road for a few hours while clouds gather on the horizon, and the wind slowly picks up. 

You walk past a wooden fingerpost that informs you of the direction of Buckbridge, Oxgate and Stenur Keep. One of the fingers points to the location of the tavern “The Old Wolf Inn,” which appears to be nearby.
[/aloud]

[h2]ominous signs[/h2]
As the characters continue forward, the character with the highest passive Perception score notices something that gleams on a tree near the road. 

If the characters investigate the item, they find a small crossbow bolt made from steel with sooted fletches that is deeply embedded in a slender winter birch. Characters who are familiar with dark elves know that it is the kind of crossbow bolt that is commonly used by the drow. 

There are no other signs or tracks of the drow, but a closer inspection of the ground reveals a few broken planks that look like they may have belonged to a carriage once.

[h2]hidden details[/h2]
Some details aren’t immediately obvious or easily found. These details are marked “--> On a closer look,” followed by the ability check and difficulty required to discover them.

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Rewards and Aftermath
articles.content:
If the characters succeed in solving 
the mystery at the Old Wolf Inn, Kara 
Tanner will reward them with 50 gp. 

In addition, @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529) gives 
them an additional 50 gp and a wooden barrel 
filled with her finest apple brandy. She adds that 
the characters are always welcome back to the 
Old Wolf Inn and never have to pay for their stay.

[h2]the guests chime in[/h2]
@[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) joins in the congratulations 
by composing a surprisingly good and catchy 
song about the whole incident. He titles the song 
“Who Killed the Guard in the Night – Can You 
Perchance Figure It Out?”, and over the following 
years, it becomes a rather popular drinking song. 
In addition, @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc) (if he is still alive) 
offers the characters a 20% discount on his current 
and future goods, should their paths cross again.

Finally, @[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c) gives the 
characters a silver spoon for “Bringing peace 
to the forest and my favourite inn.” The antique 
spoon (worth 10 gp) was recently brought to 
Enoch by his tame @[Raven](person:394a6d30-211a-42e3-acae-cdbcb9754456) Crow, who pinched it 
from a stenbumling troll’s treasure hidden deep in 
the forest. Crow isn’t overly pleased with Enoch’s 
generosity and will make a discreet attempt to 
steal the spoon back from the characters. 

[h1]epilogue[/h1]
The following years turn out to be prosperous for 
the Old Wolf Inn and @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529). 

After the ordeal with the murder and all the 
worry that came with storing contraband in her 
basement, Inga decided against working with 
the local smugglers again. However, during the 
night when the dead body was discovered, Inga 
offered all her guests free drinks, and many 
complemented her delicious and potent apple 
brandy. The apple brandy is made after a secret 
recipe that has been in her family for generations. 

With the help of a frequent customer and 
travelling brewer, Maltimar “Malte” Ashbeard, 
Inga decided to scale up the production to 
increase her earnings. 

The inn’s basement was repurposed as a 
brewery, including a large fermenter and a 
first-class distiller of gnomish design. Later 
she founded The Broadhammer Brewery, and 
its delicious brandy and ciders quickly became 
popular at many taverns in the region. 

[h2]the ghost at the old wolf inn[/h2]
It is said that once every year, on the same day 
that Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) was murdered, 
a ghostly apparition of an elven soldier can be 
seen at midnight. It appears in the Old Wolf Inn’s 
finest room, where it silently sits at the desk and 
writes a report.

The ghostly soldier has also made itself known on 
other occasions as a foreboding warning – especially 
on nights that the drow carry out raids nearby. 

[h1]aftermath[/h1]
The characters receive a group award of 1,350 XP 
for accomplishing the quest, plus additional XP 
for defeating monsters. Alternatively, at the DM’s 
discretion, this point serves as a milestone and 
the characters gain one level.

[h2]the journey continues [/h2]
When the characters are about to leave the inn, Inga 
waits by the entrance to bid them goodbye. She 
offers them a dozen freshly baked cinnamon buns 
wrapped in a kitchen towel to keep them warm.

[aloud]“Remember that you are always welcome 
back,” Inga says and smiles. “And hopefully, the 
next step of your journey will be less eventful.” 

You glance at the sky as you step outside. 
The dark clouds have passed, and the fierce 
wind has abated. The road and a bright and 
beautiful autumnal day lie before you.[/aloud]

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Rooms at the Old Wolf Inn
articles.content:
The Old Wolf Inn is a cosy countryside inn situated next to the trade route that leads to Oxgate. It is owned and managed by @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), who has spent considerable time renovating the shabby tavern, a task that included firmly relocating a family of skunks that had taken up residence under the floorboards in the common room. 

Inga’s dwarven heritage and taste can be seen all around the inn: The table lanterns are in traditional dwarven octagonal style, and most of the sturdy furniture tends to be low and wide.

While the storm rages outside, rain lashes against the windows, and the wind howls relentlessly. However, the inn is warm and cosy, with scents of freshly baked bread and long-cooking stews filling the air. 

Unless stated otherwise, the ceiling is seven feet on the ground floor and the top floor, and six feet in the basement. 

[h2]hunting for clues and evidence[/h2]
Inga allows the characters to search the common areas at the inn. However, many of the guests’ private rooms are locked, and the NPCs might not appreciate having their rooms and belongings examined. Therefore the characters might want to investigate the private rooms discreetly. 

[h2]shady operations[/h2]
The Old Wolf Inn is located near Oxgate, an old arched gate in weathered stone that dates back many centuries. This toll gate is manned by guards who collect taxes on the goods of travelling merchants before they are permitted to continue north. 

Oxgate is situated on the road in a narrow gorge. The surrounding mountains are often draped in heavy mist, easy to get lost in, and teeming with dangerous beasts. According to legend, the mountains also hide a portal to the Shadowfell whose magic is failing. It is said that strange monsters sometimes slip through to the material plane. However, so far, no one has been able to verify or disprove this hearsay.

More than one shady merchant with dubious morals has decided to risk circumnavigating Oxgate to avoid paying the toll and additional taxes. These merchants regularly make use of local smugglers. One of the smuggling bands is led by Emirys Shadowdusk, who operates in the area around Oxgate and uses the Old Wolf Inn to store contraband in a hidden room in the basement. 

[h1]ground floor[/h1] 
[h2|1]1. common room [/h2]
The rectangular common room is warm and welcoming. Sturdy round tables with comfortable horseshoe armchairs are placed haphazardly around the room. There are also several private booths with curtains and leather seats. Opposite the inn’s entrance are two staircases, which lead to the upstairs [url:#8]Landing (Area 8)[/url] and the [url:#20]Basement room (Area 20)[/url].

At one end of the room is a bar, and at the other is a sizable hearth with a crackling fire. Every now and then, the coal hisses as the stormy wind rushes through the chimney.

Near the bar is a written menu on the wall that lists the prices of food, drinks, lodgings and more. Inga keeps a heavy crossbow under the bar, but so far, she has never needed to use it.

[h3]room keys[/h3]
The keys to the upstairs guest rooms hang in a wooden locker behind the bar. It is closed, but not locked. Each guest has their own key, and Inga uses the spare keys when she is cleaning their rooms. Each key has a chain with a wooden tag that is engraved with the room number and a picture of a common forest-dwelling animal. 

Both keys to Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna's](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) room are missing. Inga carries one of them, while the other can be found in Artemis’ room. The room was locked when the body was found. 

[h2|2]2. kitchen [/h2]
On the worktops in the kitchen lie a roasted lamb’s leg, a string of sausages, and a cutting board with chopped rosemary twigs. Various herbs and ingredients are stored in white porcelain containers with labels in Dwarvish. Pots brimming with different stews simmer slowly on a massive iron-wrought stove. The room smells strongly of cooked vegetables, smoked meat, newly baked bread, and fresh herbs.

Inga spends considerable time in the kitchen as she prepares all the food for her guests. She keeps it clean and tidy, and she can tell the contents of all the jars by heart. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC13 Intelligence (Nature):[/b] As you look through the porcelain jars with herbs and dried mushrooms, you realise that some of them can be poisonous if they are consumed in large quantities.

[h2|3]3. scullery [/h2]
This room contains numerous caskets of ale, wooden crates with carrots, potatoes, and wrapped apples, as well as firewood and coal. A couple of shelves hold loaves of dried dwarven bread and jars with pickled onions and herring. The space smells of vinegar, ale, and damp timber. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] While you search the scullery, you lift the round mat on the floor on a whim. Underneath the mat is a wooden trapdoor with a pull ring handle in metal. 

The trapdoor opens to a narrow ladder that leads down to the [url:#18]Root Cellar (Area 18)[/url].

[h2|4]4. larder[/h2] 
The stone walls in this small room feature built-in wooden shelves that run from floor to ceiling. The shelves hold dozens of glass jars filled with preserves and jam, waxed-sealed cheeses, cured meat, and vegetables. The air is filled with different scents from the various goods stored here. 

Inga uses this space for storing foods that need low temperatures. The thick stone walls keep the room cool all year round.

[h2|5]5. outhouse [/h2]
A narrow path leads from the inn to a simple outhouse built in weathered grey timber. Its roof looks more recently constructed and is made of oak shingles. 

Inside the outhouse is a practical compost toilet with a wooden lid, a basket with hay and soft moss, and a bucket of clean water. Bundles of dried lavender and woodworm hang from the ceiling. 

The outhouse’s ceiling was recently replaced by Inga, but the furious storm is doing its best to rip it off.

[h1]the stables[/h1]
[h2|6]6. stables [/h2]
The stables is a stone building next to the inn. Its robust double doors are closed, but not locked. A pony trap stands in the courtyard just outside the entrance. 

Inside the stables are five stalls that are occupied by a large black warhorse, two sturdy military riding horses, an elegant grey mare with a silver mane, and a piebald pony. 

A rickety-looking ladder that leans against the wall leads up to a hayloft. There is a door to the left of the stable entrance with a sign that reads Tack Room. 

The warhorse belonged to @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f), and the military riding horses belong to @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) and @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc). All three are marked on their hindquarters with the symbol of Winterhold:  Two crossed pickaxes over a snowflake. The grey mare belongs to @[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d), and the piebald pony is owned by @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc). 

If the characters climb the ladder to the hayloft, they find an unrolled bedroll and a scruffy leather backpack that smells intensely of herbs. 

The bedroll and the backpack belong to @[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c). Inside the backpack are a mess kit, a red-brown feather, three rations of food, a waterskin, and a satchel full of various herbs. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Intelligence (Nature):[/b] As you go through the satchel of herbs, you find several herbs that are used for cooking: Thyme, winter peppercorn, wild garlic root, and a few frog leaves.

Enoch collected frog leaves earlier for Inga, which she used to make @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) nauseated. 

[h3]who is there? [/h3]
If one or more characters enter the stables, the character with the highest passive Perception hears noises from the tack room after a short while:

[aloud]You hear a low thump from behind the slightly open wooden door that leads to the tack room. The sound is followed by a pained yelp and muffled cursing. [/aloud]

The noises come from @[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a), who decided to hide in the tack room when he saw the characters approaching the stables. However, curiosity soon got the better of William. He tried to steal a glance through the partly open door from his hiding spot, but accidentally bashed his head against a wooden saddle holder. 

[h2|7]7. tack room [/h2]
This room contains a range of saddles, bridles, harnesses, blankets, and other equestrian gear. There is also a pile of old buckets, a couple of hay bales and sacks of grains. The space smells sharply of leather oil and hay.

[h3]the fast-talking would-be bard [/h3]
@[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) is currently trying to hide behind a sack of grain. If he suspects that his presence has been noticed, he addresses the characters in a cheerful manner. 

[aloud]“Ah, well! Hello there, fellow travellers.” A male gnome with a stylish haircut and a short, immaculately trimmed beard stands up behind a grain-filled sack. He wears traveller’s clothes, studded leather armour, and a fine black-and-red chequered cloak. 

“We’re having a dreadful spell of weather tonight, aren’t we?” The gnome continues without pause, “I was…just searching for some horse oil in here, as I have lost mine at the, eh, last inn where I was staying.”[/aloud]

@[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) proceeds to jovially present himself as William the Bard, a travelling minstrel from the south.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 8 Wisdom (Insight):[/b] You have a strong feeling that “William the Bard” isn’t telling the whole truth. In fact, every other word that passes his lips rings false!

[h1]top floor [/h1]
[h2|8]8. landing [/h2]
The landing is a larger room with a round oak table and four matching rustic chairs. There are also two wooden wardrobes and an oaken chest. 

The landing offers access to all the upstairs rooms. Each room has a carved sign with a number and a depiction of a forest animal. The wardrobes contain wicker baskets with wax candles, jars of oil, a broken lantern, and cleaning supplies. Inside the oak chest are extra bed linen and spare blankets. 

[h2|9]9. Hugo von Silverton’s room[/h2] 
This guest room is used by @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc). Its door (AC 15, HP 12) is locked, but the iron lock can be picked with a successful DC 13 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check.

The room is furnished with a wide wooden bed that is unmade, a round table with two chairs, and a washing stand with a mismatching pitcher and washbasin in porcelain. A large travel trunk in mahogany with studded leather bands stands along one of the walls. There is also a plain chest by the foot of the bed and an open chest by the door. Several colourful shirts have escaped from the open chest. A single candle burns on the writing desk near the window and fills the room with a weak, flickering light. 

The open chest is full of clothes that range from linen shirts in various colours, tight trousers, a couple of worn but nicely tailored coats and jackets, and a waxed rain cloak. There are also a few mundane objects: a fine comb, a toothbrush with a bottle of tooth-cleansing powder, and a small vial of floral-scented perfume.

The chest by the foot of the bed is unlocked and empty. 

The mahogany travel trunk is locked with a complicated steel lock that requires a successful DC 16 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] As you search the room, you find that the windows have been firmly jammed shut so that it is impossible to open them from the outside. Hugo has secured the windows, as he constantly worries that old enemies – or unhappy customers – may catch up with him one day. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 12 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] When you investigate the clothes in the open chest, you notice that one of the frock jackets has concealed sewn-in pockets along its hem. The pockets can be used to discreetly carry hidden items, such as valuable gems or small weapons.

[h3]trap[/h3]
The mahogany travel trunk is fitted with a hidden trap. If the trunk is opened without using the proper key, a dozen poisoned @[Tiny](person:f75ef7a2-2d1e-48f5-970d-ddf2a76c55a4) needles spring out from its lid. 

All creatures within 10 feet of the trunk take 1d4 + 1 piercing damage and must make a DC 14 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is also affected by a sleeping poison as if they were affected by the spell sleep. As the effect is caused by a poison, it affects creatures who have resistance or immunity to being Charmed.

The trap can be spotted by succeeding on a DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation) check and disarmed by succeeding on a DC 13 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check. A failed attempt to pick the lock or disarm it triggers the trap.

Hugo is well aware that using poison is highly illegal and likely to land him in trouble – or make him a suspect – if it is discovered at this particular time. This is one of the many reasons that Hugo is exceedingly nervous during the investigation.

[h3]the travel trunk[/h3]
The trunk contains an assortment of merchant’s goods, including several bolts of textiles, five bottles of wine, an hourglass, a long rod with a red crystal, several books in a wide range of subjects and fiction, 20 paper sheets, nine parchments, 30 feet of silk rope, a bag with a sealing wax and signet ring in the shape of three circles and an owl, seven individually wrapped soap bars that smell of roses, one leather bag, and four pouches.

At the bottom of the trunk is a dagger in a sheath with embossed climbing ivy. The dagger is engraved with the words “Duty” on one side and “Revenge” on the other side in Common.

The dagger is a [i]Dagger +1[/i].

The leather bag has several compartments with potions and contains two [i]Potions of Healing[/i], one [i]Potion of Greater Healing[/i], one [i]Oil of Slipperiness[/i], one [i]Poison of Sleep[/i], and one [i]Antitoxin[/i].

The four pouches contain a total of 54 gp, 120 sp, 34 cp, various types of gems (worth a total of 27 gp), and 18 different gold and silver rings (which could be worth a total of 360 gp). 

The rod with the crystal is a spellcasting focus. 

[i]--> On a closer look, DC 16 Intelligence (Investigation):[/i] As you study the rings more closely, you notice that they are unexpectedly light. You realise that the rings are fake and probably worth only around 1 sp each. 

[h3]key evidence[/h3]
If the characters have found the empty vial in @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f)’s room, they might compare it with the potions in the leather bag.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] You compare the empty vial with the bottles in the leather bag. The bottles are similar, but the written text, which reads [i]“Potion of Healing,”[/i] is the exact same. 

Jon bought a [i]Potion of Healing[/i] previously from Hugo, removed the label, and carefully stuck it on a bottle of poison that he gave to @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f). The bottles in the leather bag are from the same maker, and therefore the handwriting matches.

[h2|10]10. linen storage[/h2]
The small storage room contains built-in shelves with stacks of fresh towels and bed linen. On the bottom shelves is a range of cleaning equipment, including a scrubbing brush, a bag with salt and fine sand, a bucket, and a grey rag. There is also a small bar of soap.

The cupboard smells strongly of lavender and pine soap. 

[h2|11]11. unused guest room [/h2]
This guest room is currently vacant. It is chilly and rests in dim light. Occasionally, a flash of lightning or glimpse of @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) chases the shadows away before they rush back in. The room is furnished with two beds, a bedside table, and a sturdy wardrobe. There is also a small writing desk with a simple writing set for guests. 

The wardrobe is empty, and the stone fireplace has been swept clean. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 17 Intelligence (Investigation): [/b]You search the room and find that it is neat and tidy and appears to have been unused for some time. However, you notice something gleaming in the light between two floorboards. 

The item is a silver coin that has slipped between the planks some time ago. It can be fished out by succeeding on a DC 8 Dexterity check.

[h2|12]12. Lieutenant Artemis Nahanna’s room[/h2] 
This room was used by @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) before he met his death. It is furnished with a wide double bed, two bedside tables, a writing desk and a wardrobe in oak. See @[The Crime Scene](plot:2e1c1114-8754-44b7-ae69-dbb03e1089a4) for more details about the crime scene.

[h2|13]13. Kara Tanner’s room [/h2]
This is @[Kara Tanner's](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) room. The door is unlocked, as she has forgotten to lock it. 

The room is a general mess. A pair of dirty socks have been tossed into a corner. Near the unmade bed stands a bottle of apple brandy and a couple of greasy jugs in dwarven design. A rugged leather backpack and a bedroll have carelessly been thrown on the floor, and an oiled tent has been shoved under the bed. 

The writing desk is covered with papers. A short, wooden pen lies next to the documents. The pages on the table form the beginning of a report that Kara began to write when Artemis’ body was discovered. It has been rewritten multiple times with several false starts. On the floor lie several crumpled pages of earlier attempts. The handwriting is cramped and slightly childish. 

The backpack holds a range of equipment for outdoor activities, including a mess kit, a tinderbox, 30 feet of rope, a lantern with a flask of oil, five food rations, two torches, and a waterskin. It also includes a spare set of clothes and an oiled loak for bad weather. 

At the bottom of the backpack is a small wooden box. It is locked and requires a successful DC 14 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick.

Inside the box is a simple cloth bag and a rolled-up garotte made of a steel string and two wooden handles. The cloth bag contains a set of wooden dice, a deck of cards with dog-eared corners, and a rounded stone with a carved Dwarven rune. 

A closer inspection reveal that the dice are weighted prison dice, and that one of the cards is smeared with old, dried blood. Characters who can read Dwarfish also realise that the rune on the stone represents the letter L. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Intelligence (History):[/b] You recall that this type of carved stone is called Dwarven stones or Lovers’ stones. They are commonly exchanged between young couples as a symbol of a secret engagement, for example, if their parents disagree with the match. 

Dwarven characters or characters who have grown up among dwarves automatically pass this check. 

[h2|14]14. Jon Callion’s room [/h2]
This guest room is used by @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc). Its door (AC 15, HP 12) is locked. The wrought-iron lock requires a successful DC 13 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick.

The room is immaculately clean, and all items are flawlessly organised. The room is furnished with a narrow bed that is neatly made with its blankets folded to perfection. Next to the bed stands a bedside table, and by the foot of the bed is a large wooden chest. In one corner is a reading armchair, and a soldier’s backpack stands on the floor next to the entrance. There is also a writing desk with neatly arranged ink jars and a row of sharpened pens sorted by size. 

The room is lit by a crackling fire that radiates a pleasant warmth, which is in stark contrast to the dreadful weather outside. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] When you investigate the open fireplace, you find a small piece of partially scorched paper that has escaped the flames.

The partly burnt paper is the last remnant of the pages that Jon stole from @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f)’s report. There is no writing on the page, but if the characters compare the paper with the rest of the report in Artemis’ room, they find that the papers match. However, the same type of paper can be found on Jon’s writing desk and in @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12)’s room. 

The drawer in the bedside table contains a well-thumbed copy of [i]One Hundred Masterful Military Strategies and How to Counter Them: Volume Three[/i], written by Commander C. K. Rosewood. 

The chest contains a stack of neatly folded clothes organised by colour and type. Even the socks are placed in impeccable rows. On the other side of the chest are various items, including a bedroll, an empty map case, a set of fine clothes, a bar of soap, a shaving kit with a foldable comb, a waterskin, and a polishing kit. 

The backpack is empty, as Jon has meticulously unpacked everything for his stay. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] You discover a hidden pocket in the backpack. Inside the pocket is a fine golden ring (worth 10 gp) fitted with a hidden spike that can be folded in and out. 

This type of ring is often used by assassins. Jon confiscated the ring a few months ago from a group of highwaymen that he and a group of soldiers from Winterhold arrested. Since Jon realised that the ring could be useful in the future, he decided to keep it. 

[h2|15]15. Cael Thraelive’s room[/h2] 
This room is currently used by @[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d). Its door (AC 15, HP 12) is locked with a built-in lock, which requires a successful DC 13 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick.

The room is chilly and rests in complete darkness. It is furnished with a sturdy oaken bed, a chest, a bedside table, and a comfortable reading armchair. A faint smell of fragrant incense hangs in the air.

A pair of unwashed tea mugs stand on a wooden bedside table and have left blemishes on its top. Shelves on the wall hold assorted books for guests to read. Standing in front of the books on one of the shelves is a foldable traveller’s shrine that depicts a paladin riding a dragon. 

Inside the chest is a leather backpack. It contains a set of spare travelling clothes, cooking utensils, a small cauldron, a tinderbox, two days’ worth of food rations, and a waterskin engraved with a prancing deer. 

At the bottom of the backpack, there is a flat steel box roughly the size of a book. The box is decorated with bronze corner mounts and has a small keyhole in its side. 

The steel box is locked with a complicated gnomish lock that required a successful DC 18 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick. Any attempt to tamper with the lock might set off its trap (see below). 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 14 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] You poke around in the ash and charred logs in the fireplace and find a small, scorched piece of bone. It appears to be a chicken bone. The bone was used by Cael during his most recent unholy midnight ceremony. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 16 Intelligence (Investigation): [/b]You study the foldable travel shrine. It is a triptych made in gold-painted wood featuring a depiction of the god of duty riding to battle on his golden dragon.  The symbol of the deity, a metal gauntlet, graces the top of the shrine. However, as you turn the shrine around, you find a small, inconspicuous latch that secures a pair of hidden doors. If the inner doors are opened, they reveal an exquisitely and disturbingly detailed carving of a gory scene, which portrays the demonic god Baal in the process of enslaving the world and punishing his adversaries.

[h3]peculiar insights[/h3]
If the characters start to search the room, let everyone make a DC 14 Wisdom (Insight) check. Characters who succeed realise that although Cael claims to be a mage and a scholar, there is a distinct lack of books and tomes in the room except for the assorted books on the shelves, which clearly belong to the inn. 

[h3]trap[/h3]
The steel box in the backpack is trapped with an enchantment that strikes potential thieves or snooping busybodies with a lightning bolt. A creature that attempts to open the box or pick its lock without speaking the correct password out loud must make a DC 14 Dexterity saving throw, taking 2d8 lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. 

The trap also emits a mighty thunderclap that echoes through the room and temporarily deafens all creatures within 30 feet of the box for one round. The sound is audible to all guests inside the inn, who will immediately run to find out what happened. Everyone except for Cael will believe that the building was struck by lightning.The password is “I welcome death” uttered in Abyssal. The trap can be disarmed by the spell dispel magic or a similar spell that suppresses or negates magic.

The steel box is large enough to hold the foldable shrine. It also contains a worn prayer book dedicated to Baal, a pack of incense, and a flat engraved steel plate for ritual sacrifices.

[h2|16]16. unoccupied room [/h2]
This guest room is currently vacant. It is furnished with a sturdy bed in pale oak, a writing desk with a chair, and a massive wardrobe. On the bedside table stand two fresh wax candles that smell faintly of honey. A red oval rug with a simple yet decorative woven pattern covers most of the floor. 

The wardrobe is empty save for a layer of dust and a startled [b]spider[/b], which immediately scuttles away into hiding.

[h2|17]17. Inga Broadhammer’s room [/h2]
This is @[Inga Broadhammer's](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529) private quarters. She usually leaves the sturdy door (AC 15, HP 12) to her room unlocked, but she has decided to lock it because of recent events at the inn. The iron lock requires a successful DC 12 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check to pick.

The room is slightly cramped but comfortable and cosy. The furnishing consists of a dwarven-sized bed with a matching bedside table, a small sofa, a table, a large wardrobe, and a corner bookcase. There is also a cluttered writing desk that holds a large pile of letters, two quills, three smudged bottles of ink, a carved pipe, and a worn leather pouch filled with pipe-weed.

Standing on the mantelpiece is a collection of oval frames with artistic paintings of other dwarves, many of whom carry a strong resemblance to Inga. 

On the bedside table is a half-filled glass of water and a thick book with a brown leather bookmark slipped in between the pages.Several of the letters on the writing desk are written in Dwarvish. Characters who can read Dwarven runes realise that the letters seem to be correspondences with various family members and go back many years. However, a few letters appear to be addressed to Inga’s father. In the letters Inga defends her decision to leave the clan and seek adventure. These letters never seem to have been sent. 

The book on the bedside table is written in Dwarvish and features a collection of folktales. 

The wardrobe is packed with simple everyday shirts, dresses, skirts, and other clothes. Pairs of practical shoes are lined up on its floor. At the back of the wardrobe hangs a mended travelling cloak, a suit of studded leather armour, and a heavy warhammer. 

The corner bookshelf contains various books, most of which are in Dwarvish and feature classic heroic stories and folklore tales. There are also a few titles on cooking and herbalism. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] You open the book on the bedside table and find that it is inscribed with the following words: “To my oldest daughter. May you always follow your heart. From your father, Runalf Broadhammer.” 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 12 Wisdom (Perception):[/b] As you study the warhammer and the armour in the wardrobe more closely, you notice that the weapon is covered with cobwebs, and the studded leather armour is dry and cracked. Neither of the items can have been used for many years.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 19 Intelligence (Investigation):[/b] When you pull out a couple of the books from the bookcase, you notice that one of them is hollow and features a concealed compartment. Inside the book is a small leather drawstring bag with a label that reads [u][i]“For emergencies only”[/i][/u]. The bag contains 100 gp and a note with what appears to be a secret recipe for how to make apple brandy. 

You also find that one of the cooking books has been used frequently. As you flick through it, you find a dog-eared page where the following text has been underlined: “Frog leaves can be used as a delicious and flavourous addition to stews and broths. However, they must be used sparsely, as they are poisonous, and overconsumption might lead to an upset stomach.”

The gold is the last of the money from Inga’s adventuring days, and the apple brandy recipe is a closely guarded family secret. The section about frog leaves in the cooking book prompted Inga to use these for poisoning @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f)’s food, in the hope that he would avoid staying at the Old Wolf Inn in the future. 

[h1]basement[/h1]
[h2|18]18. root cellar[/h2]
This room can be accessed by a narrow ladder that leads down from the [url:#3]Scullery (Area 3)[/url].

Numerous crates that contain root vegetables, apples, and pears are stored in this room together with half a dozen wooden barrels that hold apple brandy and various ales. The basement room also contains a small woodworking bench. The walls are made of crudely cut stone blocks, and the floor consists of packed dirt. The chilly air smells of earth and damp stone. 

This room is used for storing goods and food used at the inn. The workbench is used by Inga for repairs and crafting wooden furniture. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Wisdom (Perception):[/b] As you look around the basement, it strikes you as odd that there are no crates or barrels stored near the western wall. You also notice a small puddle on the floor, which has formed from water that drips from a crack in the wall. Your gaze traces the outline of the crack, and you realise that it forms a rectangular shape –rather similar to a low door.

The concealed door is an expertly crafted pivot door. It can be opened by pushing a specific area on one of its sides. The door opens to the [url:#19]Hidden room (Area 19).[/url] 

[h2|19]19. hidden room[/h2]
The ceiling in this narrow room reaches only three feet. Inside the room are several crates, a couple of chests, and an empty wooden barrel. 

The crates and chests contain various high-quality merchant’s goods: Bottles of fine wine from distant regions carefully packed in layers of hay and wood shavings, iron ingots stamped with Hammersong’s sigil, bolts of fine textiles, wrapped furs, and elegant glassware for alchemy purposes. 

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Wisdom (Perception): [/b]You notice that the wine bottles are labelled with the letters O.I.

The label stands for Outer Islands. The bottles are similar to those found at the glade where the stenbumling troll clashed with the smugglers. 

This room was originally built long ago as a secret space for the inhabitants to hide in if the inn was attacked by marauding monsters or overrun by bandits. It is currently being used by @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), who helps local smugglers hide their contraband before it is moved through the mountains to avoid the toll at Oxgate. 

[h2|20]20. basement room [/h2]
This room can be accessed by the stairs that lead down from the [url:#1]Common room (Area 1)[/url].

The room is simple with stone walls and a well-scrubbed floor. It is furnished with a wardrobe and a cleaning closet. The air smells of pine and drying laundry.

The wardrobe contains fresh soap and clean fluffy towels for guests using the [url:#21]Bath (Area 21)[/url]. The cleaning closet contains two brooms, three buckets, a mop, a brush, a dustpan, and a jug with homemade soft soap.

The smell from the drying laundry comes from the nearby [url:#22]Laundry room (Area 22)[/url].

[h2|21]21. bath [/h2]
A majestic bathtub in gleaming copper with golden griffon’s claws for feet immediately steals the attention of those who enter this room. In front of the bath lies a welcoming plush round rug. There is also a changing area with a hanging sheet that offers privacy, a rack for hanging clothes, and a shelf with stacks of clean towels, bars of soap, and a small selection of scrubbing brushes with long and short handles. 

The bath can be used by guests for a small fee. The bathtub is a magic item called Zacki Buttercup’s Magnificent Bathtub, and it can bestow a range of different magical effects upon creatures who bathe in it. See chapter 11 for more information about this wondrous item.

[h2|22]22. laundry room [/h2]
This room contains a long wooden tub filled with water, diluted lye, and pine soap. On a table are several washboards and scrubs. A rack of shelves holds clean and ironed sheets, towels, and customers’ clothes. The air smells strongly of fresh pine soap and drying laundry.

Inga uses this room for laundering the inn’s bed linen, tablecloths, the clothes of her guests, and other items. The wooden tub is used for soaking particularly dirty textiles.

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Solving the Murder
articles.content:
To identify who killed Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f), the characters must figure out which guest has the strongest motive and how the murder was carried out, all while avoiding getting sidetracked by red herrings. 

[h2]interviewing witnesses [/h2]
Talking to the guests will give the characters a general idea of what is going on at the inn and might reveal people’s motives for killing Artemis. 

However, not everyone will share all their thoughts and feelings – especially if it might get 
them in trouble. Therefore, the characters may have to persuade, trick, or intimidate some NPCs into parting with certain information. 

[h2]looking for evidence[/h2]
The innkeeper, @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529), permits the characters to search all the common rooms. However, the characters must be stealthy or create a distraction to covertly go through the NPCs’ private rooms. 

[h2]red herrings and misdirection[/h2]
There are plenty of red herrings that can lead the characters astray. For example, Inga believes that she unwittingly killed Artemis by adding frog leaves to his food. 

Another red herring is the local smuggling activities near the inn. As Inga stores contraband in the basement and @[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) used to be part of a band of smugglers, their involvement can make them look particularly suspicious. 

[h1]developments[/h1]
When the characters arrive at the Old Wolf Inn, all the NPCs except @[William Fastfinger](person:771bf737-b659-4f26-a97a-0f27bc24a44a) are gathered in the common room. However, the guests might not stay there at all times. As the evening progresses, the guests withdraw to their rooms and go to bed. Eventually, the only sounds heard at the inn are the howling wind and the rattling of the chains of the inn’s sign, which has been torn away by the storm. 

[h2]destination unknown[/h2]
At some point during the night, @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) decides to leave the inn during the night and ride hard for Oxgate despite the storm. She worries that the murderer will remain unidentified and wants to call in reinforcements before it is too late. If the characters see her slip off to the stables and confront her, she will tell them about her plan.

[h2]murder is easy[/h2]
@[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) has poisoned @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc)’s wine with an inconspicuous ingestion poison, which Hugo will drink in his room around midnight to soothe his frazzled nerves. Hugo will die one hour after consuming the wine unless the poison is neutralised by magic or an antidote. Characters who are awake at this time and succeed on a DC 12 Wisdom (Perception) check will hear heavy breathing from Hugo’s room as he crawls for a vial of antitoxin in his luggage. 

[h2]the key to unveiling the murderer[/h2]
Two pieces of information are central to solving the mystery. 

One is the empty vial in Artemis’ room. If the characters find the set of potions in @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc)’s room, they might realise that while the writing and the labels are similar, the bottles are different. This indicates that someone has changed the label on the empty potion. 

Another is that several pages of Artemis’ reports are missing. These pages were stolen by Jon, who tried to burn them, and the scorched remains can be found in his fireplace. 

Furthermore, @[Cael Thraelive](person:b095a26a-f846-434d-988f-ab226b5ea93d) saw Jon knock on Artemis’ door and give him a potion. Cael is delighted by the nefarious murder and won’t readily share what he knows – but he may be compelled or tricked into doing so. 

[h2]time is running out[/h2]
At dawn when the storm is moving on, the NPCs will gather in the common room, and the characters must present their findings. 

At this point, there are several alternative endings to the adventure depending on the characters’ actions. (See chapter 8 for more information.)

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: The Crime Scene
articles.content:
The late Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f)’s 
room (Area 12) is located upstairs. The 
room is the finest room at the Old Wolf 
Inn, and the fireplace is larger than 
in all the other guest rooms. (See @[MatOWI: Rooms at the Old Wolf Inn](plot:36024920-71db-4e58-b0f5-15657b63ef73) for a 
map of the Old Wolf Inn.)

The door (AC 15, HP 12) to the room is locked 
with a sturdy iron lock. The lock requires a 
successful DC 13 Dexterity (Thieves’ Tools) check 
to pick.

Inga has the key to this room and will unlock 
the door if the characters ask her. She will also 
tell them that the door was locked when they 
discovered the dead body, and that no one has 
touched the body.

[aloud]As you enter the fine guest room, a shiver 
dances down your spine. 

Your eyes are drawn to the unnaturally still 
shape of a male elf, who lies face down on 
the wide double bed. There are no immediate 
signs of struggle or violence.

The room is dark, and the air is chilly. 
Outside, the wind howls like an angry wolf, 
and cascades of rain hammer without 
remorse on the roof.

The walls in this guest room are painted sage 
green and feature a golden-painted picture rail. 
Two empty candlestick holders, made of brushed 
iron and wrought in traditional hexagonal 
dwarven style, are fitted on one wall. A large chest 
stands near a writing desk. 
On either side of the 
bed are two bedside tables. On one of them is a 
wooden mug. [/aloud]

[h2]investigating the body[/h2]
Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) is clearly dead. His 
skin is discoloured, and his face is a ghastly mix 
of grey and blue hues. He wears a Winterhold 
Guards’ uniform but no armour. In his pockets are 
a key and a leather drawstring pouch. The key has 
a wooden tag engraved with the number 3 and a 
curious-looking badger. 

The wooden tag matches the sign on the door to 
this room, and the key fits the lock to the door. The 
drawstring pouch contains 17 gp, 3 sp, and 1 cp.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 13 Wisdom (Medicine): [/b]
As you investigate the body more closely, you find 
that the victim appears to have died of unnatural 
causes. Due to the discolouring of the deceased’s 
skin, it is hard to establish the true cause of death, 
but he appears to have been either poisoned or 
strangled. You also notice several greenish bruises 
along the victim’s ribcage, which looks like the 
result of one or two broken ribs. 

The discolouration of Artemis’ face is a result 
of the poison that he drank before he died. The 
bruises stem from when Artemis fell off his horse 
during Jon’s first murder attempt. 

[h1]searching the crime scene [/h1]
This room hides numerous clues that are vital to 
the investigation, so the characters will do well to 
investigate it thoroughly.

[h2]looking around the room[/h2]
[b]--> On a closer look, DC 8 Wis (Perception):[/b] You 
notice that one of the windows is slightly ajar. 

The window was opened by Inga after she 
discovered the body. According to a common 
tradition in Eastern Farraway, opening a window 
in a room where a person has passed away can 
help their soul depart to the afterlife. 

[h2]searching the fireplace[/h2]
[b]--> On a closer look, DC 12 Intelligence 
(Investigation):[/b] As you poke through the cold and 
charred logs in the open fireplace, you notice that 
some of the ash flakes resemble torn sheets of 
paper. On a few of the pieces are faint traces of 
unreadable handwriting.

The burnt pieces of paper are the first draft of 
the report that Artemis was working on during 
previous evening. Artemis later wrote the full 
report, but the pages were later stolen by Jon 
Callion after his death. 

[h2]investigating the bedside tables[/h2]
In one of the drawers is a small wooden box. 
Inside the box is a golden locket with an 
exquisitely painted miniature portrait of a female 
half-elf woman with long, silvery hair in a noble 
gown. The locket rests on a small deep purple 
velvet cushion.

The painted picture is a portrait of Artemis’ 
sister, Thanelïa Nahanna.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 13 Wisdom (Survival) 
or Intelligence (Nature):[/b] You find that the wooden 
mug on the bedside table is empty, but you notice 
the faint scents of peppermint and fennel root 
upon it.

The mug contained peppermint and fennel tea 
with honey that Artemis ordered from Inga after 
dinner when the frog leaves started to affect him 
and he felt queasy.

[h2]looking through the chest[/h2]
The chest contains Artemis’ well-polished armour 
and weapons, as well as a selection of clean and 
neatly folded clothes. There is also a bag of dirty 
laundry, a shaving kit, a steel mirror, a shoe brush 
set, and a few other mundane items.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 10 Intelligence 
(Investigation):[/b] You find an empty glass vial 
labelled “Potion of Healing” that has slid down 
between the clothes. You also discover a uniform 
jacket with mud stains in the laundry bag.

The empty vial was a bottle of poison, but Jon 
changed the labels and gave the vial to Artemis, 
who drank the lethal poison in the belief that it 
was a healing potion. There are no traces of the 
poison left. 

The uniform jacket was worn by Artemis during his 
riding accident when he fell into a puddle of mud.

[h2]investigating the writing desk[/h2]
The wax candles in the candlestick holders on the 
elegant writing desk have burnt down to stumps. 
On the desk is an elegant letter opener in silver, 
a pair of quills, a small jar of black ink, and a @[Tiny](person:f75ef7a2-2d1e-48f5-970d-ddf2a76c55a4) 
pile of shavings that appears to be the result of 
someone sharpening the quills more than once. 

The desk’s drawer contains several papers 
with a report written in Common. The report 
is unfinished and concerns a recent military 
scouting mission conducted by Artemis. The 
handwriting is initially neat, but it degrades 
drastically towards the end of the text. It is 
impossible to tell if this is because the writer was 
tired or feeling unwell.

[b]--> On a closer look, DC 15 Intelligence 
(Investigation):[/b] As you skim the report, you realise 
that several pages seem to be missing.

The report was written by Artemis. It contained 
detailed information about his discovery of the cave 
that the drow use, which is the reason behind why 
Jon decided to kill him. After the murder, Jon stole 
the pages with the most sensitive information.

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: The Storm is Gathering
articles.content:
The characters are currently on the road near the market town Oxgate, which is located on the border between Buckwild and the North Star region. It is early autumn, the air is crisp, and the sky is clear and azure blue. 

[h2]a change in the weather [/h2]
Have all characters roll a DC 10 Wisdom (Survival) check. Characters who succeed notice the following: Although the autumnal skies are clear, you notice that blackbirds and sparrows are flying low, and there is a stillness to the air that is uncommon at this time of day. It is quite possible that the weather soon will take a turn for the worse. 

Rangers and druids have advantage on this check. 

[h2]musicians on the move[/h2]
[aloud]
You are following the old trade road towards Oxgate. On the way, you have met several merchants who are travelling to Buckbridge village for its yearly Hunter’s Full Moon Festival, a three-day-long fair. 

You pass a hilly area where the summer’s wildfires have scorched the ground. Among the blackened tree stumps, fire lilies in bright orange and burning yellow bloom in the ash.

Suddenly, you hear the sound of singing from farther ahead.
[/aloud]

The choral voices belong to a group of musicians who call themselves the Travelling Band of Bards. Their leader is Gerda “Starstruck” Timberman, a female gnome with braided hair who wears colourful clothes in green, purple, and saffron yellow. 

The musicians have just lit a fire and sat down for an afternoon meal at a resting place next to the road, where three large logs have been dragged near a campfire. 

If the characters approach them, Gerda merrily invites them to join them and share their food.

[h2]gossip and cautions[/h2]
If the characters join the musicians, Gerda and her friends ask them who they are and where they are going. She explains that her band are bound for Buckbridge, but they plan to stay in Redbridge for the winter. 

Gerda adds in a hushed voice that the characters may want to be careful on their travels. She has spied gnolls, drow and other shady individuals in the forests at night, and she recommends that the characters keep their eyes open for trouble. 

[h2]light-fingered companions[/h2]
When the characters have left the musicians, one random character realises that something among their belongings is missing. Roll 1d6 to determine what is gone:

[ul]
[li]On a result of 1–2: A practical item, such as a bedroll, a travel ration, or a mess kit.
[li]On a result of 3–4: 1d20 + 5 silver pieces  (or the nearest amount).
[li]On a result of 5–6: A small item such as a trinket, an inkwell, or a bar of soap.
[/ul]

The missing item was stolen by one of the bards who doubles as an opportunistic thief. No weapons, armour, spell foci, or more valuable items have been stolen.

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: MatOWI: Unfolding the Mystery
articles.content:
At dawn, everyone has gathered in the common room. The storm has begun to move on, but the inn still creaks in the blustery winds. 

If @[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) managed to slip away during the night, she has now returned together with two soldiers from the Winterhold Guards. 

The characters are asked by Kara if they believe that Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f) was murdered. She also asks them if they have found any evidence and if they know who killed him, in that case. 

[h2]the innkeeper’s confession[/h2]
However, before the characters can reply, @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529) suddenly breaks down. 

[aloud]“Wait!” @[Inga Broadhammer](person:6a5722d6-2bbf-4a07-9afb-156765e90529) says, in an unsteady voice. “Before you continue, I…I have something to admit.” 

She draws a long, ragged breath. “It was I. I murdered the lieutenant.”

The guests gasp in unison. 

“I poisoned him,” Inga says. “But I never meant to kill him – please, it is true. You must believe me!”

She buries her face in her hands. 

The other guests look at Inga in stunned silence after her tearful confession. [/aloud]

Only @[Enoch the Birdwhisperer](person:0569934d-5392-4cc1-92bd-26db16be105c) doesn’t appear to be entirely surprised by Inga’s revelation. 

[h2]if the characters can’t provide any contrary evidence or make the real murderer confess:[/h2]
@[Kara Tanner](person:385a552b-d3be-41f1-b5ce-442506c56a12) and @[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) will arrest Inga and take her to Winterhold to face justice, and the adventure ends.

Jon will later double-cross the drow and reveal their cave to his superiors. However, the drow never truly trusted him, and Jon is eventually found dead in the woods with a poisoned dart buried in his neck. 

[h2]if the characters point out Jon Callion as the murderer:[/h2]
@[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) will pretend to be both surprised and offended to be accused of the murder. However, if he is pressed with questions that he can’t answer, or if the characters have gathered compelling evidence, he will snap and admit that he poisoned Artemis. Jon’s handsome face is contorted by an ugly sneer. 

[aloud]“Fine,” he hisses between gritted teeth. “I admit I murdered the lieutenant. He was pompous and not fit to lead a pack of street dogs, let alone someone like me.” 

“But why?” Kara says, clearly taken aback. “No one liked the man, but that’s no reason to kill him!” 

“During our scouting mission, we found a cave that the drow frequently use. He would have blabbed about it to the captain. I simply had to stop him.” Jon scoffs and glares at the characters. “And if these idiots hadn’t shown up, I would have gotten away with the murder.”[/aloud]

With that, Jon draws his sword but before he can attack . . .

[aloud]An ice-cold and fierce wind sweeps through the common room. It is so strong that it topples chairs and knocks over several of the table lanterns. The crackling fire at the hearth is snuffed out. Darkness descends, and shadows fill the room while a hoarse voice echoes in the air.

“Justice?” the chilling voice whispers mockingly. 

“You mortals think you know the meaning of the word, but you have no idea. I have come from the grey mists beyond the veil to show you.” There is a pause. “I shall have my bittersweet revenge and punish the guilty – they shall not be given the opportunity to grow old or have time to regret neither slyness nor cruelty.”

Dragging footsteps are heard from outside the inn. With a mighty crash, the sturdy oak door to the inn is thrown wide open and reveals a hideous creature.[/aloud]

The creature is the late Lieutenant @[Artemis Nahanna](person:7db3308e-7226-434c-bc3b-fa5cc253c00f), who has risen from the dead to avenge his death. He has the statistics of a wight and will fight anyone who stands in his way.

[b]Notes on the fight:[/b]
[ul][li]Due to the extinguished lanterns, the [mark]common room is now dimly lit[/mark].
[li]Inga will reach for her heavy crossbow behind the bar, but she will [mark]only use it to defend herself[/mark]. 
[li]If given a chance, [mark]@[Jon Callion](person:6676f148-1ca6-49f5-89e7-fec9882ffefc) will attempt to sneak away[/mark] while everyone is distracted by the wight.
[/ul]


[h1]the final evidence [/h1]
If the characters search Jon’s person, they find two bottles of [i]drow poison[/i] and one [i]Potion of Healing[/i] without a label. The glass bottle matches the [i]healing potions[/i] that can be found in @[Hugo von Silverton](person:384f255e-519e-45ba-8e94-c5f2d08e35fc)’s room. There is also an empty poison bottle that Jon used to poison Hugo’s wine.

articleParent.title: Key: Murder at the Old Wolf Inn

articles.title: Meeting the Young Stallions
articles.content:
The conflict between the upstart @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c) and the major guild families presents many opportunities for players to interact with major NPC's from the @[Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b). In addition to the possibility of meeting the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c) in their normal haunts - @[The Golden Ducat](landmark:6c10e646-893f-4767-b911-e0cfeae3ea04) or @[The Aerie](landmark:f24b8234-a53d-47df-84dd-26adc048a141) - they may be encountered at various times in any section of the city. 

If there is a dispute between workers and management that causes a temporary work stoppage, or a public protest about backed-up sewers or crime in the streets, it’s a good bet that one or more of the Stallions will be somewhere on the scene, adding their voice to the outcry (it's also likely that one or two members of the city guard will be on hand, keeping an eye on the situation). The Stallions exert their strongest influence on events in @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954), on @[Trade Island](settlement:eec2fc60-5565-40fa-9047-69924589c400) (where the Guildmasters have recently stepped up their efforts to collect licenses and tax fees from the vendors), and in parts of the @[Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be) section. Their support is weakest around the docks (where merchant traffic has increased greatly under Guild rule) and on the @[Street of Harps](settlement:6053011d-37a6-4d6b-8e0b-41925f3bbd77), although one can find a few staunch royalists even in these areas.

There are three primary ways in which a player may become directly involved with the Stallions, described below.

[h2]Purchasing Land[/h2]
At any given time, there is a 60% chance that one of the noble families, beset by debts, will be looking to sell some of their country property (located 3d10 miles from the city gates). If the buyer is not of noble blood, and the Stallions get wind of the transaction, they will try to stop the sale by pressuring both parties. Including, perhaps a late night visit en masse to the room of the prospective purchaser. A player character may also become involved in a land-buying effort as an anonymous agent for @[Heidel Varrasch](person:e28b24da-4d76-4538-8315-6a13ac6449a3) (see @[Varrasch Family](organization:3947f0eb-ed12-44f7-a3f0-f04678bec261) description).

[h2]Accepting Guild Hospitality[/h2]
If a traveling player-character with an artisan background chances to meet a member of a prominent Guildsman's household, and the two get along together well, it is not uncommon for the latter to offer the player the hospitality of his home for the duration of the player's stay in the city. @[Anson Blackbourne](person:681f57c3-a3f7-4a8d-a44a-34b06fbbf06d) and @[Portia Maggia](person:6b5a47fe-05ef-415d-82e8-3fc6f63fa142) are two examples of Guild NPC's likely to extend such an invitation. 

If the player and Guild family member begin to appear together in public frequently, there is a 35% chance that the @[Young Stallions](organization:7340b23d-2c0c-45fd-8222-13b00aa2760c) will decide to teach the newcomer his place, by putting the player character in a position where he must fight or face public humiliation. @[Paul de la Riis](person:61db5c85-e436-459e-926e-17ad7e116b03) is a master at engineering and manipulating such situations, many of which are the product of elaborate preparations - a purse left lying in the player's path, and then claimed to be stolen. A bumping incident in a crowded barroom, etc. 

The Stallions will always be willing to carry a confrontation to its violent conclusion if the player character does not yield. All of the Stallions are quite competent fighters, despite their hotheadedness; Any one would give an Intermediate or Veteran level fighter a tough battle, regardless of the weapons used, and in fact the Stallions are likely to have their opponents significantly outnumbered.[/li]

[h2]Joining the Stallions[/h2]
A player of noble blood may decide that the cause of the Stallions (or their brash, devil-may-care lifestyle) appeals to him, and seek to join their ranks. This is by no means an easy proposition. First, the player must make a favorable impression on the Stallions he expresses his interest to (must make a die roll of 11 or greater on 3d6, with one point added to the dice roll for each point that the player's CHA rating exceeds the CHA of the Stallion he is addressing). 

If successful, arrangements will be made for the player to meet with @[Paul de la Riis](person:61db5c85-e436-459e-926e-17ad7e116b03) and/or @[Antonio Berensten](person:ec8f50d3-84f6-48a6-95d0-d7d6b5b65cb8) (the meeting will take place at either one of the Stallions' homes, or at the Golden Ducat). At the meeting, the Stallion leaders will question the player extensively, testing his commitment to their cause and trying to reveal any "fatal" weaknesses in the player's character (cowardice, a frivolous view of the Guild/Noble struggle, addiction to serious vices). 

If the player passes their inspection, he becomes eligible for membership if he can satisfy any one of the following three criteria:
[ul]
  [li]has a CHA>13 (the Stallions always want strong leader types, who can build up support among the masses based on personal appeal).[/li]
  [li]has sufficient personal wealth to bankroll some of the Stallions' political activities (at least 10,000 GP to devote to the cause now, with a reasonable probability of future contributions).[/li]
  [li]lacking the other two requirements, the player must successfully complete a test of initiation, which usually involves performing some sort of symbolic gesture that affirms their courage (e.g., breaking into the upper floor of Guildmasters Hall, and replacing the insignias representing the nine Great Guilds with banners bearing the heraldic emblems of the local nobility). No second chances are given if a player fails the test.[/li]
[/ul]

If the character passes this final hurdle, he can become a Stallion. He must, however, agree to spend at least half of his time within the confines of the city, lending his support to the struggle (thus the GM can exact payment for the player's increased political influence, by restricting the character's mobility to go adventuring). If the player refuses to make this sacrifice, the deal is off, and the Stallions will keep a close eye on his activities in the city thereafter.[/li]

articles.title: MYOB: Epilogue
articles.content:
After the party exits the mine, they can return to @[Oakridge](settlement:7ea6d7eb-ee01-4ebb-9e88-226dca2f1246). Here, they are presented with a choice: 
[ul]
  [li]pursue a further investigation to uncover the identity of the mysterious 'T' and determine how the monsters came to inhabit the mine, or
  [li]decide to end their involvement, treating the mission as completed. 
[/ul]

Choosing to wrap up their adventure, they can return to the miners, claim their reward, and consider their quest successfully concluded.

[h2]Confronting the mayor (or his wife)[/h2]
[ul]
[li][u]Mayor Gingar Terion is unwavering [/u]in his commitment to protect his wife, Theresia, even at great personal risk. 
[li]If the party presses him too hard, his [u]behaviour and mannerisms will betray deep guilt[/u], offering astute observers a glimpse into his awareness of the actual events with a successful [u]Wisdom (Insight) roll against DC14[/u]. 
[li]Should the [u]adventurers piece together that it is not Gingar but rather Theresia[/u] who orchestrated the mine's monstrous disturbances, Gingar will not hesitate to confront them directly, [u]prioritising his wife's safety above all else[/u].
[li]In the event that the [u]party seeks to apprehend or harm Gingar[/u], Theresia will seize the opportunity to escape their residence, prioritising her own safety. 
[/ul]

[h2]The Future[/h2]
With the party back in @[Oakridge](settlement:7ea6d7eb-ee01-4ebb-9e88-226dca2f1246), it's time to begin @[The Hag, the Bear and the Goblins](plot:bf013338-1b89-499b-8c5f-b9675e5a59c8)
