articles.title: Rescue by Moonlight
articles.content:
[h2]Players' Information[/h2]
The players are approached by a veiled half-elven girl who appears to be a low class courtesan. She will attempt to "pick up” the most solicitous  party member and persuade him to accompany her back to her rooms at @[Petals of the Rose](landmark:62d2bb3f-008b-4d70-9b25-14d4cebbd609), a @[Commons](settlement:8ff1c19a-c29d-4050-9c7c-7200f23453be) brothel. Should her first target appear unreceptive, she will switch her attention to the next most likely party member, and so on. She will be attracted first to size and strength, and will appeal to the character who best displays those attributes before approaching his less brawny companions. It should quickly become clear that she is quite desperate, and (though she tries to conceal it) her intentions are not amorous.

Once she gets a player character alone, her story will come out in a rather confused fashion, heavily interspersed with long outbursts of sobbing. Her name is @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb), and her beautiful, innocent younger half-sister, a human girl called @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136), has recently disappeared, and has not yet been found. @[Moonlight's](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) human father was a member of one of Haven's mercenary troops but was killed some years ago. 

He lived in free companionship with @[Moonlight's](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) elven mother, a courtesan on the @[Street of Silk Veils](settlement:587a7a3a-ba45-4bfd-94bc-fe6e17ae023c), for several years, although their union was frowned upon by both families. It produced only one child, @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb). Later, some two years before he died, @[Moonlight's](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) father married the daughter of a @[Magic Street](settlement:7f47361e-705c-471d-9c89-8beaa6aad852) shopkeeper, and @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) was born shortly thereafter. 

Since his death, @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) has been raised by her mother's kin, who are deeply distrustful of @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb). The two girls managed to become close friends despite the disapproval of @[Electra's](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) family, however, and visited each other as much as possible. 

@[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) found out about @[Electra's](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) disappearance yesterday, when she did not show up at an arranged rendezvous to go shopping near @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9); @[Moonlight's](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) attempts to approach @[Electra's](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) family have been rebuffed, and all that she has been able to learn is that @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) was last seen near Lorien Street the previous evening. @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) describes @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) as a lovely girl of average height, with long red hair with a pronounced streak of silver (a very rare hair combination in Haven).

@[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) explains that one of the girls at the brothel is the mistress of a tall, older, sinister man who (Moonlight whispers) is "one of them". This girl, whose professional name is Lily McGill (the other girls at the Rose call her Nancy), lives in the room adjacent to @[Moonlight's](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb). 

Late last night @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) overheard a conversation between Nancy and her lover. The two were apparently somewhat tipsy and not very discreet. The tall man was loudly cursing a number of people, including "that grungy double-crossing rat-faced twice-be-damned slime eating bastard @[Maylino](person:1d55f105-0e13-485c-956f-ac2c1696e432)" and a "mange-eared scab-faced little two-bit drug dealer in Thieves' Market", because he was going to have to work a double shift sometime soon. When Nancy asked him why, he replied "I gotta pick up a special shipment from the @['Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c) for export, and I bloody well hope they won't hafta be carried, 'cause I hate to carry people when they got two good working legs on 'em." After a nasty chuckle, "One of 'em's a not 'alf bad look'n redhead wif a fancy silver streak in her hair. Maybe I'll get a crack at 'er for my troubles."

@[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) explains, after relating this, that she suspects that her sister is the girl that the tall man mentioned. She thinks that her sister has been captured by slavers, and will soon be smuggled out of the city to be sold. She wants the player (she will be leery of bringing others into this, because she does not want "them" to find out) to help rescue her sister. Unfortunately, other than her own voluptuous self, @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) has only her life savings (some 56 GP in assorted small change, kept in an earthenware piggy-bank) to offer the player for payment. Anyone who would take this distraught young lady's gold would be a heartless cad, and little wealthier.

[h2]GM Notes[/h2]
Nancy's lover is @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14), the night watchman of Dockside Service's warehouse near Warehouse Row. @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) was sober the night that @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6), (@[Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) slaver who serves as @[Adrielle Ilorim's](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50) contact), came to the Dockside Services warehouse to discuss proper storage of his next shipment of slaves with @[Constantino Maylino](person:1d55f105-0e13-485c-956f-ac2c1696e432). At that time it was decided that @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) would pick up @[Angus'](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) "cargo", a pair of young women (one of whom is Electra), at @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c) in about two weeks, after they had received their "training". This is the extra duty that @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) was so vociferously complaining about when @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) overheard him.

@[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) is deathly afraid of @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60). She has picked the players to approach because they are obviously strangers to the area, and thus (so her reasoning goes) unlikely to be Black Hand members. She will not, even so, mention the Hand by name, and if pressed as to who "they" are, will reply "I can't tell you, they'll kill me".

@[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) had the misfortune to resemble one of @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50) former slaves, and was captured by one of Adrielle's henchmen two nights ago while walking a grassy common near her home. She had been shopping that day in Adrielle's fabric store. She was briefly held at the @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d) warehouse before being sold to @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) MacFarlane, Adrielle's Black Hand contact.

@[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) is ostensibly a dealer in spices and mineral powders. He owns a small pavilion in the @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb), from which he and his assistant, @[Hallen MacCormac](person:c9154c27-3bf4-494e-b301-cec4a1cb0d57), may usually be found hawking their wares. @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) is also a small time dealer in uncut hashish, and is widely known throughout the market as one of the sleaziest and most disreputable drug dealers around. As a consequence, most merchants at the Market will not suggest @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6)' booth if asked for directions to a dealer in drugs.

At present, the two girls are being kept in the rear of @[Angus'](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) tent, behind bundles of his wares. Both are heavily drugged. Late tomorrow night, a small donkey cart will arrive at the @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb), accompanied by two muscular men. The cart will stop at one of the market booths and pick up a number of bolts of cheap linen, and then move to @[Angus](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6)' Pavilion. There the two men, @[Angus](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6) and MacCormac, will shove the women into large, heavy-weight canvas sacks, and load them onto the cart to be taken to @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c) for two weeks of "indoctrination". The bolts of linen will be spread over the two girls, which, along with the lumbering movements of the cart, should adequately disguise any movements that the girls may make in their drug induced slumber. The entire operation, once the cart gets to the @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb), should only take about fifteen minutes.

Should the player elect to assist @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb), he will have to locate where @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) is being held and somehow free her. If the player attempts to rescue her from @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6), he will have to discover the location of @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6)' tent and then deal with the feisty little slaver and his hulking accomplice. The player will discover that @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136) herself has been thoroughly drugged. It is unlikely that she can be awakened anytime soon after being released (the drug will maintain its effectiveness for 1d4 hours after the player has rescued her), and it is improbable that she will be able to aid in her own escape.

It is possible that the player may attempt to locate @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) and discover @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136)'s whereabouts from him. This is a much more difficult feat, since the @[South Docks](settlement:e2e38a16-2c10-443d-b40a-6789aa7c041a) are large, and @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) is not a well-known personality. Should the player manage to find @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) and get him appropriately inebriated, there is a chance (saving roll against WIS of player) that the player will be able to ask leading questions in such a manner that @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) will not become suspicious. In this case, the player will find that all that @[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) knows is the date he is to pick up his charges at @[The Black Whip](landmark:6f66e600-9089-49a6-b9dc-7e4dd9febe5c), and that the slaver (Rufus does not know @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6)' name) is a drug dealer at @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb). Of course, if the player does not locate @[Angus MacFarland](person:164c1af1-3217-4879-aaa1-3f5c49eb70f6)' booth before one o'clock the following evening, the girls will have been moved to the Whip, an establishment from which it will be almost impossible to rescue @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136).

@[Rufus](person:16e2a95b-ef60-477f-99ef-237cef2eef14) will go to the Whip fifteen days from now, accompanied by one other Black Hand thug and one of the Dockside Services wagons. At the Whip he will pick up five girls, drugged and bound, and packaged as before in the donkey cart. One of these will be @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136). They will be taken back to the warehouse, where they will be kept for the night. Early the next morning they will be loaded onto Falling Star, a Black Hand smuggler, where, listed as "passengers", they will be carried from the city. Falling Star will sail with the tide and will be gone by ten o'clock that morning.

Should the player successfully rescue @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136), he will have earned, in addition to the undying gratitude of @[Moonlight](person:86d6a078-d3c3-4d08-aa2d-25a4c7a077eb) and her sister, the small reward (500 gold pieces) that @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136)'s parents have posted for information concerning her whereabouts. It is also possible, depending upon how he rescued @[Electra](person:f0fff5a5-63f0-4bf5-a857-5a1761cef136), that the player may receive unwanted attention in the form of a band of Black Hand thugs, who will attempt to quite thoroughly trash him for his interference.

articles.title: Search for the Bearded Dwarven Women
articles.content:
[h2]Players Information[/h2]
An expedition is being assembled to leave Haven shortly, bound for a hidden dwarven city said to be located near the borders of the Namori Empire. The expedition travels in search of the bearded dwarven women said to live in this city and seeks to bring back some of these rare beauties to @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954) as wives. 

The expedition is being organized and led by @[A. Gori Greataxe](person:e45f77bd-aace-4c5f-bf40-811011d61b14), the owner of a small print shop in @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954), he is fanatically convinced of the truth of this legend and has attracted quite a following among the younger dwarves of @[The Dwarrow](settlement:fa91fd8a-f74e-409d-a703-26a9b8e09954). Most of the money to outfit and provision the expedition has been put up by @[Browin Treebeard](person:4c0d4fe9-907c-45f9-8239-087a69b08d0a), the eccentric proprietor of a shop (Martin Metals) that produces pewter figurines. Browin has temporarily closed his shop and announced his intention to travel with the expedition as its unofficial historian (although no one is quite sure what he intends to do). Any and all dwarven characters are welcomed to join the expedition.

[h2]GM's Notes[/h2]
The source of A. Gori's belief in the existence of bearded dwarven women is a book of memoirs written by the renowned human explorer and world traveler, Revi Llug. Revi did indeed visit (quite by accident) the well-hidden underground stronghold of a tribe of deep dwarves called Wellerfast where he was attended and served by bearded dwarven women. 

To this day, a sizable percentage of the female population of this city is facially hirsute. However, contrary to Gori's own views, the male dwarves of Wellerfast consider their bearded maidens to be the least desirable females in the city, and their low status explains why they were allowed to come in contact with male outsiders. 

Ironically, this means that if the expedition reaches Wellerfast, they will be likely to bring some of the bearded women away as wives, and without the pitched battle Gori expects - for these are the only women the residents of Wellerfast would willingly part with.

Getting there, however, may not be as easy as it seems. Revi Llug's account is almost 500 years old, and the science of cartography has greatly improved in that time - consequently, the proportions on his maps are slightly distorted, and one must use visual landmarks rather than the actual distances given in his book to correctly retrace his route. Unfortunately, the sum of A. Gori's wilderness knowhow could be placed on the head of a pin with plenty of room left over for various additional notes. Thus, unless someone else in the party with cartographic training looks over these maps and realizes the difficulty facing them (60% chance, 80% if expert, of grasping the problem), the party is likely to become hopelessly off the trail within two weeks. 

Moreover, A. Gori is totally confident of his ability to lead the group and will very strongly discourage others from getting involved in the decision making process.

A second complication facing the expedition is the presence of @[Browin Treebeard](person:4c0d4fe9-907c-45f9-8239-087a69b08d0a). Though Browin has the physical appearance of a dwarf, he is actually an alien being from another sphere which has a portal that opens onto this world on rare occasions. He also has a diabolical plan for making use of the travelers on this adventure. Browin will spend his waking hours sculpting life-sized replicas of the party's members. When a duplicate is completed, it draws life forces from its original while the latter sleeps and assumes its consciousness (leaving the original in a state of suspended animation until this replacement has been destroyed). 

These inert forms will be carried off and kept in Browin's wagons, (and later, in the sub-basements of his shop), for that body must be kept alive in order for the creation to function. Browin will complete one replica every 2 days (victim selected at random from the party members, unless someone spends a lot of time in Browin's company). The activated replicas will perform as their counterparts. However, their thought patterns will resemble those of their creator, and they will occasionally do inexplicably "weird" things of the sort that have branded Browin as an eccentric.

articles.title: Second Story Legacy
articles.content:
[h2]Players' Information[/h2]
The players are approached by a boy of about 14, plainly dressed in a worn tunic and breeches, who asks them if they would be willing to take on a "risky gig for a nice lady whot needs some help". If they appear interested, he will explain that a lady he knows who has been kind to him and his friends, lost her husband, a Guild thief, some months ago in the destruction of Rogues' Roost. The thief had completed a job that very night, and the Guild has been pressuring the widow to produce the take from that lucrative robbery. Unfortunately, she does not know where he cached his booty, and is afraid for her life, and those of her children. She is willing to split the proceeds with whoever can help her locate the cache and get her and her children safely out of Haven.

The boy, a street urchin named Julio, has been on the lookout for adventuring types, preferably from out of town, and not members of the Guild. He will lead the players down several side streets in a roundabout route to a quiet street of old row houses in the upper end of the @[Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee), just off Southbridge Street, near @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb). 

There the lady in question, Lucia Marino, will explain that her husband did a second story job at @[The Pendragon Inn](landmark:8aaefea5-dcd6-4b71-9167-d94a465e453f) on Street of Caravans the night of the fire, and must have cached the loot somewhere for safekeeping. After stopping for a late dinner, he went over to the Guild headquarters to report, and never returned. Since he stopped at home, the Guild believes that he must have told Lucia where the cache was and have been threatening her unless she reveals where the loot is hidden. 

The only clue she has is a chance comment her husband made about finding a good nest for the "stuff", where it wasn't going to hatch out too early. He seemed to find this amusing but did not explain it further.

[h2]GM's Notes[/h2]
Lucia's husband, a skilled second-story man named Petri, robbed a spice merchant's room in @[The Pendragon Inn](landmark:8aaefea5-dcd6-4b71-9167-d94a465e453f) on Caravan Street while the latter was out dining. 

The booty included 200gp in cash, around 500gp in small pearls and gems, a letter of credit drawn on the House of Denarian in Mandalai worth 1,200gp, and one worth 500gp drawn on the @[House of Rand](landmark:a3539989-f5f7-4026-b788-c2e9a20b9c57) in Haven, some personal jewelry worth about 300gp, and a small vial of cinnamon. 

There was also a locked leather case that contained, in carefully padded niches, a half dozen ornate eggs, made of enameled copper and encrusted with pearls and gems, each with a @[Tiny](person:f75ef7a2-2d1e-48f5-970d-ddf2a76c55a4) replica of a different bird inside. The birds magically appear to move and sing if the egg is taken from its case, and each is worth around 2,000gp. 

Petri hid the eggs and other booty in one of his favorite caches, @[Delmarva's Fine Eggs and Poultry](landmark:0f363bc5-f075-458c-9cb9-dee88e04b9ee) (QY), on the Horseshoe. The shop sells eggs and young live hens and has a large shed in the back where the laying hens are housed, right against the Old Wall; Petri hid the loot under a loose section of floor, under the nest boxes. The henhouse also happens to be on the shortest possible route from the Pendragon to Petri's home near @[Thieves' Market](settlement:8b3bf96f-ea9a-4e39-8c3f-bda804df5cdb).

The players have several avenues to pursue in trying to solve this riddle. They can seek more information as to exactly what was stolen, either at the Pendragon, or from the local constabulary (Horse Gate Barracks), which might shed some light on Petri's cryptic joke. 

Of the two, the town guard would have the more reliable information since the theft was meticulously recorded. But there is only a 15% chance of the clerk being able to find the file (and requires at least a 10gp bribe to even get him to look unless the players can successfully pass themselves off as some kind of government official). 

There is a 45% chance of one of the employees of the Pendragon remembering the incident (the merchant was extremely irate about the lack of security, even though it had been his decision to keep the valuables in his room rather than the inn's safe). There is a 25% chance that jeweled eggs will be remembered as one of the stolen items. 

The players may also try to trace Petri's route home from the scene of the crime. 

Without some clue concerning the eggs, or some idea of looking for something to do with eggs, birds, etc., the players have only a 20% chance of noticing Delmarva's among all the other shops on the street.

If the GM wishes to make the adventure more complicated, it is quite possible that the lieutenant who has been hassling Lucia for the money is having her house watched. The players would then be followed by Guild operatives who would wait until it seemed that they had found the cache before moving in.
articles.sidepanelcontenttop:
[b]Locations/Start[/b] [br]
- The @[Labyrinth](settlement:e05f9a94-a44d-4991-b419-28cae89db0ee)
- @[The Pendragon Inn](landmark:8aaefea5-dcd6-4b71-9167-d94a465e453f)
- @[Delmarva's Fine Eggs and Poultry](landmark:0f363bc5-f075-458c-9cb9-dee88e04b9ee)
- Horse Gate Barracks


[b]Key Participants[/b] [br]
- Julio 
- Lucia Marino
[hr]

articles.title: Silks and Slavery
articles.content:
[h2]Players' Information[/h2]
The shop owned by @[Adrielle Ilorim](person:5d239bee-e2a5-4952-b7ad-412596ca7c50), @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d), has been under the scrutiny of the @[Thieves' Guild](organization:08dce3b2-80f7-468b-be7b-dd5fdbfac799) (as the object of a probable robbery) for some time. As a result of this, the guild has noticed that Adrielle has a second "business" - trade in human flesh. The guild has instructed its minions to case the shop, and then burglarize it. In the process, they are to take as much merchandise as possible, as well as any money from the cash box (as usual), and to search for any documents concerning the illegal trafficking of human cargo (for future blackmailing purposes, naturally!).

In addition, there seem to have been a high number of disappearances of travelers, although only those of human ancestry, in Haven. In most cases, these people were last seen in the vicinity of @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9), usually somewhere along Lorien Street. There appear to be fewer women missing than men. The numbers, in terms of the total population of the city, are not large, but the drain has apparently been reasonably steady over a period of several years. The nobleman whose daughter recently disappeared has alluded to the fact that she used to frequent the Loft in @[Erinhir](settlement:96d2680b-e669-4c07-ba6b-f4727fb8d9d9).

[h2]GM's Notes[/h2]
Adrielle does hate humans, not all of them, just particular individuals, and with seemingly good reason. While a young girl, she was taken captive by slave traders. Transferred from owner to owner (among them a particularly vicious woman, who mistreated her terribly), she finally escaped and succeeded in returning to her family in Haven. She has told no one of her plight, but she is very vengeful toward the slavers and many of her former masters and wishes to capture and enslave those who first took her into captivity and those of her masters who were cruel to her. As she had no way of ever knowing where any of these slavers are currently likely to be, she instituted a watch by a group of henchmen for persons who appear to meet the descriptions of her captors.

Through the years since the lovely elven girl began taking her vengeance, many men and women who match the appearances of Adrielle's captors have been kidnapped and sold to slavers. Rough look-alikes of Adrielle's sworn enemies were captured, drugged, and taken out of Haven under the cover of forged indenturing papers (which obligate the person described therein to a certain number of years of service, usually 7).

Adrielle has a problem at the moment. A young elf of whom she is quite fond has proposed union with her, and she is thinking that her vengeance is nearing completion (she does not want @[Taubardur Elengul](person:4bf38b25-3671-441b-ad91-c557b6c61c3f), her suitor, to discover what she has done). Unfortunately, @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60), whom she has been using to expedite some of the sales of her victims, is loath to lose one of its more profitable sidelines, and is threatening dire consequences if she ceases operations.

If the scenario is that of the burglary, there is a fair chance that the Guild thieves will discover @[The Black Hand](organization:d1a96263-4191-482e-ace6-360d25106f60) connection in the course of either the surveillance or the actual robbery attempt. In the cashbox, at the very bottom under the other papers, is a note about a meeting with Father @[Marco](person:92490b02-65df-4f78-9e42-accf38ae00d1) (the current leader of the Hand). Also, there is a 15% chance that a recognizable Black Hander will visit the shop on any given day.

Any human players who venture into @[Fine Silks and Fabrics](landmark:123c418e-9d2e-4cf9-affe-234e2cb1317d) might resemble Adrielle's captors. There is a 5% chance (1 in 20) that any given character may fit the description. If so, Adrielle will dispatch some of her henchmen to tail that player to his or her home, or to frequented establishments (taverns, inns, shops, etc.). A kidnapping attempt will normally be plotted, using a drug which reduces willpower to zero, concealed in a drink. For example, the character might be met by a man in a tavern, who strikes up a conversation, and offers to buy a round of drinks. There is a chance equal to the character's perceptiveness rating that he will notice the drugging attempt. If not noticed, the player will later awaken to the harsh reality of bond slavery.

articles.title: SotP Bridge Intro
articles.excerpt: Intro bridging from Secret Ceremony to Sins of the Past for Haven Irregulars campaign
articles.content:
This is not part of the original module but belongs to the [i][b]Haven Irregulars[/b][/i] campaign.  It assumes the party is finishing up something (the meeting from @[The Secret Ceremony](plot:93731b17-b5e9-4347-81a9-304763198ad5) in my case). [mark]Adjust as necessary for time of day as this happens at night[/mark].

 
[aloud]
When you return to @[The Giggling Gryphon](landmark:29260de7-ef14-4aa9-a123-5d0858f00927) you see a centaur cab parked outside the front door. The fact that it's not some fancy private carriage suggests something but you aren't sure what. 

Working your way around the cab to the door you spy @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) pacing just inside, he seems rather agitated. Seeing you, he rushes out and says: [i]"Something big happened at @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4)! @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) can't break away to deal with it but sent a cab to take you there."[/i] [/aloud]

Here's what @[Porky](person:e267fbb1-0d16-45d6-88c5-2bae4738a1dc) does(n't) know:
[ul]
[li]@[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) is at a reception at the @[Mayor's Palace](landmark:c0ab8179-cf4b-415f-b825-6c2535ae893b)
[li]He doesn't know what happened but it must be more that the regular watch can handle
[li]The cab is paid for
[/ul]

Here's what the cabbie does(n't) know:
[ul]
[li]His name is Koroneus
[li]He doesn't know why they're being called
[li]He is being paid to wait for your return and to take you to @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4)
[li]Something happened there and rumour says the dead are rising
[li]He won't say this but given the rumours, he is only willing to take them to the corner of Tailor St. and @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605) at the south end of the park.
[/ul]

[h1]What the Party Already Knows[/h1]
The party will already know the following about @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4): 

[aloud]@[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4) is in @[The North Corridor](settlement:4554e4a9-db69-47da-9921-6c5850001ce8) at the foot of the cliff marking the border with @[The Heights](settlement:1f0571c0-b8de-4b50-b010-5529cab6603b). It is bounded by Tailor Street on the south, @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605) on the east and Chambard's Way to the north. 

The main entrance is on @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605) where it meets Ware Street. Just inside this entrance is a small watch post, a satellite of Noble's Way Station. While the park is enclosed by a tall iron fence, there are many smaller entrances scattered around the perimeter. There are no gates on any of the entrances and citizens are free to wander where they will.
 
Originally established as a cemetery, it became the city's largest park park long ago when Havenites abandoned the practice of burying their dead inside the city walls. Now only the noble and richest families still actively bury their dead here in family mausoleums. Some of these are rumoured to "contain" extradimensional rooms.

The park features crafty sculptures, astounding mausoleums, beautiful trees and bushes, and curly walkways that allow visitors to walk and see all of its splendors. It looks most astounding at night when hundreds of driftglobes light the paths.[/aloud]

[h1]The Cab Ride[/h1]
Once the party boards the cab for the journey:

[aloud]This may be the fastest cab ride you've ever taken or likely to take again. Apparently he was paid to get you there yesterday and damn the consequences. The cabbie careens through the streets yelling for people to get out of the way. Crossing North Caravan St., the cab swerves around a wagon and slides wide behind the cabbie. It goes up on two wheels and your life flashes before your eyes - followed by a sudden lurch and a familiar feeling in your stomachs.

As you make your way along the fence up @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605) Haven’s biggest public park looks calm and peaceful.  
You see driftglobes lighting flower beds, trees, beautiful statues, and private mausoleums. The singing of nightbirds pleases your ears and the soft wind brings the smell of flowers. 

Eventually you reach the main gate where you find several Watch members milling about. Some look very pale while a few look down right queasy.
[/aloud]

Jump now to @[SotP City of the Dead](plot:ce813e1a-844c-49e4-bebf-0e4c029517b5) but skip the "aloud" section.

articles.title: SotP City of the Dead
articles.excerpt: Key for the above ground portions of the module
articles.content:
When players arrive at the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4), read this text to them: 

[aloud]You approach the south end of the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4),  and Haven’s biggest public park looks calm and peaceful. As you make your way along the fence up @[Studio Lane](landmark:a3a5c599-c67d-41a6-b3be-d94b4271a605) to the main gate at Ware St, you see driftglobes lighting flower beds, trees, beautiful statues, and private mausoleums. The singing of nightbirds pleases your ears and the soft wind brings the smell of flowers.[/aloud]

[h1]City Watch Post[/h1]
The post is situated just inside the cemetery gate at Ware St. It’s a small, single-story building with a traditional yellow-green lantern burning outside of it, lit by a continual flame spell. The post is normally manned by a sergeant from Noble's Way Station.

[b]Brel Skilren[/b] is the sergeant on duty when the party arrives. He is a tall, darkhaired, middle-aged man. After starting as a regular guard in the Dock Ward he got promoted and assigned to this post. Content with his new job at first, he now understands that maintaining peace in the cemetery can be hard work.

[b]What Brel knows:[/b]
[ul]
[li]Three members of the Jackals gang were brutally murdered in the cemetery. Their corpses are currently held in the morgue in the cellar of the guard post.
[li]Their names are Kin Simorsk, Indra Glide, Tornus Icebringer - tiefling, human, and dwarf respectively.
[li]It wasn’t useful to use [i][b]speak with dead[/b][/i] on the corpses for interrogation because their jaws were completely destroyed.
[li]The gang presumably has two more members, because witnesses reported seeing five people skulking about. They
might still be at the park somewhere because Jackals always work together.
[/ul] 

[h2]Morgue [/h2]
Characters who want to inspect the bodies can make a [mark]DC 15 Intelligence (Medicine) check[/mark] to ascertain that the dwarf and tiefling died from the human’s sword. He destroyed their jaws with his bare hands, then did the same to himself. After finishing this horrible job, he stabbed himself with the sword.

Alternatively, they can defer to @[Thom](person:c39bd715-1d56-409a-b5eb-8fc7c142ab95) who will come running in shortly after they get to the morgue. She was also summoned by Lyza but came separate location. She will shoo people aside while she performs her examination. 
[anchor|lodge]

[h1]Ambrose Everdawn's Lodge[/h1]
This small, one-story lodge is situated in the middle of the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4). Ambrose, an old man in his fifties, lives and works here. During the daytime, he usually can be found in the lodge, reading a book, or sleeping. During the night, he patrols the cemetery and watches for threats and intruders.

[b]What Ambrose knows:[/b]
[ul]
[li]Last night something murdered three members of the Jackals gang, whom Ambrose wants to catch for quite some time. They were trying to run from the park.
[li]All three bodies were found near the three family mausoleums: Tenley, Savonna, and Phylund.
[li]Their wounds indicate that one of them killed two others and then committed suicide. This can be the work of some undead entity.
[/ul]

Ambrose provides PCs with directions to the exact spot where he found all three bodies.
[anchor|battle]

[h1]Murder Site Battle[/h1]
Approximately 100 feet from the mausoleum entrance, there is a spot on the grass still red with blood. The whole location has a gloomy unsettling mood. Once characters arrive here, read to them:

[aloud]Shadows grow longer at the cemetery, as sunset approaches. Hundreds of shining driftglobes make their way through the front gates and gather at the different parts of the @[City of the Dead](landmark:bbfddc91-1a44-4b0b-8161-0e768eba0ef4). You see a small glade a bit off the walkway, grass still red with blood, and understand that you reached the place Ambrose mentioned.[/aloud]

Three driftglobes, that are nearest to the characters, are vile [b]will-o-wisps[/b]. After facing brutal death by Nesan's hand, three Jackals have risen as these angry undead spirits. 

One of the “driftglobes” picks a character who is furthest from others and starts flickering and moving away, trying to lure the character to come after him into the dangerous razorvine. Once the character steps into a trap, will-o-wisp attacks with his shock ability and initiates combat. During it, will-o-wisps quietly whisper names of surviving gang members - Rani and Haral.

The character who sees the wisp can discern it from the driftglobe with a successful [mark]DC 18 Wisdom (Perception) check[/mark].

[h2]Map[/h2]
Use provided battle map for this encounter. The razorvine is situated in the northern part of the map, forming a fence around an old statue of a knight.

[container:indent-20] [b]Razorvine:[/b] Razorvine is a plant that grows in wild tangles and hedges. It also clings to the sides of buildings and other surfaces as ivy does. A 10-foot-high, 10-footwide, 5-foot-thick wall or hedge of razorvine has [b]AC 11[/b], [b]25 hp[/b], and [mark]immunity to bludgeoning, piercing, and psychic damage[/mark]. When a creature comes into direct contact with razorvine for the first time on a turn, the creature must succeed on a [mark]DC 10 Dexterity saving throw[/mark] or take 5 [roll:1d10] slashing damage from the razorvine's bladelike thorns. [/container]

[h2]Will-o-Wisp Tactics[/h2]
While one of them lures the character into a trap, others attack with their shock ability from the invisibility on the second round of combat.
[anchor|mausoleum]

[h1]Reaching the Mausoleum[/h1]
Characters must succeed on [mark]DC 12 Wisdom (Survival) check[/mark] to follow the blood trail and find their way to the Phylund mausoleum entrance.

If they fail and decide to inspect three mausoleums mentioned by Ambrose one-by-one, they discover that only one of them has its door opened - Phylund mausoleum.

articles.title: SotP Geronde Mausoleum
articles.excerpt: Key for most of the mausoleum encounters
articles.content:
At this point, characters investigated the graveyard, fought with will-o-wisps, and arrived at the gates of the Geronde family mausoleum.  

During mausoleum investigation, characters will encounter Nesan Geronde (see @[appendix C. NPCs](article:4e9bc44c-a6cc-4d35-869c-4fc47f369a9c)) twice, once shortly after arriving in the workshop and the second time once they discover the Inner Crypt and try to enter it. This can happen in the [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/sotp-phylund-mausoleum-plot#p6]Old Crypt (P6)[/url] or in the [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/sotp-inner-crypt-plot]Inner Crypt[/url] itself if characters approach it through the secret door in [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/sotp-phylund-mausoleum-plot#p5]False Crypt (P5)[/url]. If the battle takes place in the [url:https://www.worldanvil.com/w/antheros-phre3d/a/sotp-inner-crypt-plot]Inner Crypt[/url], Nesan carefully averts his eyes from the mirror.

Nesan's tactic during the first battle is to possess one character and attack the other members of the group. If this attempt fails, he flees to the Ethereal plane and repeats it after he has recharged his Possession ability. 

During the last battle, he fights to the death (which is temporary in his case), angry at the characters for breaking into the family's mausoleum.

[h1]Mausoleum Features[/h1]
[b]Ceilings.[/b]Ceilings in the mausoleum have a height of 10 feet. 

[b]Doors.[/b] All doors are unlocked unless noted otherwise.

[b]Light.[/b] The mausoleum is generally in darkness. Some rooms have illumination produced by the continual flame spell, which makes them well-lit.

[b]Ghostly manifestations.[/b] Almost all rooms in the mausoleum have effects produced by the ghost's presence. They are listed in the room's descriptions.

[b]Haunted.[/b] If you are using Haunted supernatural region with this adventure and ghostly manifestation occurs, or one of the Haunted conditions met, you can roll on the table in appendix @[D. Supernatual Regions](article:cdaf27a1-daa8-489c-a94c-84fa9416c517) Reroll the result if it doesn't make sense in the context of the story.
[anchor|p1]

[h1]P1. Entrance[/h1]
When characters reach the mausoleum, read this text to them:

[aloud]The mausoleum is an old one-story building carved from black marble with two massive columns near the entrance. Between the columns is a big double door which is slightly opened. Above the door hangs a beautifully carved plate which tells “[i]Geronde family. What you fear, we master[/i]”. Ravens are engraved to the left and right of the plate. Both columns have engravings that depict various monster's heads - manticore, wyvern, troll, and green dragon.[/aloud]

Characters can recall details about the Geronde family with a successful [mark]DC 15 Intelligence {History} check[/mark].

[h2]Ghostly manifestation[/h2] Stream of cold air emerges from the mausoleum and gives goosebumps to the first character who enters it.

[h2]Door[/h2] The door was locked and protected by [i][b]glyph of warding[/b][/i], but the Jackals successfully opened it, deactivating the glyph. Characters can notice remaining glyph runes and blood trails on the door left by escaping gang members with a successful [mark]DC 12 Wisdom {Perception} check[/mark]. Occasional droplets of blood can be found across all main corridor.
[anchor|p2]

[h1]P2. Antechamber[/h1]
When characters approach this room, read to them:

[aloud]This big hall is well-lit and staffed with fine furniture. It contains several chairs, a small table made of oak, and a large fireplace. Four tapestries on the wall depict Geronde family members fighting various monsters.[/aloud]

This room is used by family members and visitors to prepare for the funeral ceremony. 

The fireplace is lit with [i][b]continual flame[/b][/i] spell.

[h2]Ghostly manifestation[/h2] When characters inspect this room, they suddenly feel shimmering cold - which is very unnatural while being in the room with a fireplace. The eyes of Gerondes on the tapestries seem to follow them wherever they move.

[h2]Tapestries[/h2] The dates on the tapestries indicate that depicted events occurred more than two hundred years ago.  

[h2]Jackals[/h2] A character who searches the room and succeeds on [mark]DC 15 Intelligence {Investigation}[/mark] check notices several droplets of blood on the floor leading from the northern entrance to the mausoleum exit, as well as occasional pieces of armor scattered on the floor.
[anchor|p3] 
 
[h1]P3. Workshop[/h1]
When characters enter this room, read to them:

[aloud]This chamber looks like a workshop, but a bit messy. In the middle stands a working table with a chair. Several broken vials lie on the table and produce a thick smell of embalming oil. One more wooden chair and an empty coffin lie broken on the floor. The room also has several alcoves. The northern one appears to be oil storage, the eastern one has different instruments and the southern one contains empty coffins.[/aloud]

This room is a workshop for embalming the dead. Gerondes stopped using this place long ago. All dead members of the family are now cremated in the temple and their remains are placed in the Inner Crypt.

Tornus, one of the Jackals, ran into this room while escaping and tried to use the chair as a weapon to defend against the possessed Kin. He was smashed by an empty coffin and run from this room towards the mausoleum exit. 

Characters who inspect the room can notice that embalming tools were not used for a very long time with successful [mark]DC 15 Wisdom {Perception} check[/mark]. 

[h2]Ghostly manifestation[/h2] When characters enter the room, they smell a choking stench that suddenly fills the air. They must make a [mark]DC 15 Constitution saving throw[/mark] or suffer [roll:1d4] poison damage.

[h1]P3a, b, c. Storage Alcoves[/h1]
These three alcoves are used to store different instruments and tools for maintaining the tomb and preparing the dead for burial. 
[ul]
[li]P3a - a collection of oils, 
[li]P3b - instruments, used to maintain the mausoleum, and 
[li]P3c - several empty coffins.
[/ul]

[h2]Secret Door[/h2] Alcove P3b contains a secret door, which can be found with [mark]DC 18 Intelligence {Investigation} check[/mark].

[h2]Nesan's attack[/h2] Shortly after characters enter and start inspecting the room, Nesan makes his first attempt to attack them and drive them out of the mausoleum. His combat tactics are simple: 
[ul]
[li]he tries to possess the strongest character and tell everybody else to leave the mausoleum. 
[li]if the characters refuse, he attacks them. 
[li]If a possession attempt fails, he retreats to the Ethereal plane and waits until it's recharged. 
[li]If he is ousted from the body, he uses his Incorporeal Movement and disappears through the wall. 
[/ul]

As this is their first meeting with the ghost, Nesan surprises characters on the first round of combat.

[h2]Flying coffins[/h2] During combat, on initiative count 20, ghostly manifestation occurs - one coffin from alcove P3b flies to the nearest character and smashes him. He should succeed on [mark]DC 15 Dexterity saving throw[/mark] or take [roll:1d6] bludgeoning damage.
[anchor|p4]

[h1]P4. Chapel[/h1]
When characters open the door to this chamber, read to them:

[aloud]The door opens into the room which seems to be a small chapel. Beautiful tapestries with symbols of a skeletal hand holding the scales cover the walls. In the center of the room stands a raised altar. On the top of the altar lies a sword with a shining blade and an ornate hilt in the form of a griffin's head. Moonlight lights the chamber and the sword's blade, coming from the opening in the ceiling. Behind the altar near the western wall stands a suit of heavy armor.[/aloud]

A Cleric, or will know this small chapel is dedicated to @[Mandos](person:74a5fbe1-efe8-4723-b118-35eec9865113), others will need a [mark]DC 12 Intelligence {Religion} check[/mark]. It is used by visiting family members to pray for the dead and as a place for divination to contact them if such need arises.  

[h2]Ghostly manifestation[/h2] Unnatural silence fills this room and creates an unsettling atmosphere. Armor and sword. The sword is a magical +1 weapon (see appendix @[A. Magic Items](article:d18c9a26-1808-4ae1-92fe-4b52a5dc48e8)). The armor suit is animated armor, which immediately attacks anybody who takes the sword.
[anchor|p5]

[h1]P5. False Crypt[/h1]
When characters open the door to this room at the end of the secret corridor, read:

[aloud]This small chamber contains two beautifully decorated coffins that lie to either side. There is another door at the western wall of the room.[/aloud]

This crypt’s only purpose is to distract and kill thieves. No one is buried here. All coffins contained in the room are empty.

[h2]Trap[/h2] In the passageway beyond the room, just before it turns, there is a pressure plate, which can be noticed with [mark]DC 15 Wisdom {Perception} check[/mark].  When a character steps on the plate they activate it. The door locks behind them and the passage starts filling with poisonous gas. At the beginning of their turn, characters who are in the room, must make a [mark]DC 15 Constitution saving throw[/mark] or take [roll:2d6] poison damage and be poisoned for 1 hour. The gas disperses itself in 1 minute.

The locked door can be opened with [mark]DC 18 Strength {Athletics} check[/mark] or picked with [mark]DC 15 Dexterity {Sleight of hand} check[/mark].

[h2]Secret Door[/h2] The secret door at the end of the corridor can be found with [mark]DC 18 Intelligence {Investigation} check[/mark].
[anchor|p6]

[h1]P6. Old Crypt[/h1]
When characters reach this chamber, narrate:

[aloud]This large room is decorated with beautiful tapestries on the northern and eastern walls. One of them depicts an epic battle between the knight in black armor suit with a shining sword in his hand and a giant red dragon. The other one depicts a group of people kneeling before the beautifully decorated coffin, in which lies a knight from the previous painting, but now wearing armor made from the red dragon scales. Five craftily made coffins are placed near the northern wall of the room. [/aloud]

This is the old crypt in which members of the Geronde family were buried until the curse has taken place. Coffins belong to the oldest members of the family. Knight from the tapestries is Ekhan Geronde, founder of the house. Characters who previously visited the chapel immediately recognize his sword in the paintings.

[h2]Ghostly manifestation[/h2] Characters who enter the room start hearing strange whispers in the language that can’t be understood.

  [h2]Coffins[/h2] Characters can spot that all coffins look very old and there are no new ones within this chamber with [mark]DC 15 Wisdom {Perception} check[/mark]. This raises questions about where Gerondes might actually bury their dead.

[h2]Secret Door[/h2] The western wall contains a secret door that can be found with [mark]DC 18 Intelligence {Investigation} check[/mark].

[h2]Jackals[/h2] Blood trail continues in this room from the corridor and ends near the western wall. It makes it easier to locate the secret door (above) to the character who decides to follow it, [mark]reducing the DC of the check to 12[/mark].

[h2]Nesan's attack[/h2] When characters reach this room and discover the secret door, Nesan makes his final stand to prevent them from going into the inner crypt. His tactics stay the same at the start of the fight - Possession and then attack of the other characters. If driven out of the body, he uses his Horrifying Visage and then Withering Touch to finish intruders one by one. On the second round of combat, three [b]zombies[/b] rise from the coffins and attack the characters.

articles.title: SotP Inner Crypt
articles.content:
When characters reach this room, read:

[aloud]The room lies in shadows cast by the torches on the walls. Across the room, 11 urns with nameplates stand on pedestals in a circle. Near the northern wall stands an old mahogany cabinet. Standing near the western wall is a large mirror in a gold ornate frame.[/aloud] 

This crypt is intentionally hidden from the eyes and accessible only via secret doors. Its purpose was to store valuable things like family treasures, which still remain here.

[h2]Urns[/h2] Since the curse has taken place, the remains of the dead Geronde family members rest here. The chamber contains [mark]11 urns in total[/mark]. Each of them has a nameplate, one of them says "Nesan Geronde".

[h2]Mirror[/h2] Beautiful mirror in an ornate frame stands near the western wall. It immediately catches the eyes of arriving characters. This is a [i][b]mirror of life trapping[/b][/i] (see appendix @[A. Magic Items](article:d18c9a26-1808-4ae1-92fe-4b52a5dc48e8)), except that undead creatures who see their reflections in the activated mirror fail their saving throws automatically.

[h2]Cabinet[/h2] Large mahogany cabinet stands at the northern wall. It contains several statuettes and books on family history.

The cabinet has a secret compartment inside one of the shelves. Characters can find it with a successful [mark]DC 15
Intelligence (Investigation) check[/mark].  It is locked, and picking a lock requires a [mark]DC 18 Dexterity (Sleight of hand) check[/mark]. The lock is trapped with Poison Needle, which fires at a character who failed the check.

[container:indent-20]
A [i][b]poisoned needle[/b][/i] is hidden within a treasure chest's lock, or in something else that a creature might open. Opening the chest without the proper key causes the needle to spring out, delivering a dose of poison. 

When the trap is triggered, the needle extends 3 inches straight out from the lock. A creature within range takes 1 piercing damage and 11 [roll:2d10] poison damage, and must succeed on a [mark]DC 15 Constitution saving throw[/mark] or be poisoned for 1 hour.

A successful [mark]DC 20 Intelligence (Investigation) check[/mark] allows a character to deduce the trap's presence from alterations made to the lock to accommodate the needle.

A successful [mark]DC 15 Dexterity check using thieves' tools[/mark] disarms the trap, removing the needle from the lock. Unsuccessfully attempting to pick the lock triggers the trap.
[/container]

[h2]Secret Doors[/h2] Both eastern and southern secret doors that lead to this room are clearly visible from the inside. 

[h2]Treasure[/h2] Inside the cabinet's secret compartment is a [i][b]book[/b][/i] (see below) with the history of the Geronde family, written by Nesan Geronde himself, [i][b]ring of protection[/b][/i] (see appendix @[A. Magic Items](article:d18c9a26-1808-4ae1-92fe-4b52a5dc48e8)) and an [i][b]onyx statuette of a manticore[/b][/i], worth 150 gp.

[h2]Book[/h2] The book explains why the mirror is present in this chamber (to trap ghosts and keep curse in secret), providing instructions on how to use it. It also describes Nesan's encounter with the hag after which the curse has taken place (it doesn't say, however, that Ursula survived and fled). The book also contains precise hag’s words:

[aloud][b]The Hag's curse[/b][br] [i]"Let your blood be cursed forever and may your family not find their rest even in death unless a full story of this curse reaches the world."[/i][/aloud]
[anchor|mirror]

To summon a specific trapped individual, the following words must be spoken aloud, using the correct name:

[container:indent-40]
 [center][b][i]"Ka karangatia e au te[/i] <name>[i]"[/i][/b][/center]
[/container]

[h1]Facing the Mirror[/h1]
Characters who came into the room and looked towards the mirror should make a [mark] DC 15 saving throw (with advantage if they know what the mirror is)[/mark]. If they fail and disappear inside the mirror, [mark]roll a d12[/mark]. 

[ul]
[li][b]On 1-10[/b], the remaining characters must face another ghost who has statistics of a [b]specter[/b]. All of them are insane to some degree after sitting in the mirror's cages and attack characters whom they see as intruders. If summoned using the mirror, they scream at characters. A couple of them who were buried not so long ago ask to free them and lift the curse.

The characters can deal with new ghosts either by killing them or forcing them to look into the mirror.

[li][b]On 11[/b], beaten and exhausted Haral Sask (see appendix @[C. NPCs](article:4e9bc44c-a6cc-4d35-869c-4fc47f369a9c)) appears in front of the mirror. He asks the characters to take him to the hospital.  If summoned using the mirror, he begs for mercy and asks to free him.

[li][b]On 12[/b], characters face angry Rani Dathret (see appendix @[C. NPCs](article:4e9bc44c-a6cc-4d35-869c-4fc47f369a9c)). She immediately tries to leave the mausoleum, telling the characters a lie that she is a daughter of a noble lord and got trapped in here by her enemies long ago.  If summoned using the mirror, Rani orders characters to free her, using her noble status to threaten them if they refuse.
[/ul]
[anchor|curse]

[h2]Mirror-Trapped Players[/h2]
Players trapped by the mirror exist in an extradimensional cell that is an infinite expanse filled with thick fog (visibility 10 feet). Creatures trapped in the mirror's cells don't age, and they don't need to eat, drink, or sleep. A creature trapped within a cell can escape using magic that permits planar travel. Otherwise, the creature is confined to the cell until freed.

[h2]Destroying the Mirror[/h2]
If the mirror is shattered, all creatures it contains are freed and appear in unoccupied spaces near it. This would be BAD for a low level party.

[h1]Dealing with the Curse[/h1]
The characters have a choice here. They can either trap the ghosts back inside the mirror by tricking/forcing them to look at it and then walk away.

Their other option is to spread the word about the curse and thus free all trapped ghosts - but at the cost of attracting the hate of the Geronde family.

articles.title: SotP Resolution
articles.content:
Once the characters deal with the problem, they are thanked by the Brel and the City Watch. They also receive a promised reward from Brel as well as renown inside their faction. Word spreads of their heroism and they become more popular in the City of Splendors. 

If characters decide to bring Rani and Haral to justice, Brel quickly calls a magister who makes his judgment and chooses punishment according to the Code Legal:

[ul]
[li]Imprisonment for 3 months for Haral and 1.5 month for Rani (due to her noble status) 

[li]Fine equal to 1500 gp which they must pay both, by coin or hard labor.
[/ul]

[h1]Outcome 1: Hiding the Truth[/h1]
Characters imprison the ghost back in the mirror and tell their quest giver and City Watch that job is done. This outcome means that when another burial occurs / other grave robbers stalk in the mausoleum, the history will repeat.

If they talk with Shifalra when she returns to the city the day after and explain their findings, she rewards them with 300 gp. She tells them to keep the story secret, promising to become their enemy if they don’t.

If characters part with Shifalra on good terms, she can offer them to work for her in the future. She tells the characters a full story and explains that the last hag, Ursula, is still alive somewhere. Shifalra believes that the hag’s death could also break the curse, and promises characters to pay handsomely if they manage to kill her.

[h1]Outcome 2: Lifting the Curse[/h1]
Characters manage to find Nesan’s notes and lift the curse by telling the City Watch and their quest giver the truth. However, they make enemies of Phylund family and Shifalra Phylund personally, which will surely make their lives more complicated in the City of Splendors. 

[h1]Rewards / XP[/h1]
At the end of Part III, characters should reach 4th level. They should also receive 1 renown inside their faction if this was a faction mission.

articles.title: TCW: Chickens
articles.content:
[h1]Introduction[/h1]
[aloud]
You arrive back at Port Perro after your quest, and find the North Gate to the city is wide open. There are no City Watch on duty.

On the other side of the gate, the street is deserted, but you can hear noises coming from different directions. To the left is the sound of fighting and the clanking of swords; to the right is angry shouting, some crashes, and…the sound of chickens squawking loudly. 
[/aloud]

[h1]Act I[/h1]
[table]
[tr]
[td]TO THE LEFT [br]Porky, the landlord of The Giggling Gargoyle , is nearly finished slaughtering a pack of Goblins with his huge battle axe.  A dozen bodies are piled up around him. He doesn’t need any help, but if the PCs can kill one each, he would appreciate the gesture. He might tell the PCs that killing goblins is boring.[/td]
[td]TO THE RIGHT[br]Four Goblins are trying to round up a dozen or so chickens to put them into a big cage. They have a couple in there already but the rest are proving elusive. Several citizens (including Mr and Mrs Muffin, the bakers) are shouting abuse at the goblins and throwing things from the safety of their upstairs windows.[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[spoiler][block:1235982]|Goblins[/spoiler]  (MMp166)

Once the PCs have dealt with the selected threat, either Porky or Mrs Muffin will explain that during the night, all The City Watch disappeared, and now there are chickens everywhere! Somebody left the North Gate open, and scores of goblins poured in and started rounding up the chickens. Most of them went off in the direction of the market square.

[container:tablebox-30]
Note that the goblins cannot just kill the chickens if a chicken is reduced to 0 hp it will turn back into a guard.
[/container]

The truth is that Count Jasper made his move in the night; a powerful polymorph potion was added to the guards’ supper, and they have all turned into chickens. One of Jasper’s men opened the gate, and the goblins, following Jasper’s orders, have started to round up the chickens before the magic wears off.

If the PCs selected the ‘to the right’ option above, then they will encounter Porky slaughtering yet more goblins on his way to the market square.

Either way, Porky will reveal at this point, if the PCs have not already guessed, that he was the fourth member of the adventuring party along with Lyza, Tograf, and Marcus. He knows all about the PCs’ mission, and will enquire about the sceptre, before grimly heading off towards the market square, telling the PCs “follow me.”

[h1]Act II[/h1]
In the market square, Count Jasper is sitting on Lord Tograf’s throne, smirking, surrounded by a small army of new guards. The PCs, as City Watch themselves, will recognise many of these new guards as the thugs and criminal gang members that they have been trying to keep under control for the past months and years (use Bandit stats block for them if needed MMp343).

Count Jasper watches with amusement as his Goblins continue to round up chickens and put them in sacks. Dozens of squirming sacks of chickens already litter the square. As well as guards and goblins, the PCs will notice that Jasper has:

[ul]
[li]a pet Owlbear (MMp249) on a chain, sat to his left[/li][br]
[li]On his right, also watching the goblins with amusement, stands Barnacle Betty and/or Braxter (if either are still alive)[/li][br]
[li]Behind him, in a wooden cage suspended from a makeshift gallows, is a badly beaten Lord Tograf[/li][br]
[li]On his right, an 18’ tall Fire Giant (MM p154)[/li]
[/ul]

What happens next depends on the PCs’ actions. At first, the battle will go against them:

[row]
[col3]Although Porky puts up a good fight, he is outmatched. The Bandits and Goblins watch the contest in fear, and will not intervene. Jasper watches with fascination. [/col3]
[col3]As soon as Jasper spots the PCs, he will cast a [spoiler][block:374192]|Hold Person[/spoiler] spell (PHB p251) on up to three of them, then order his guards to seize them . Within seconds the PCs will be surrounded on all sides by dozens of guards, hopelessly outnumbered.
[/col3]
[col3]Barnacle Betty shouts across to the PCs that if they surrender now, Count Jasper will allow them to continue their jobs and double their wages.
[/col3]
[/row]

But then, just as all seems lost, the PC wearing the copper ring will feel it tingling. There follows a blinding flash of light, then Marcus the Mage appears out of nowhere in the middle the square. Before anyone can react, he casts a powerful version of [spoiler][block:745064]|Dispel Magic[/spoiler] (PHB p234) which affects the whole square; this means that any held PCs are released, and the chickens turn back into The City Watch . The tide turns.

[h1]Act III[/h1]
The restored City Watch immediately attack Jasper’s guards, and the square becomes a battleground. Marcus and Jasper begin a battle of spells. Flames and lightning bolts fly between them, and it is hard to see who has the upper hand. 

In the chaos, the PCs have been forgotten about. It is up to them to choose how they join in the battle. The GM should keep track of how many combat rounds have taken place.

[container:tablebox-100]
[table]
[tr]
[td:30]Barnacle Betty decides to kill Lord Tograf. She draws a dagger, looks up at the cage, then activates her invisibility ring. She has to climb up the gallows first, making her a target to any PC using a Gem of Seeing (DMGp172). If the PCs do not intervene, Lord Tograf’s fate is up to the GM. 
[/td]
[td:30]Porky continues to fight the Fire Giant (MMp154). The Giant kills him in seven rounds unless the PCs intervene. If the PCs intervene, the Giant continues to target Porky, but if the PCs inflict 30hp of damage on the giant, the giant will die. Slowing down the Giant’s attacks, or healing Porky’s wounds, could also enable Porky to survive.  [/col]
[td:20]
If any PC gets a weapon (or thieves’ tools) to Lord Tograf , he will escape the cage and join in the battle.  
[/td]
[td:20]
The Owlbear (MMp249) attacks The City Watch and/or PCs. 
[/td]
[/tr]
[/table]
[spoiler][block:1235856]|Barnacle Betty[/spoiler] -- [spoiler]|Lord Tograf[/spoiler] -- [spoiler]|Porky[/spoiler] -- [spoiler][block:1237401]|Fire Giant[/spoiler] -- [spoiler][block:1236645]|Owlbear[/spoiler] -- 
[/container]

After four combat rounds, the PCs hear what sounds like a bird screeching above them, and the voice of Lyza calling out the name of the PC who has the Sceptre of Exilium. Lyza is riding a Hippogriff; she drops the emerald for the PC to catch.

[ul]
[li]The PC must pass a DC15 Dexterity check to catch it (or spend the next round trying to pick it up in the middle of the battle DC12 Dexterity check)[br][br]
[li]Next, the PC must pass a DC10 Intelligence check to insert the emerald correctly into the housing in the sceptre. Failures will result in delays (and extend the battle) but multiple checks are allowed. 
[/ul]

As soon as the emerald is in the sceptre, Count Jasper loses all of his power, and collapses, sobbing and pleading for mercy. Any surviving bandits run towards the alleyways to return to their lives of crime. Marcus casts a Sleep spell on the owlbear. The goblins and the fire giant run for the North Gate to flee the city, but they are pursued by some of The City Watch, and Lyza on the hippogriff, so they will not get far.

[h1]Act IV[/h1]
In The Giggling Gargoyle, depending on how the battle went, the surviving PCs will either be celebrating or toasting their fallen comrade(s).

Either way, Lord Tograf himself will pay a visit (if he survived), accompanied by Lyza and Marcus. Tograf will offer his thanks to the PCs and, as a reward, he will give them enough gold that they can leave The City Watch and set up a business if they want to.

He might also suggest that the PCs would make an excellent adventuring party, like the one he used to be in when he was a younger man, and he might give them each a magic item they might find useful on their adventures. For example:

[ul]
[li]Sword +1 (for a fighter) DMGp213
[li][spoiler][block:1237914]|Scimitar of Warning[/spoiler] (for a rogue) DMG p213
[li][spoiler][block:1237916]|Robe of Useful Items[/spoiler] (for a wizard) DMG p195
[li][spoiler][block:1237921]|Goggles of the Night[/spoiler] (for anyone without darkvision) DMGp172
[li][spoiler][block:1237922]|Gloves of Swimming and Climbing[/spoiler] (for anyone) DMGp172
[/ul]

Then he will thank them for their service, wish them good luck, and leave. Lyza and Marcus will follow, with a smile and a wink.

If the PCs did not save Lord Tograf, they will not get the rewards from him, but Lyza could still present them with a Sword +1 as a thank you for what they did achieve.

articles.title: TCW: My Baby!
articles.content:
[h1]Introduction[/h1]
 [aloud]
You are on duty at Shield Gate (G21) when a young woman comes running up to you, yelling "Help! Help! My baby! My baby! It's taken Billy! It's taken Billy!" Panicking, and barely coherent, she tells you that a flying monster has picked up her baby and carried it off. She points out of the city into the sky, where a winged creature can indeed be seen carrying a small wriggling thing, towards some crags a mile or two distant.

Grimly, @[Lyza](person:7bdbde76-bf2e-4c7d-9d8a-87b0d66a54de) instructs you to take some horses, follow the creature to its lair, and bring back the baby. She warns you that goblins have been spotted in the area. She tells you not to waste a second, before adding under her breath that it is probably already too late.
[/aloud]

The woman is @[Claudia de la Riis](person:646e36a4-cc41-4a91-8076-5af2dcb007fb), and her "baby" is in fact her dog Billy. At no time will this fact come up as she's too freaked out to say much other than to rescuee her baby.


[h1]Act I[/h1]
[aloud]
You gallop across the plains towards the crags and eventually the road runs though a small canyon. There are scattered bushes on either side and if it weren't for your mad rush it would be a very tranquil scene.  The air dances with the sweet scent of blooming flowers, and the melody of birdsong painted a symphony of nature's delight. Everything appeared idyllic, as if worries and fears were banished from this serene realm.

In the road ahead you see what looks like a small boulder in the middle. As you get closer though, you see that it is a tattered leather pack spilling its contents - what looks to be gold nuggets.
[/aloud]

Any PC who says they are on their guard, will, on passing a DC10 perception check, identify this as a good place for an ambush.

A party of six [spoiler][block:1235982]|Goblins (MM p166)[/spoiler] are hiding in the bushes (5 to the east and on in a tree in the middle of the river), waiting to attack. 

If the players sense a trap, or for some other reason ride past on the west side of the stream they can bypass the goblin for the most part. 3 arrows will still be fired. Riding past on the road results in 6 arrows. The goblins will be unable to follow as they do no have mounts. As they leave the area, they might hear one of them shouting expletives in Goblin.

Any player who stops to investigate will be targeted by six more arrows. The goblins have three arrows each, and after they run out, the goblins will rush out to attack.

The goblins have no treasure. The sack is actually full of pebbles, with the top ones painted gold.

 [h1]Act II[/h1]
  [aloud]
Eventually you reach the base of the high hills, and looking up you can see something large flying launch itself into the air and fly away to the north. To the left you see a path that climbs the steep hill via a series of switchbacks.
 [/aloud]
 
When the party is halfway up, they will be attacked by [spoiler][block:703998]|8 Stirges[/spoiler] (MM p284) who have a nest nearby.

[h1]Act III[/h1]
As the PCs near to top, they will hear screeching and *probably/maybe* barking? It turns out the “baby” is actually the woman’s beloved pet dog. It has escaped from the nest and is cowering some thick bushes while the creature, a [spoiler][block:504280]|Hippogriff[/spoiler] (MM p184), tries to catch it again, to feed it to its [spoiler][block:436689]|3 baby hippogriffs[/spoiler].

The players should be able to rescue the dog without having to kill the hippogriff. They could distract it with their food rations. Also, a dead stirge makes a tasty baby hippogriff snack. A skill check will be needed, but they can have advantage if they can remember the dog's name.

* If the players were slow to get here (e.g., they fought the goblins in Act One ) then they will find three well fed baby hippogriffs, and a discarded dog collar with a tag saying “Billy” on it.

[h1]Epilogue[/h1]
If the PCs manage to return Billy to its owner, Claudia will be extrememly grateful and will the one who has Billy (her hero) with a
[spoiler][block:1237850]|Stone of Good Luck[/spoiler]. 

[aloud]
Back at the Gryphon, you find Porkey struggling with today's Riddling Rascal conundrum:

[container:indent-40] Under the light of the moon my form does twist, [br]
With fur and fangs, in shadows, I exist. [br]
A howl in the night, a hunger untamed, [br]
What creature am I, with a curse named? [/container]
[/aloud]

[b]Answer:[/b] a werewolf, or any other form of were.
articles.sidepanelcontenttop:
[b]Start / Locations[/b][br]
- @[Boutique Lane](landmark:6f3e4291-b4ff-4380-afd6-00be23bb479d) (G21), or just outside Shield Gate.


[b]Key Participants[/b][br]
- @[Claudia de la Riis](person:646e36a4-cc41-4a91-8076-5af2dcb007fb)
- Her dog, Billy
[hr]
